name: 动补构式-let sb do
usageNote: let + 宾语 + 不带to的不定式补语，使役动词
example: Let me help you with that. | My parents don't let me stay out late.
variants: let him go / let them choose

name: 动补构式-make sb do
usageNote: make + 宾语 + 不带to的不定式补语，使役动词
example: The teacher made us do extra homework. | She made him apologize.
variants: make him work / make them wait

name: 动补构式-have sb do
usageNote: have + 宾语 + 不带to的不定式补语，使役动词
example: I had my assistant call the meeting. | She had her son clean his room.
variants: have him come / have them finish

name: 动补构式-help sb (to) do
usageNote: help + 宾语 + 可带to可不带to的不定式补语
example: Can you help me carry these bags? | She helped me to understand the problem.
variants: help him (to) learn / help them (to) move

name: 双宾语构式-give sb sth
template: Subj + give + IO + DO / Subj + give + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，间接宾语(IO)为人，直接宾语(DO)为物，可转换为give sth to sb
example: She gave me a beautiful gift. | Please give the book to Tom.
variants: give him a pen / give advice to her

name: 双宾语构式-send sb sth
template: Subj + send + IO + DO / Subj + send + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为send sth to sb
example: He sent his mother flowers. | I will send an email to you tomorrow.
variants: send her a letter / send the package to him

name: 双宾语构式-show sb sth
template: Subj + show + IO + DO / Subj + show + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为show sth to sb
example: Show me your homework. | She showed her photos to everyone.
variants: show him the way / show the map to them

name: 双宾语构式-tell sb sth
template: Subj + tell + IO + DO / Subj + tell + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为tell sth to sb
example: Tell me the truth. | He told the story to his children.
variants: tell her a joke / tell the news to him

name: 双宾语构式-teach sb sth
template: Subj + teach + IO + DO / Subj + teach + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为teach sth to sb
example: She teaches us English. | He taught the song to the class.
variants: teach him math / teach the skill to her

name: 双宾语构式-offer sb sth
template: Subj + offer + IO + DO / Subj + offer + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为offer sth to sb
example: He offered me a cup of tea. | They offered help to the victims.
variants: offer her a job / offer flowers to them

name: 双宾语构式-lend sb sth
template: Subj + lend + IO + DO / Subj + lend + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为lend sth to sb
example: Can you lend me your pen? | She lent her book to a friend.
variants: lend him money / lend the bike to her

name: 双宾语构式-pass sb sth
template: Subj + pass + IO + DO / Subj + pass + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为pass sth to sb
example: Pass me the salt, please. | He passed the ball to his teammate.
variants: pass her the water / pass the note to him

name: 双宾语构式-hand sb sth
template: Subj + hand + IO + DO / Subj + hand + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为hand sth to sb
example: Hand me that book on the shelf. | She handed the letter to the manager.
variants: hand him the paper / hand the key to her

name: 双宾语构式-bring sb sth
template: Subj + bring + IO + DO / Subj + bring + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为bring sth to sb
example: Bring me a glass of water. | She brought a gift to the party.
variants: bring her some fruit / bring the news to him

name: 双宾语构式-buy sb sth
template: Subj + buy + IO + DO / Subj + buy + DO + for + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，转换为buy sth for sb（注意用for不用to）
example: She bought her daughter a doll. | I bought flowers for my mother.
variants: buy him a watch / buy a gift for her

name: 双宾语构式-get sb sth
template: Subj + get + IO + DO / Subj + get + DO + for + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，转换为get sth for sb（用for）
example: Get me a chair from the next room. | She got a ticket for her friend.
variants: get him some coffee / get a book for her

name: 双宾语构式-make sb sth
template: Subj + make + IO + DO / Subj + make + DO + for + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，转换为make sth for sb（用for）
example: Mom made us dinner. | She made a cake for my birthday.
variants: make him a sandwich / make a card for her

name: 双宾语构式-cook sb sth
template: Subj + cook + IO + DO / Subj + cook + DO + for + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，转换为cook sth for sb（用for）
example: She cooked her family a delicious meal. | He cooked dinner for his wife.
variants: cook him breakfast / cook a meal for them

name: 双宾语构式-find sb sth
template: Subj + find + IO + DO / Subj + find + DO + for + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，转换为find sth for sb（用for）
example: Can you find me a seat? | She found a job for her brother.
variants: find him a place / find a room for her

name: 双宾语构式-save sb sth
template: Subj + save + IO + DO / Subj + save + DO + for + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，转换为save sth for sb（用for）
example: Save me a piece of cake. | She saved a seat for her friend.
variants: save him some time / save money for them

name: 双宾语构式-order sb sth
template: Subj + order + IO + DO / Subj + order + DO + for + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，转换为order sth for sb（用for）
example: He ordered her a drink. | I ordered a pizza for dinner.
variants: order him a meal / order flowers for her

name: 双宾语构式-promise sb sth
template: Subj + promise + IO + DO / Subj + promise + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为promise sth to sb
example: He promised me a new bike. | She promised help to the team.
variants: promise her a gift / promise the truth to him

name: 双宾语构式-read sb sth
template: Subj + read + IO + DO / Subj + read + DO + to + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为read sth to sb
example: Dad reads me a story every night. | She read the letter to her mother.
variants: read him a book / read the news to them

name: 双宾语构式-sing sb sth
template: Subj + sing + IO + DO / Subj + sing + DO + to + IO / for + IO
usageNote: 双宾语构式，可转换为sing sth to/for sb
example: She sang the baby a lullaby. | He sang a love song to her.
variants: sing her a song / sing a song for him

name: 空间介词-in front of
template: NP + be/V + in front of + NP
usageNote: 表示一个物体在另一个物体的外部前方
example: There is a car in front of the house. | She sat in front of me in class.
variants: in front of it / in front of the building

name: 空间介词-in the front of
template: NP + be/V + in the front of + NP
usageNote: 表示在某一空间内部的前部位置
example: The teacher stands in the front of the classroom. | I prefer to sit in the front of the bus.
variants: in the front of it / in the front of the room

name: 空间介词-at the back of
template: NP + be/V + at the back of + NP
usageNote: 表示在某一空间内部的后部位置
example: The kitchen is at the back of the house. | Exit at the back of the theater.
variants: at the back of it / at the back of the shop

name: 空间介词-in the back of
template: NP + be/V + in the back of + NP
usageNote: 与at the back of近义，强调内部后部的空间里
example: I sat in the back of the car. | Put the heavy bags in the back of the truck.
variants: in the back of it / in the back of the van

name: 空间介词-next to
example: My house is next to the school. | Sit next to me, please.
variants: next to it / next to the window

name: 空间介词-close to
usageNote: 表示距离近，可作表语或后置定语
example: The store is close to my home. | Come close to the fire to get warm.
variants: close to it / close to the station

name: 空间介词-near to
example: Is there a hospital near to here? | The park is near to our school.
variants: near to it / near to the center

name: 空间介词-far from
example: The village is far from the city. | You are far from the truth.
variants: far from it / far from home

name: 空间介词-away from
usageNote: 表示距离或方向上的远离
example: Stay away from the fire. | The beach is five minutes away from the hotel.
variants: away from it / away from danger

name: 空间介词-out of
usageNote: 表示从内部到外部，或表示状态（缺乏）
example: She walked out of the room. | We are out of milk.
variants: out of it / out of the house

name: 空间介词-into
usageNote: 表示从外部进入内部的动态过程
example: He walked into the kitchen. | The cat jumped into the box.
variants: go into it / run into the room

name: 空间介词-onto
usageNote: 表示移动到某物表面的动态过程
example: The dog jumped onto the sofa. | She placed the book onto the shelf.
variants: jump onto it / step onto the stage

name: 空间介词-off
example: The picture fell off the wall. | Keep off the grass.
variants: fall off it / get off the bus

name: 空间介词-across
usageNote: 表示从一边到另一边的表面穿越
example: Walk across the road carefully. | There is a bridge across the river.
variants: walk across it / swim across the lake

name: 空间介词-through
example: The train goes through the tunnel. | I looked through the window.
variants: go through it / walk through the forest

name: 空间介词-along
usageNote: 表示沿着某条线或路径
example: Walk along this street and turn left. | Trees grow along the river.
variants: walk along it / along the road

name: 空间介词-around
example: We walked around the lake. | There are many shops around the station.
variants: walk around it / around the city

name: 空间介词-past
example: He walked past me without saying hello. | Drive past the bank and turn right.
variants: walk past it / drive past the school

name: 空间介词-over
usageNote: 表示在上方不接触，或跨越
example: A plane flew over the house. | Jump over the fence.
variants: fly over it / jump over the wall

name: 空间介词-under
example: The cat is sleeping under the table. | The tunnel runs under the river.

name: 空间介词-below
usageNote: 表示位置低于，不一定垂直
example: The temperature is below zero. | Write your name below the line.

name: 空间介词-above
usageNote: 表示位置高于，不一定垂直
example: The moon was above the trees. | We live in the apartment above the store.

name: 空间介词-up
example: Climb up the hill. | Walk up the stairs.
variants: climb up it / walk up the street

name: 空间介词-down
example: Run down the hill. | Go down this road.
variants: run down it / walk down the stairs

name: 空间介词-between
template: NP + be/V + between + NP + and + NP
example: The library is between the bank and the post office. | Sit between your parents.
variants: between them / between A and B

name: 空间介词-among
example: She stood among her friends. | The house is hidden among the trees.

name: 空间介词-within
example: The noise is within hearing distance. | All rooms are within the building.

name: 空间介词-outside
example: Wait outside the door. | There is a garden outside the house.

name: 空间介词-inside
example: Come inside the house. | Put the letter inside the envelope.

name: 空间介词-beside
example: She sat beside her mother. | There is a lamp beside the bed.

name: 空间介词-behind
example: The garden is behind the house. | Hide behind the door.

name: 空间介词-beyond
usageNote: 表示更远的位置或超出范围
example: The village lies beyond the mountains. | The problem is beyond my understanding.

name: 空间介词-against
example: Lean against the wall. | The rain beat against the window.

name: 空间介词-towards
example: She walked towards the door. | The cat ran towards me.

name: 时间介词-at night
usageNote: at + 表示时间段的名词
example: I prefer to study at night. | The city is beautiful at night.

name: 时间介词-at noon
usageNote: at + 表示时间点的名词
example: We eat lunch at noon. | The meeting will start at noon.
variants: at noon sharp / at about noon

name: 时间介词-at midnight
usageNote: at + 表示时间点的名词
example: The train leaves at midnight. | I woke up at midnight.
variants: at midnight exactly / at around midnight

name: 时间介词-at dawn
usageNote: at + 表示时间点的名词
example: Farmers get up at dawn. | The birds start singing at dawn.
variants: at dawn / at the break of dawn

name: 时间介词-at dusk
usageNote: at + 表示时间点的名词
example: The street lights come on at dusk. | We arrived home at dusk.

name: 时间介词-at the moment
usageNote: at + 表示特定时刻的名词
example: I am busy at the moment. | At the moment, she is on vacation.

name: 时间介词-at present
example: At present, I am studying English. | He is not at home at present.

name: 时间介词-at that time
example: At that time, I was still a child. | Life was hard at that time.
variants: at that time / at the time

name: 时间介词-at the age of
template: NP + V + at the age of + Num
usageNote: at + the age of + 数字
example: She went abroad at the age of eighteen. | He started piano at the age of five.
variants: at the age of 10 / at a young age

name: 时间介词-at Christmas
example: We exchange gifts at Christmas. | I will visit my family at Christmas.

name: 时间介词-at the weekend
usageNote: 英式at the weekend，美式on the weekend
example: What do you do at the weekend? | I prefer to rest at the weekend.

name: 时间介词-in the morning
example: I exercise in the morning. | The accident happened in the early morning.
variants: in the morning / in the afternoon

name: 时间介词-in spring
example: Flowers bloom in spring. | We often travel in summer.

name: 时间介词-in January
example: School starts in January. | My birthday is in March.

name: 时间介词-in 2024
example: She graduated in 2020. | The event took place in 2019.

name: 时间介词-in the future
example: I hope to travel more in the future. | What will happen in the future?
variants: in the future / in the near future

name: 时间介词-in the past
example: In the past, people wrote letters. | Life was simpler in the past.
variants: in the past / in years past

name: 时间介词-in time
usageNote: in time表示来得及，on time表示准时
example: We arrived in time for the concert. | Study hard and you will succeed in time.

name: 时间介词-in advance
example: Please book tickets in advance. | I paid the rent in advance.

name: 时间介词-in the end
usageNote: in the end固定搭配，与at last近义
example: In the end, we decided to stay home. | Everything will be fine in the end.

name: 时间介词-on Monday
example: The class is on Monday. | I have a meeting on Friday.

name: 时间介词-on June 1st
example: My birthday is on June 1st. | The exam is on May 15th.

name: 时间介词-on time
usageNote: on time固定搭配，强调准时性
example: The train arrived on time. | Please be on time for class.

name: 时间介词-on holiday
example: She is on holiday in Spain. | I will be on holiday next week.

name: 时间介词-on duty
example: The doctor is on duty tonight. | Who is on duty today?

name: 时间介词-on business
example: He went to Shanghai on business. | I am here on business.

name: 时间介词-during the day
example: I sleep during the day and work at night. | Don't make noise during the day.
variants: during the day / during the night

name: 时间介词-before long
usageNote: before long固定搭配，置于句末
example: Before long, the rain stopped. | You will understand before long.

name: 时间介词-after all
usageNote: after all固定搭配，常置于句末或句首
example: It is not surprising after all. | After all, he is only a child.
variants: after all / when all is said and done

name: 时间介词-since then
example: I have lived here since then. | Since then, we have been good friends.

name: 时间介词-until now
example: Until now, I have not received an answer. | I did not know until now.

name: 时间介词-for a long time
usageNote: for + 时间段，常用于完成时
example: I have known her for a long time. | He waited for a long time.
variants: for a long time / for ages

name: 时间介词-from...to...
example: The shop is open from 9 to 5. | I will stay here from Monday to Friday.
variants: from morning to night / from start to finish

name: 时间介词-by the end of
template: by the end of + NP，主句完成时
usageNote: by the end of + 时间名词，主句用完成时
example: By the end of this year, I will graduate. | By the end of the day, finish your work.
variants: by the end of the week / by the end of the month

name: 逻辑介词-because of
usageNote: 后接名词/代词/动名词，表原因
example: The match was delayed because of rain. | She was late because of traffic.
variants: because of it / because of the weather

name: 逻辑介词-thanks to
example: Thanks to your help, I passed the exam. | Thanks to modern technology, life is easier.
variants: thanks to him / thanks to their support

name: 逻辑介词-due to
usageNote: 常用于正式文体，多作表语或状语
example: The flight was cancelled due to bad weather. | His success was due to hard work.
variants: due to illness / due to the delay

name: 逻辑介词-owing to
usageNote: 与due to近义，常用于句首
example: Owing to the rain, the game was postponed. | Owing to his age, he retired early.
variants: owing to that / owing to the fact

name: 逻辑介词-instead of
example: I'll have tea instead of coffee. | He stayed home instead of going out.
variants: instead of it / instead of working

name: 逻辑介词-apart from
usageNote: 既可表除……之外(还)，也可表除去
example: Apart from English, she speaks French. | Apart from that, everything is fine.
variants: apart from him / apart from the cost

name: 逻辑介词-aside from
usageNote: 与apart from近义，美式英语更常用
example: Aside from the cost, it will take a lot of time. | Aside from that, I agree.
variants: aside from that / aside from the price

name: 逻辑介词-except for
usageNote: 用于引出例外情况，整体肯定局部例外
example: The essay is good except for a few spelling errors. | Except for John, everyone came.
variants: except for that / except for the beginning

name: 逻辑介词-in spite of
usageNote: 后接名词或动名词，表让步
example: In spite of the rain, we went hiking. | In spite of feeling tired, she finished.
variants: in spite of it / in spite of the difficulty

name: 逻辑介词-despite
usageNote: 与in spite of同义，更简洁
example: Despite the rain, we went out. | Despite his age, he runs every day.

name: 逻辑介词-with regard to
usageNote: 正式用语，用于引出话题
example: With regard to your question, I will check. | With regard to payment, cash is preferred.
variants: with regard to it / in regard to

name: 逻辑介词-according to
usageNote: 后接信息来源，注意不接第一人称
example: According to the news, it will rain tomorrow. | According to Tom, the meeting is cancelled.
variants: according to him / according to the report

name: 逻辑介词-in accordance with
example: Act in accordance with the rules. | In accordance with your request, we will proceed.
variants: in accordance with it / in line with

name: 逻辑介词-in addition to
example: In addition to English, she studies Spanish. | In addition to teaching, he writes books.

name: 逻辑介词-in case of
example: In case of fire, use the stairs. | Take an umbrella in case of rain.
variants: in case of emergency / in case of trouble

name: 逻辑介词-in favor of
example: Are you in favor of this plan? | He resigned in favor of a younger leader.
variants: in favor of it / in favor of change

name: 逻辑介词-in need of
example: The house is in need of repair. | She is in need of a good rest.
variants: in need of help / in urgent need

name: 逻辑介词-in search of
example: They went out in search of food. | He set off in search of adventure.
variants: in search of truth / in search of peace

name: 逻辑介词-in charge of
template: NP + be + in charge of + NP
example: She is in charge of the project. | Who is in charge of this class?
variants: in charge of it / take charge of

name: 逻辑介词-in control of
template: NP + be + in control of + NP
example: The army is in control of the city. | He is in control of the situation.
variants: in control of it / under control

name: 逻辑介词-in memory of
usageNote: 表示为纪念某人或某事
example: The statue was built in memory of the hero. | We held a service in memory of the victims.
variants: in memory of him / in his memory

name: 逻辑介词-in honor of
example: A dinner was given in honor of the guest. | The award was named in honor of the founder.
variants: in honor of her / in her honor

name: 逻辑介词-in place of
example: She attended the meeting in place of her boss. | Use honey in place of sugar.
variants: in place of him / take the place of

name: 逻辑介词-in exchange for
example: I gave him my pen in exchange for his book. | She offered help in exchange for information.
variants: in exchange for it / in return for

name: 逻辑介词-in return for
example: I bought him lunch in return for his help. | What can I do in return for your kindness?
variants: in return for it / in return for support

name: 逻辑介词-in reaction to
example: The policy was introduced in reaction to public pressure. | She smiled in reaction to his joke.
variants: in reaction to it / in response to

name: 逻辑介词-in response to
example: The company acted in response to complaints. | In response to your letter, we confirm.
variants: in response to it / in answer to

name: 逻辑介词-in relation to
example: Prices rose in relation to demand. | What did he say in relation to the plan?
variants: in relation to it / with relation to

name: 逻辑介词-in connection with
usageNote: 表关联，常用于法律或正式场合
example: He was questioned in connection with the theft. | In connection with your order, we confirm.
variants: in connection with it / connected with

name: 逻辑介词-in comparison with
example: In comparison with last year, sales are up. | She is tall in comparison with her sister.
variants: in comparison with it / by comparison

name: 逻辑介词-in contrast to
example: In contrast to her brother, she is outgoing. | In contrast to the city, the village is quiet.
variants: in contrast to it / by contrast

name: 逻辑介词-in reference to
example: In reference to your email, I reply as follows. | He said nothing in reference to the incident.
variants: in reference to it / with reference to

name: 逻辑介词-with respect to
example: With respect to your proposal, we agree. | Changes were made with respect to cost.
variants: with respect to it / in respect of

name: 逻辑介词-on behalf of
usageNote: 表示代表某人或某团体
example: On behalf of the team, I thank you. | She spoke on behalf of the students.
variants: on behalf of him / on my behalf

name: 逻辑介词-on account of
example: The match was delayed on account of rain. | On account of his age, he was excused.
variants: on account of that / on this account

name: 逻辑介词-on top of
usageNote: 多义：空间位置、附加、掌控
example: The book is on top of the shelf. | On top of work, she cares for her parents.
variants: on top of it / on top of everything

name: a piece of
template: Det + piece + of + N[不可数/抽象]
function: 量化表达：表示不确定数量的一块/片/件
usageNote: 用于不可数名词或抽象概念，表示部分量
example: Could you give me a piece of advice?

name: a bit of
function: 微量表达：表示少量的某物
usageNote: 多用于口语，修饰不可数名词
example: Wait a bit of time. I need help.

name: a lot of
usageNote: 既可修饰可数也可修饰不可数名词
example: A lot of students study hard every day.

name: lots of
function: 大量表达：a lot of的口语变体
usageNote: 更口语化，用法同a lot of
example: There are lots of books in our library.

name: plenty of
function: 充足量表达：表示绰绰有余
usageNote: 肯定句中常用，表示足够甚至过量
example: We have plenty of food for lunch today.

name: a number of
function: 复数量化：表示若干（可数）
usageNote: 修饰可数名词复数，谓语用复数
example: A number of teachers attended the meeting.

name: the number of
usageNote: 强调整体数字概念，谓语用单数
example: The number of students is growing fast.

name: a couple of
usageNote: 原意为一对，引申为少数几个
example: I need a couple of days to finish it.

name: a pair of
usageNote: 用于自然成对出现的物品
example: I bought a pair of shoes yesterday.

name: a set of
usageNote: 强调物品的成套性或系统性
example: A set of tools is on the table.

name: a group of
usageNote: 用于人、动物或物品的聚集
example: A group of children played in the park.

name: a crowd of
usageNote: 强调数量众多的聚集状态
example: A crowd of people waited at the gate.

name: a team of
example: A team of doctors came to help us.

name: a kind of
example: The panda is a kind of animal.

name: a type of
example: This is a new type of machine.

name: a sort of
example: I had a sort of feeling about it.

name: a variety of
function: 多样化表达：各种各样的
example: The shop sells a variety of fruit.

name: a range of
example: The store offers a wide range of goods.

name: a series of
example: The TV station produced a series of films.

name: a pile of
usageNote: 形象表示物品堆叠状态
example: There is a pile of books on the desk.

name: a mass of
function: 大量堆积表达：一大团/大量
example: A mass of people blocked the road.

name: a quantity of
usageNote: 较正式，可修饰可数或不可数名词
example: A large quantity of water was wasted.

name: an amount of
function: 数量表达：一定量的（不可数）
example: An amount of money is needed for the project.

name: a supply of
function: 供应表达：一定量的供应
example: We need a fresh supply of food every week.

name: a lack of
example: The plan failed due to a lack of money.

name: a drop of
example: Just add a drop of milk to your tea.

usageNote: 形象表示极少量的颗粒状物质

example: The problem is chiefly about money.

name: largely good
function: 程度副词+形容词：很大程度上...
example: The success was largely due to teamwork.

name: generally good
example: The plan is generally good.

name: usually good
example: The bus service is usually good.

name: constantly good
example: He is constantly busy with work.

name: rarely good
example: The weather is rarely good in winter.

name: never good
example: It is never good to lie.

name: too + adj + to do
function: 程度副词构式：太...而不能...
example: He is too young to drive.
variants: too tired to work/too weak to walk

name: adj + enough + to do
function: 程度副词构式：足够...能做...
example: He is old enough to go to school.
variants: strong enough to lift/good enough to eat

name: such + a/an + adj + N
example: It is such a beautiful day.
variants: such a good idea/such a kind man

name: how + adj
template: how + Adj (+ 主语 + 谓语)
example: How beautiful the flower is!

name: what + a/an + adj + N
example: What a lovely day!
variants: what a good idea/what a surprise

name: Be + done一般现在时被动
template: 主语 + am/is/are + 过去分词 + (by + 施动者)
function: 一般现在时被动语态，表示经常性/习惯性的被动动作或状态
usageNote: 被动语态由be + 过去分词构成。一般现在时表示现在的被动。
example: English is spoken all over the world. / The windows are cleaned every day. / This book is read by many students.
variants: 否定：isn't/aren't done；疑问：Is it done?

name: Be + done一般过去时被动
template: 主语 + was/were + 过去分词 + (by + 施动者)
function: 一般过去时被动语态，表示过去的被动动作
usageNote: 一般过去时被动由was/were + 过去分词构成。
example: The book was written by Lu Xun. / The house was built in 1990. / Many trees were planted last year.

name: Be + done一般将来时被动
template: 主语 + will be / shall be / be going to be + 过去分词
function: 一般将来时被动语态，表示将来的被动动作
usageNote: 将来时被动由will be / shall be / be going to be + 过去分词构成。
example: The problem will be solved soon. / A new school will be built next year. / The meeting is going to be held tomorrow.
variants: be to be done：The meeting is to be held tomorrow.

name: Be + done现在进行时被动
template: 主语 + am/is/are + being + 过去分词
function: 现在进行时被动语态，表示正在被进行的动作
usageNote: 进行时被动由am/is/are + being + 过去分词构成。
example: A new bridge is being built. / The room is being cleaned now. / The problem is being discussed at the meeting.
variants: 过去进行时被动：was/were being done

name: Be + done现在完成时被动
template: 主语 + have/has + been + 过去分词
function: 现在完成时被动语态，表示过去发生的被动动作对现在的影响
usageNote: 完成时被动由have/has + been + 过去分词构成。
example: The book has been translated into many languages. / The work has been finished. / She has been invited to the party.
variants: 过去完成时被动：had been done

name: 情态动词 + be + done被动
template: 主语 + 情态动词 + be + 过去分词
coreWords: can, could, may, might, must, should, would, shall, will, need, ought
function: 情态动词+被动语态，表示能力/许可/义务/推测等的被动
usageNote: 情态动词后接be + 过去分词构成被动。
example: The problem must be solved soon. / This can be done easily. / The book should be read by everyone. / It may be finished tomorrow.
variants: 情态动词 + have been done(对过去的推测)：He must have been hurt.

name: Get + done被动语态
template: 主语 + get + 过去分词
function: get被动语态，非正式，常表意外/不利结果
usageNote: get + 过去分词构成非正式被动语态，常用于口语。多表示意外、不利或渐变的结果。
example: He got hurt in the accident. / The window got broken. / I got caught in the rain. / Don't get lost.

name: Have/Get something done使役被动
template: 主语 + have/get + 宾语 + 过去分词
function: '让/请别人做某事'，表示使役+被动
usageNote: have/get something done表示主语让别人做某事，或遭遇某种情况(多指不利情况)。
example: I had my hair cut yesterday. / She got her car repaired. / I had my wallet stolen. / He had his leg broken.
variants: have/get + 人 + do(让某人做)：I had him repair my car.

name: 双宾语被动语态(人作主语)
template: 人 + be + 过去分词 + 物
coreWords: be(v.), give(v.), send(v.), tell(v.), show(v.), offer(v.), teach(v.), lend(v.)
function: 双宾动词变被动，人作主语
usageNote: 双宾动词(give, send, tell, show, offer, teach, lend等)变被动时，人作主语时保留物作保留宾语。
example: He was given a book. / I was told a story. / She was offered a job. / They were taught English.
variants: 被动也可用介词引出另一宾语：He was given a book by the teacher.

name: 双宾语被动语态(物作主语)
template: 物 + be + 过去分词 + to/for + 人
coreWords: be(v.), give(v.), send(v.), tell(v.), buy(v.), make(v.), cook(v.)
function: 双宾动词变被动，物作主语
usageNote: 双宾动词变被动时，物作主语时，人需用to(表方向：give, send, tell, show)或for(表目的：buy, make, cook)引出。
example: A book was given to him. / A story was told to me. / A cake was made for her. / A pen was bought for me.
variants: to/for的选择取决于原动词搭配

name: 动词短语被动语态
template: 主语 + be + 动词短语过去分词
coreWords: put off, turn on, look after, take care of, deal with, listen to, talk about, laugh at, pay for, set up, pick up, give up
usageNote: 动词短语变被动时，短语中的介词/副词必须保留。
example: The meeting has been put off. / The lights were turned on. / The baby is well looked after. / The problem was dealt with.
variants: 被动句中介词不可省略：He was laughed at.

name: 感官/使役动词被动(不定式to还原)
template: 主语 + be + seen/heard/made/let + to do
coreWords: see(v.), hear(v.), make(v.), let(v.), watch(v.), notice(v.), feel(v.)
function: 感官/使役动词变被动时不定式to需还原
usageNote: 主动语态中see/hear/make/let等后接不带to的不定式，变被动时to必须还原。
example: He was seen to enter the room. / She was heard to sing. / I was made to finish the work. / The boy was watched to cross the street.
variants: 主动：I saw him enter. / 被动：He was seen to enter.

name: Be born被动构式
template: 主语 + be born + (时间/地点)
usageNote: be born表示'出生'，为固定被动用法，无主动形式。
example: I was born in 2006. / He was born in Beijing. / She was born on a cold winter morning.
variants: bear(生)的过去分词为born(出生)或borne(携带)

name: Be married被动/状态构式
usageNote: be married表示状态(已婚)，get married表示动作(结婚)。marry sb.表示'与某人结婚'(主动)。
example: They are married. / She got married last year. / He is married to a doctor. / They have been married for ten years.
variants: marry为及物动词：She married him. / be married to(状态)

name: Be dressed被动/状态构式
template: 主语 + be dressed + in + 衣服/颜色
usageNote: be dressed in表示穿着的状态。dress sb.给某人穿衣(主动)；get dressed穿衣(动作)。
example: She is dressed in red. / He was dressed in a black suit. / The children are dressed in white.

name: Be devoted to被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be devoted to + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be devoted to中to为介词，后接名词或动名词。
example: He is devoted to his work. / She is devoted to helping others. / The teacher is devoted to education.
variants: devote oneself to：He devoted himself to science.

name: Be addicted to被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be addicted to + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be addicted to中to为介词，后接名词或动名词。
example: He is addicted to computer games. / She is addicted to reading novels. / Many teenagers are addicted to their phones.
variants: be addicted to = be hooked on

name: Be absorbed in被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be absorbed in + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be absorbed in表示沉浸在某种活动中。
example: He was absorbed in his book. / She is absorbed in her work. / The children were absorbed in the story.

name: Be interested in被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be interested in + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be interested in中to为介词，后接名词或动名词。
example: I am interested in music. / She is interested in learning English. / Are you interested in this job?
variants: be interested to do(想知道)：I am interested to hear your news.

name: Be prepared for/ready for被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be prepared/ready for + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be prepared for和be ready for表示为某事做好准备。
example: We are prepared for the exam. / I am ready for the challenge. / She is prepared for any result.
variants: be prepared to do：I am prepared to help.

name: Be connected with/related to被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be connected/related with/to + 名词
coreWords: be(v.), connected(a.), related(a.), with(prep.), to(prep.), connect(v.), relate(v.)
usageNote: be connected with和be related to表示事物之间的关联。
example: The two events are connected with each other. / This problem is related to money. / He is related to the king.
variants: be associated with / be linked to

name: Be concerned about被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be concerned about/for + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be concerned about/for表示关心或担忧。concerned作'有关的'时后接with。
example: I am concerned about your health. / Parents are concerned for their children. / Everyone is concerned about the environment.
variants: be concerned with(与……有关)：This book is concerned with history.

name: Be involved in被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be involved in + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be involved in表示参与或卷入某事。
example: He is involved in the project. / She was involved in an accident. / Many students are involved in volunteer work.

name: Be covered with被动状态构式
template: 主语 + be covered with/in + 名词
usageNote: be covered with表示被某物覆盖；be covered in常表示覆盖满某物。
example: The ground is covered with snow. / His face was covered in sweat. / The table was covered with a cloth.
variants: be filled with / be surrounded by

name: Be known as/to/for被动认知构式
template: 主语 + be known as/to/for + 名词
usageNote: be known as(作为……身份而出名)；be known to(为……所知)；be known for(因……而闻名)。
example: He is known as a writer. / He is known to everyone. / He is known for his kindness. / The city is known for its beauty.

name: Be made of/from/up of/into/out of被动材料构式
template: 主语 + be made of/from/up of/into/out of + 材料/名词
coreWords: be(v.), made(a.), make(v.), of(prep.), from(prep.), into(prep.), out(prep.), up(ad.)
usageNote: be made of(看得出原材料)；be made from(看不出原材料)；be made up of(由……组成)；be made into(被制成)；be made out of(用……制成)。
example: The table is made of wood. / Paper is made from wood. / Water is made up of hydrogen and oxygen. / Grapes are made into wine.
variants: be composed of / consist of(主动，无被动)

name: There is/are-一般现在时存在句
template: There is/are + 名词 + 地点/时间状语
function: 表示'某地/某时有某物/某人存在'
usageNote: there be句型中，be动词的单复数取决于后面的主语(就近原则)。
example: There is a book on the table. / There are many students in the classroom. / There is a pen and two books on the desk.
variants: There be + 主语 + doing/_done/介词短语

name: There was/were-一般过去时存在句
template: There was/were + 名词 + 地点/时间状语
function: 表示过去某地/某时有某物/某人存在
usageNote: there was后接单数或不可数名词，there were后接复数名词。
example: There were many people in the park yesterday. / There was a meeting last night. / There was some water in the bottle.
variants: There used to be：There used to be a shop here.

name: There will be-将来时存在句
template: There will be + 名词 + 地点/时间状语
function: 表示将来某地/某时将有某物/某人存在
usageNote: there will be表示将来的存在。
example: There will be a meeting tomorrow. / There will be no school next Monday. / There will be a party tonight.
variants: There is/are going to be：There is going to be a film tonight.

name: There must/can/may be-情态动词存在句
template: There must/can/may/might/should be + 名词 + 状语
coreWords: there(ad.), must(modal), can(modal), may(modal), might(aux.), should(modal), be(v.)
function: 表示对存在的推测/可能性/必要性
usageNote: 情态动词+there be表示对存在的推测、可能性、必要性等。
example: There must be a mistake somewhere. / There can be no doubt about it. / There may be some rain tomorrow. / There should be an answer by now.
variants: There must have been(对过去的推测)：There must have been a party here.

name: There seems/appears/happens to be-半助动词存在句
template: There seems/appears/happens to be + 名词 + 状语
usageNote: seem/appear/happen等半助动词后接to be，构成there seem/appear/happen to be。
example: There seems to be no problem. / There appears to be a mistake. / There happened to be a doctor on the train. / There used to be a cinema here.
variants: There seems to have been：There seems to have been an accident.

name: There is no use/point/good in doing-存在评价构式
template: There is no use/point/good/harm/sense in + 动名词
coreWords: there(ad.), no(ad.), use(n.), point(n.), good(n./a.), harm(n.), sense(n.)
function: '做……没有用/没有意义/没有好处/没有害处'
usageNote: no use/point/good/harm/sense等名词后接in doing，in常可省略。
example: There is no use crying over spilt milk. / There is no point in arguing. / There is no harm in trying. / There is no sense in waiting.
variants: It is no use doing = There is no use in doing

name: There is no need to do-存在需求构式
template: There is no need to + 动词原形
usageNote: there is no need to do表示没有必要做某事。
example: There is no need to worry. / There is no need to hurry. / There is no need to apologize.
variants: There is no need for sb. to do：There is no need for you to come.

name: There lived/stood/existed/remained-存在动词存在句
template: There lived/stood/existed/remained + 名词 + 地点状语
function: 用其他存在动词替代be的存在句
usageNote: live, stand, exist, remain等动词可替代be，构成there存在句，增加描写性。
example: There lived an old man in the village. / There stands a tall building by the river. / There remained nothing to be done.
variants: There came a knock at the door. / There arose a problem.

name: 助动词do/does/did强调
template: 主语 + do/does/did + 动词原形
usageNote: 用do/does/did + 动词原形来强调谓语，表示'确实/真的'。
example: I do like you. / She does want to help. / He did finish his homework. / Do come to the party.

name: very/the same/only/simply强调
template: the very + 名词 / the same + 名词 / only + 名词 / simply + 动词
usageNote: the very(正是那个)，the same(同一个)，only(仅有的)，simply(简直/不过)等词可加强语气。
example: This is the very book I want. / He is the only person who knows. / I simply don't understand. / That's the same man I saw.
variants: at all(用于否定强调)：I don't like it at all.

name: 肯定祈使句(动词原形开头)
function: 表示命令、请求、建议、劝告等
usageNote: 肯定祈使句以动词原形开头，省略主语you。句末用感叹号或句号。
example: Open the door. / Be quiet. / Come here. / Have a seat. / Make yourself at home. / Pass me the salt.
variants: 祈使句+please：Please come in. / Come in, please.

name: 否定祈使句
template: Don't / Never + 动词原形 + (宾语) + (状语)!
usageNote: 否定祈使句以Don't或Never开头，后接动词原形。Never语气更强。
example: Don't be late. / Never give up. / Don't touch that. / Don't worry about it. / Never tell lies.
variants: No + 名词/动名词：No smoking. / No parking.

name: 强调祈使句(Do+动词原形)
function: '务必/一定/确实'，加强祈使语气
usageNote: 在肯定祈使句前加do，表示强调，意为'务必/一定'。
example: Do be careful. / Do come to the party. / Do tell me the truth. / Do sit down. / Do finish your homework.
variants: Please do + 动词原形：Please do come.

name: Let祈使句(Let's/Let him)
template: Let's/Let us/Let him/her/them + 动词原形
function: '让我们/让他'，表示建议或命令
usageNote: Let's = Let us，表示建议'让我们……'。Let him/her/them表示'让他/她/他们……'。
example: Let's go. / Let him try. / Let's not argue. / Let me help you. / Let them decide.
variants: Let's的反义疑问句用shall we；Let us(请求许可)的反义疑问句用will you。

name: 祈使句+and+陈述句
template: 祈使句 + and + 陈述句(一般将来时)
function: '……就/那么……'，祈使句相当于条件句
usageNote: 祈使句+and+陈述句，相当于if引导的条件句。and = then。
example: Work hard and you will succeed. / Hurry up and you won't be late. / Come early and you will get a good seat.

name: 祈使句+or+陈述句
template: 祈使句 + or + 陈述句(一般将来时/情态动词)
function: '……否则……'，祈使句相当于否定条件句
usageNote: 祈使句+or+陈述句，相当于if...not引导的条件句。or = otherwise。
example: Hurry up or you will be late. / Study hard or you will fail. / Be careful or you may get hurt.

name: 祈使句+反义疑问句
template: 祈使句 + , + will you/won't you/would you/can you/can't you?
function: 祈使句附加反义疑问句，使语气委婉
usageNote: 肯定祈使句+will you/won't you/can you/can't you/would you；否定祈使句+will you；Let's+shall we；Let us(请求)+will you。
example: Open the door, will you? / Be quiet, won't you? / Don't be late, will you? / Let's go, shall we? / Let us go, will you?
variants: will you最通用；won't you表邀请；can you表请求；would you表礼貌

name: 以名词/形容词开头的祈使句
function: 名词或形容词直接用于祈使句
usageNote: 某些名词或形容词可直接用于祈使句，表示命令或请求。
example: Hands up! / Attention! / Eyes front! / Silence! / Quick! / Calm down!
variants: No + 名词/动名词：No smoking! / No entry!

name: 祈使句+附加说明(带主语)
template: You + 动词原形 + 其他! / 动词原形 + 其他 + , + you!
function: 祈使句中带出主语you，表示强调或不满
usageNote: 祈使句有时加上主语you，以加强语气或表示不满。
example: You be quiet! / You wait here! / Don't you talk to me like that! / Mind your own business, you!
variants: somebody/anybody/everybody + 动词原形：Somebody answer the phone!

name: 无动词祈使句
usageNote: 某些祈使句中动词被省略，直接用名词、副词或介词短语。
example: Just a minute! / This way, please! / After you! / Down with war! / Off with his head!

name: What+a/an+adj.+可数名词单数-感叹句
template: What + a/an + 形容词 + 可数名词单数 + (主语 + 谓语)!
function: 感叹名词，表示惊讶、赞叹等
usageNote: what感叹句修饰名词。what + a/an + adj. + 可数名词单数。主语和谓语可省略。
example: What a beautiful day! / What an interesting book! / What a clever boy he is! / What a pity!
variants: What + adj. + 不可数名词/复数名词：What fine weather! / What beautiful flowers!

name: What+adj.+不可数名词/复数名词-感叹句
template: What + 形容词 + 不可数名词/复数名词 + (主语 + 谓语)!
function: 感叹不可数名词或复数名词
usageNote: what + adj. + 不可数名词/复数名词，不加a/an。
example: What beautiful flowers! / What good advice! / What fine weather! / What terrible news! / What interesting books they are!
variants: What + 名词(不加adj.)：What fun! / What nonsense!

name: How+adj./adv.-感叹句
template: How + 形容词/副词 + (主语 + 谓语)!
usageNote: how感叹句修饰形容词或副词。how + adj./adv. + 主语 + 谓语。
example: How beautiful! / How fast he runs! / How kind she is! / How well you sing! / How time flies!
variants: How + 形容词 + a/an + 名词：How clever a boy he is!

name: How+adj.+a/an+名词-感叹句
template: How + 形容词 + a/an + 可数名词单数 + (主语 + 谓语)!
usageNote: how + adj. + a/an + 名词，是how感叹句的特殊形式，相当于What a/an + adj. + 名词。
example: How clever a boy he is! / How beautiful a flower it is! / How interesting a story it was!

name: 陈述句/疑问句/祈使句变感叹句
template: 陈述句/疑问句 + ! / How + 主语 + 谓语!
function: 将陈述句、疑问句或祈使句转换为感叹句
usageNote: 陈述句/疑问句/祈使句可通过加强语气和使用感叹号变为感叹句。
example: You are so kind! / Isn't it beautiful! / Do stop talking! / That's wonderful! / Oh, my God!

name: What/How + 主语 + 谓语 + 其他成分
template: What + (a/an) + adj. + n. + 主语 + 谓语! / How + adj./adv. + 主语 + 谓语!
function: 感叹句带完整主谓结构
usageNote: 感叹句中主语和谓语可以保留，也可以省略。
example: What a beautiful girl she is! / How fast he runs! / What an interesting story it is! / How hard they work!
variants: 省略主谓：What a beautiful girl! / How fast!

name: 省略主语(祈使句/日常用语)
function: 省略主语，常见于祈使句和日常口语
usageNote: 祈使句省略主语you；日常口语中I, it常省略。
example: (I) Thank you. / (I) Hope to see you again. / (It) Sounds good. / (I) Don't know. / (You) Sit down.
variants: 电话用语：(This is) Tom speaking. / (It is) Nice to meet you.

name: 省略不定式(保留to)
template: 主语 + 谓语 + to(省略动词原形)
function: 省略与前面重复的不定式部分，保留to
usageNote: 当不定式短语与前面已出现的不定式重复时，可省略动词原形，只保留to。
example: Would you like to come? I'd love to (come). / I want to go but I don't know how to (go). / You can leave if you want to (leave).
variants: have to / ought to / used to等后的省略：I don't play tennis, but I used to.

name: 省略谓语动词(保留助动词)
template: 主语 + 助动词/情态动词(省略实义动词)
function: 省略与前面重复的实义动词部分
usageNote: 当前后谓语部分重复时，可省略实义动词，保留助动词/情态动词。
example: He can speak English better than I can (speak English). / She sings as well as he does (sing). / I haven't finished, but I will (finish).
variants: 省略实义动词及宾语：Does he like it? Yes, he does.

name: 并列句中的省略
template: 分句A + and/or/but + (省略重复成分) + 分句B
function: 并列句中省略重复的成分
usageNote: 在并列句中，后一分句中与前面重复的成分可省略。
example: Tom is a doctor and Mary (is) a nurse. / I work in Beijing and my brother (works) in Shanghai. / He likes tea but I (like) coffee.
variants: 省略主语和谓语：He came by bus and (he came) by taxi.

name: 特殊疑问句中的省略
template: 疑问词 + (助动词 + 主语 + 谓语中的重复部分)?
function: 特殊疑问句中省略重复成分
usageNote: 特殊疑问句中，与上文重复的成分常省略。
example: Where (are you going)? To the park. / Why (did you do it)? Because I had to. / What (do you want)? Money.

name: So+主语+助动词-强调确认
template: So + 主语 + 助动词/情态动词/be
function: '确实如此'，表示同意前面所说的话
usageNote: So + 主语 + 助动词表示对前面所说内容的确认和附和(主语相同)。与So + 助动词 + 主语(主语不同)区分。
example: It is a fine day. So it is. / He works hard. So he does. / She is clever. So she is.

name: To tell you the truth-插入语省略构式
template: To tell you the truth / To be honest / Frankly speaking + , + 主句
function: 不定式短语作插入语/评注性状语
usageNote: 不定式短语或分词短语可作插入语，表示说话人的态度或评论。
example: To tell you the truth, I don't like it. / To be honest, I forgot. / Frankly speaking, he is lazy. / Judging from his look, he is tired.
variants: Generally speaking / Strictly speaking / Considering his age

name: With/Without复合结构
template: With/Without + 宾语 + doing/done/adj./adv./介词短语 + , + 主句
function: with/without复合结构作状语或定语
usageNote: with/without + 宾语 + 宾补(doing/done/adj./adv./介词短语)构成复合结构，作伴随、原因、条件等状语。
example: With time going by, we grow older. / With the work done, we went home. / He slept with the window open. / Without a word, he left.
variants: with + 宾语 + to do(表将来)：With much work to do, I can't go out.

name: What/How about-建议构式
template: What/How about + 名词/代词/动名词?
usageNote: what/how about后接名词、代词或动名词，用于提出建议或询问情况。
example: What about a cup of tea? / How about going for a walk? / What about you? / How about this one?

name: Why not/Why don't you-建议构式
template: Why not + 动词原形? / Why don't you + 动词原形?
usageNote: why not和why don't you用于提出建议，语气委婉。
example: Why not try again? / Why don't you come with us? / Why not ask the teacher? / Why don't we go out?

name: Had better/Would rather/Would sooner-偏好建议构式
template: 主语 + had better + 动词原形 / 主语 + would rather/sooner + 动词原形
usageNote: had better do(最好做，语气较强)；would rather do(宁愿做，表偏好)。否定形式：had better not do / would rather not do。
example: You had better go now. / I'd rather stay home. / You'd better not be late. / I'd sooner walk than take a bus.
variants: had better have done(本该做)：You had better have come earlier.

name: Used to/Be used to/Get used to-习惯构式
template: 主语 + used to + do / 主语 + be/get used to + doing
function: used to(过去常常)；be/get used to(习惯于)
usageNote: used to do(过去常常做，现在不做了)；be used to doing(习惯于做)；get used to doing(变得习惯于做)。
example: I used to play tennis. / I am used to getting up early. / She got used to living alone. / He is used to hard work.
variants: used to的疑问/否定：Did you use to...? / I didn't use to... / Used you to...?

name: Prefer...to.../Would prefer...to...-偏好构式
template: 主语 + prefer + A + to + B / 主语 + would prefer + to do + rather than + do
usageNote: prefer A to B(A和B为名词或动名词)；prefer to do A rather than do B；prefer doing A to doing B。
example: I prefer tea to coffee. / I prefer walking to running. / I would prefer to stay rather than go out. / She prefers reading to watching TV.
variants: prefer + that从句(虚拟)：I prefer that he (should) come early.

name: Would like/feel like/want-意愿构式
template: 主语 + would like + to do/noun / 主语 + feel like + doing / 主语 + want + to do/noun
usageNote: would like to do/doing/noun(礼貌请求)；feel like doing(想要做)；want to do/noun(想做/想要)。
example: I would like a cup of tea. / Would you like to dance? / I feel like eating something. / I want to go home.
variants: Would you like...?的礼貌回答：Yes, please. / No, thank you.

name: Be about to/Be going to/Be to-将来构式
template: 主语 + be about to do / 主语 + be going to do / 主语 + be to do
usageNote: be about to do(即将做，马上)；be going to do(打算/将要做)；be to do(计划/安排/命令/命运)。
example: I am about to leave. / It is going to rain. / We are to meet at 8. / The president is to visit China.
variants: be about to不能与时间状语连用：I was about to leave when he came.(正确)

name: Have been to/Have gone to-经历构式
template: 主语 + have/has been to + 地点 / 主语 + have/has gone to + 地点
coreWords: have(v.), has(v.), been(v.), gone(v.), go(v.), to(prep.)
function: have been to(去过已回)；have gone to(去了未回)
usageNote: have been to表示曾经去过某地(现在已回来)；have gone to表示去了某地(现在还在那里或途中)。
example: I have been to Beijing twice. / He has gone to Beijing.(他现在在北京) / She has been to Paris. / They have gone to the park.
variants: have been in/at(在某地待了一段时间)：I have been in Beijing for three years.

name: Be busy doing/Have trouble/difficulty doing-固定构式
template: 主语 + be busy + doing / 主语 + have trouble/difficulty + (in) doing
usageNote: be busy doing(忙于做)；have trouble/difficulty (in) doing(做……有困难)；have fun (in) doing(做……有趣)。
example: He is busy preparing for the exam. / I have trouble (in) understanding him. / She had difficulty (in) solving the problem. / We had fun playing games.
variants: have a good time (in) doing / spend time (in) doing / waste time (in) doing

name: It takes/took sb. time to do-时间花费构式
template: It takes/took + 某人 + 时间 + to do
usageNote: it作形式主语，真正的主语是后面的不定式。
example: It takes me an hour to get to school. / It took him three days to finish the work. / It will take us two hours to drive there.
variants: spend time (in) doing / spend time on sth.：I spent an hour doing my homework.

name: It is/was + adj./n. + for/of sb. to do-评价构式
template: It is/was + 形容词/名词 + for/of + 某人 + to do
usageNote: for sb.用于形容词描述事物特征(difficult, easy, important等)；of sb.用于形容词描述人的品质(kind, nice, clever, foolish等)。
example: It is important for us to learn English. / It is kind of you to help me. / It was foolish of him to say that. / It is easy for her to solve.
variants: It is + adj. + that从句：It is important that we learn English.

name: Sb. happen to do/occur to sb./seem to do-非人称构式
template: 主语 + happen to do / It occurs to sb. that... / 主语 + seem to do
function: '碰巧做/某人突然想起/似乎做'
usageNote: happen to do(碰巧做)；It occurs to sb. that...(某人突然想起)；seem to do(似乎做)。
example: I happened to meet him. / It occurred to me that I had forgotten my key. / He seems to know everything. / There seems to be a mistake.
variants: It strikes sb. that... / It hits sb. that...(某人突然想到)

name: See/Hear/Watch/Notice/Observe/Find + sb. do/doing-感官动词构式
template: 主语 + see/hear/watch/notice/observe/find + 宾语 + do/doing/done
coreWords: see(v.), hear(v.), watch(v.), notice(v.), observe(v.), find(v.)
function: '看见/听见/注意到某人做某事'
usageNote: 感官动词后接宾补：do(全过程/经常性)；doing(正在进行)；done(被动/完成)。
example: I saw him enter the room. / I heard her singing. / I found him lying on the floor. / I saw the window broken.
variants: 被动时to还原：He was seen to enter the room.

name: Have/Make/Let + sb. + do-使役动词构式
template: 主语 + have/make/let + 宾语 + do(不带to的不定式)
usageNote: have/make/let后接宾语+不带to的不定式。get后接带to的不定式。
example: I had him repair my car. / She made me laugh. / Let me help you. / I'll get him to do it.
variants: have sb. doing(让某人一直做)；have sth. done(让某事被做)

name: Keep/Have/Leave + sb./sth. + doing/done/adj./介词短语-宾补构式
template: 主语 + keep/have/leave + 宾语 + doing/done/adj./介词短语
example: Keep the door open. / Don't leave me waiting. / I had my hair cut. / He left the work unfinished. / Please keep me informed.
variants: keep sb. from doing(阻止某人做)：Keep him from making mistakes.

name: Stop/Prevent/Keep/Discourage + sb. from doing-阻止构式
template: 主语 + stop/prevent/keep/discourage + sb. + from + doing
usageNote: stop/prevent/keep/discourage sb. from doing都表示阻止某人做某事。from在stop后常可省略(美式英语)，在prevent/keep后不可省略。
example: Stop him (from) running away. / Prevent him from leaving. / Keep him from making mistakes. / Discourage him from smoking.
variants: hinder sb. from doing / prohibit sb. from doing

name: See to/Look forward to/Object to/Stick to + doing-介词to构式
template: 主语 + see to/look forward to/object to/stick to/confess to/admit to + doing
coreWords: see(v.), look(v.), forward(ad.), object(v.), stick(v.), to(prep.), confess(v.), admit(v.)
usageNote: 某些短语中to是介词而非不定式符号，后须接动名词。常见：look forward to, object to, stick to, get down to, devote...to, be used to, be accustomed to, prefer...to, pay attention to, contribute to, lead to。
example: I look forward to meeting you. / He objects to changing the plan. / She sticks to her decision. / I am used to getting up early.
variants: be devoted to doing / be addicted to doing / be opposed to doing

name: Can't help/Can't stand/Can't bear + doing-情感动词构式
template: 主语 + can't help + doing / 主语 + can't stand/bear + doing
usageNote: can't help doing(忍不住做)；can't stand/bear doing(无法忍受做)。
example: I can't help laughing. / She can't stand waiting. / I can't bear being alone. / He can't help crying when he watches sad films.
variants: can't help but do = can't help doing：I can't help but laugh.

name: Be worth/Be worthy of/Be worthy to be done-价值构式
template: 主语 + be worth + doing / 主语 + be worthy of + being done / 主语 + be worthy + to be done
usageNote: be worth doing(主动表被动)；be worthy of being done；be worthy to be done。
example: The book is worth reading. / The film is worthy of being watched. / He is worthy to be chosen. / The museum is worth visiting.
variants: be worth + 名词：It is worth the money.

name: Too...to/Enough to/So...as to/Only to-不定式结果构式
template: 主语 + be/动词 + too + adj./adv. + to do / adj./adv. + enough + to do
function: '太……以至于不能/足够……能/结果是'
usageNote: too...to(太……不能)；adj./adv. + enough to(足够……能)；so...as to(如此……以至于)；only to(结果却……表示意外)。
example: He is too young to go to school. / She is old enough to go to school. / He got up early only to find the shop closed. / I am so tired as to fall asleep.
variants: too...not to(双重否定=肯定)：He is too wise not to see that.

name: It is no good/use/point doing-评价构式
coreWords: it(pron.), no(ad.), good(n./a.), use(n.), point(n.), sense(n.)
usageNote: it is no good/use/point/sense后接动名词。相当于there is no use/point in doing。
example: It is no use crying over spilt milk. / It is no good waiting here. / It is no point arguing. / It makes no sense worrying.

name: 倍数比较构式
template: A + be + 倍数 + as + adj.原级 + as + B / A + be + 倍数 + 比较级 + than + B
usageNote: 倍数表达：1) A is + 倍数 + as + adj. + as B；2) A is + 倍数 + 比较级 + than B；3) A is + 倍数 + the size/length/width of B。
example: This room is twice as big as that one. / This room is twice bigger than that one. / The river is three times the width of that one.

name: 主谓一致-就近原则
template: Either...or... / Neither...nor... / Not only...but also... / There be + A + and + B
coreWords: either(a./pron.), neither(a./pron.), nor(conj.), not(ad.), only(ad.), but(conj.), also(ad.), there(ad.)
function: 谓语动词与最近的主语保持一致
usageNote: either...or..., neither...nor..., not only...but also..., there be句型等遵循就近原则。
example: Either you or he is wrong. / Neither Tom nor his friends are coming. / Not only you but also he is wrong. / There is a pen and two books.
variants: Either the students or the teacher knows the answer.

name: 主谓一致-就远原则
template: 主语 + with/together with/as well as/along with/besides/except/but/rather than + 其他 + 谓语
coreWords: with(prep.), together(ad.), as(conj.), well(ad.), along(prep.), besides(prep.), except(prep.), but(prep.), rather(ad.)
function: 谓语动词与前面的主语保持一致
usageNote: 主语后有with, together with, as well as, along with, besides, except, but, rather than等插入成分时，谓语与前面的主语一致。
example: Tom with his parents is going to Beijing. / The teacher as well as the students likes the film. / I, rather than you, am responsible.
variants: Tom, together with his friends, is playing.

name: 主谓一致-集合名词
template: 集体名词 + 谓语(单数/复数)
coreWords: family, team, class, group, crowd, audience, committee, government, public
function: 集合名词谓语用单数或复数
usageNote: 集合名词表示整体时用单数谓语；表示成员时用复数谓语。常见集合名词：family, team, class, group, crowd, audience, committee, police, cattle等。
example: My family is large.(整体) / My family are watching TV.(成员) / The team is the best.(整体) / The team are playing well.(成员)
variants: The police are searching.(police等词只用复数)

name: There is something/nothing wrong with...-存在问题构式
template: There is something/nothing wrong with + 名词/代词/动名词
usageNote: There is something wrong with...表示'……出了毛病'；There is nothing wrong with...表示'……没问题'。
example: There is something wrong with my bike. / There is nothing wrong with your answer. / Is there anything wrong with him?
variants: What's wrong with...? / What's the matter with...?

name: Sb. be to blame-主动表被动构式
template: 主语 + be to blame + (for + 名词)
function: '该受责备/该负责'，主动形式表被动意义
usageNote: be to blame主动形式表被动意义，表示'该受责备/应负责'。不用be to be blamed。
example: He is to blame for the accident. / Who is to blame? / I am to blame for the mistake.
variants: be to blame for = be responsible for

name: Can't/Can never...enough/too...-再……也不为过
template: 主语 + can't/can never + 动词 + enough/too + adj./adv.
usageNote: can't...enough/too表示'无论怎样……都不过分'。
example: You can't be too careful. / I can't thank you enough. / A book can never be too interesting. / You can never be too kind.

name: 祈使句+附带问句(反义疑问句)
template: 祈使句 + , + will you/won't you/can you/would you?
function: 祈使句附加问句，使语气更委婉
usageNote: 肯定祈使句+will you/won't you/can you/would you；否定祈使句+will you；Let's+shall we；Let us(请求)+will you。
example: Pass me the salt, will you? / Be quiet, won't you? / Don't forget, will you? / Let's go, shall we?
variants: Open the door, would you?(礼貌) / Give me a hand, can you?(请求)

name: What if...?假设构式
template: What if + 主语 + 谓语?
usageNote: what if表示'如果……将会怎样'，后接陈述语序。
example: What if he doesn't come? / What if it rains? / What if I fail? / What if she says no?

name: When it comes to...-提及构式
template: When it comes to + 名词/动名词 + , + 主句
usageNote: when it comes to为固定搭配，表示'当谈到/涉及到……时'。
example: When it comes to English, he is the best. / When it comes to cooking, she knows everything. / When it comes to money, he is careful.

name: Not all/both/every...+肯定谓语-部分否定构式
coreWords: not(ad.), all(pron.), both(a./pron.), every(a.), each(a./pron.), always(ad.)
usageNote: not与all, both, every, each, always等连用表示部分否定。
example: Not all students like English.(并非所有学生都喜欢英语) / Both of them are not doctors.(并非两人都是医生) / I don't know everything.(我并非什么都知道)

name: All/None/Nothing/Nobody/Neither/No one...+肯定谓语-全部否定
template: None/Nothing/Nobody/Neither/No one/Not either + 肯定谓语
coreWords: none(pron.), nothing(pron.), nobody(pron.), neither(a./pron.), no(ad.), not(ad.)
usageNote: none, nothing, nobody, neither, no one, not either等表示全部否定。
example: None of them came. / Nobody knows the answer. / Nothing is impossible. / Neither of them is right. / No one can do it.
variants: all...not(部分否定) vs. none...(全部否定)

name: All that glitters is not gold-部分否定谚语构式
function: '闪光的并不都是金子'，部分否定
usageNote: all...is not...为部分否定，'并非所有……都……'。
example: All that glitters is not gold. / All men are not honest. / Both answers are not right.
variants: None that glitters is gold.(全部否定)

name: One...the other.../Some...others...-不定代词指代构式
template: One...the other... / Some...others...the others... / Some...the others...
function: 不定代词指代两个或多个事物/人
usageNote: one...the other...(两者中的一个……另一个)；some...others...(一些……另一些……)；some...the others...(一些……其余的全部……)。
example: I have two brothers. One is a doctor, the other is a teacher. / Some students like math, others prefer English. / Some came, the others stayed home.

name: Used to do / Would do-过去习惯构式
template: 主语 + used to + do / 主语 + would + do
usageNote: used to do(过去常常做，现在不做了，可表示状态或动作)；would do(过去常常做，只表示动作，不表示状态)。
example: I used to play tennis. / He would walk to school. / There used to be a shop here.(状态，不用would)
variants: used to的否定：I didn't use to... / I usedn't to...

name: Be used to doing / Get used to doing-习惯构式
template: 主语 + be/get used to + doing / 名词
usageNote: be used to doing(习惯于做，表状态)；get used to doing(变得习惯于做，表变化)。to为介词。
example: I am used to getting up early. / She got used to living alone. / He is used to hard work. / You'll get used to the weather.

name: Need / Dare + do-情态动词构式
template: 主语 + need/dare + do(否定/疑问) / 主语 + need/dare + to do(肯定陈述)
function: need/dare作情态动词或实义动词
usageNote: need/dare可作情态动词(否定/疑问直接用，第三人称不加s)或实义动词(need/dare to do，有人称和数的变化)。
example: Need I come?(情态) / You needn't worry.(情态) / He needs to go.(实义) / Dare he try?(情态) / He dares to speak.(实义) / He doesn't dare (to) go.
variants: I dare say(固定用法)：I dare say he will come.

name: Help oneself to-自用构式
usageNote: help oneself to表示'请自便/随便吃/用'。
example: Help yourself to some tea. / Please help yourselves to the food. / Make yourself at home.

name: Make oneself + done/understood/heard-使自己被……构式
usageNote: make oneself + 过去分词，表示使自己处于某种状态。
example: I couldn't make myself understood. / Speak louder to make yourself heard. / He made himself known to everyone.

name: There is no doing...-不可能构式
template: There is no + doing / 名词
usageNote: there is no doing表示'不可能做/无法做'。
example: There is no knowing what will happen. / There is no telling when he will come. / There is no denying the fact.

name: There is something/nothing wrong with...-问题构式
template: There is something/nothing wrong with + 名词/代词
usageNote: there is something wrong with...表示'……出了问题'。
example: There is something wrong with my bike. / There is nothing wrong with you. / Is there anything wrong with the machine?
variants: What's wrong with...? / What's the matter with...?

name: There is no need/point/sense/harm/good in doing-评价构式
template: There is no need/point/sense/harm/good + in + doing
coreWords: there(ad.), no(ad.), need(n.), point(n.), sense(n.), harm(n.), good(n.), in(prep.)
function: '没有必要/没有意义/没有害处做……'
usageNote: there is no need/point/sense/harm/good in doing表示没有必要/意义/害处/好处做某事。
example: There is no need in worrying. / There is no point in arguing. / There is no harm in trying. / There is no sense in waiting.
variants: in可省略：There is no use crying.

name: Be supposed to / Be expected to-期望构式
template: 主语 + be supposed/expected/required/requested + to do
usageNote: be supposed to do(应该做/被期望做)；be expected to do(被期望做)；be required/requested to do(被要求做)。
example: You are supposed to finish by Friday. / He is expected to arrive soon. / You are required to wear a uniform.

name: Be to blame for / Be responsible for-责任构式
template: 主语 + be to blame for + 名词 / 主语 + be responsible for + 名词
usageNote: be to blame for(主动表被动)；be responsible for(对……负责)。
example: He is to blame for the accident. / Who is responsible for this? / I am responsible for the mistake.

name: Be satisfied with / Be content with / Be happy with-满意构式
template: 主语 + be satisfied/content/happy/pleased/delighted + with + 名词/动名词
coreWords: satisfy(v.), content(a./n.), happy(a.), pleased(a.), delighted(a.), with(prep.)
usageNote: be satisfied/content/happy/pleased/delighted with表示对某事满意或高兴。
example: I am satisfied with the result. / She is content with her life. / Are you happy with your job? / He was pleased with the gift.
variants: be satisfied that从句：I am satisfied that he did his best.

name: Be proud of / Take pride in-自豪构式
template: 主语 + be proud of + 名词/动名词 / 主语 + take pride in + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be proud of = take pride in。of/in后接名词或动名词。
example: I am proud of my country. / She takes pride in her work. / He is proud of having won. / We are proud of being Chinese.

name: Be afraid of / Be frightened of / Be scared of-害怕构式
template: 主语 + be afraid/frightened/scared/terrified + of + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be afraid/frightened/scared/terrified of表示害怕某事。be afraid to do(不敢做，强调勇气)；be afraid of doing(害怕某事发生)。
example: I am afraid of dogs. / She is frightened of flying. / He is scared of the dark. / I am afraid to tell him.
variants: be afraid that从句：I am afraid (that) he won't come.

name: Be aware of / Be conscious of-意识构式
template: 主语 + be aware/conscious/mindful + of + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be aware/conscious/mindful of表示意识到或知道某事。
example: I am aware of the problem. / He was conscious of being watched. / We should be mindful of the environment.
variants: be aware that从句：I am aware that time is limited.

name: Be capable of / Be able to-能力构式
template: 主语 + be capable of + 名词/动名词 / 主语 + be able to + do
usageNote: be capable of doing(能够做，可用于各种时态)；be able to do(能够做，有各种时态形式)；can(只有现在和过去时could)。
example: He is capable of solving the problem. / She is able to speak French. / I was able to finish on time. / He will be able to come.

name: Be grateful to sb. for sth. / Be thankful to sb. for sth.-感激构式
template: 主语 + be grateful/thankful/obliged + to + 人 + for + 名词/动名词
usageNote: be grateful/thankful/obliged to sb. for sth.表示因某事感谢某人。
example: I am grateful to you for your help. / She is thankful to her parents for their support. / I feel obliged to you for your kindness.

name: Be familiar with / Be strange to-熟悉/陌生构式
template: 主语 + be familiar/acquainted + with + 名词 / 名词 + be familiar/strange + to + 人
usageNote: sb. be familiar with sth.(某人熟悉某物)；sth. be familiar to sb.(某物为某人所熟悉)；sth. be strange to sb.(某物对某人来说陌生)。
example: I am familiar with this place. / This place is familiar to me. / The custom is strange to me. / I am acquainted with him.

name: Be different from / Be the same as-异同构式
template: A + be different from / unlike / distinct from + B / A + be the same as / similar to + B
coreWords: different(a.), same(a.), similar(a.), from(prep.), as(prep.), to(prep.)
usageNote: be different from(与……不同)；be the same as(与……相同)；be similar to(与……相似)。
example: My opinion is different from yours. / Your bag is the same as mine. / Cats are similar to tigers. / His case is unlike mine.
variants: A differs from B. / A is identical to B.

name: Be full of / Be filled with-充满构式
template: 主语 + be full of + 名词 / 主语 + be filled with/crowded with + 名词
usageNote: be full of(充满，表状态)；be filled with(被……充满，可表动作完成)；be crowded with(挤满)。
example: The room is full of people. / The bottle is filled with water. / The street was crowded with shoppers. / Her eyes were full of tears.

name: Be short of / Be lack of / Be lacking in-缺乏构式
template: 主语 + be short of / be lacking in / lack + 名词
usageNote: be short of(缺少)；be lacking in(在……方面缺乏)；lack(及物动词，直接接宾语)。
example: We are short of time. / He is lacking in courage. / She lacks experience. / The area is short of water.
variants: for lack of(因缺乏)：The plan failed for lack of money.

name: Be rich in / Be poor in-贫富构式
template: 主语 + be rich/poor/abundant/deficient + in + 名词
usageNote: be rich/abundant in(富含)；be poor/deficient in(缺乏)。
example: The area is rich in minerals. / He is poor in experience. / The soil is abundant in nutrients. / She is deficient in vitamin D.

name: Be strict with sb. in sth. / Be hard on sb.-严格构式
template: 主语 + be strict with + 人 + in + 事 / 主语 + be hard on + 人
usageNote: be strict with sb.(对某人严格)；be strict in sth.(对某事严格)；be hard on sb.(对某人苛刻)。
example: The teacher is strict with us. / He is strict in his work. / Don't be too hard on him. / Parents should be strict with children.
variants: be severe with / be rigorous in

name: Be popular with / Be welcomed by-受欢迎构式
template: 主语 + be popular with / among + 人 / 主语 + be welcomed by + 人
usageNote: be popular with/among(受……欢迎)；be welcomed by(受到……欢迎)。
example: The singer is popular with young people. / This book is popular among students. / He was welcomed by everyone.

name: Be famous/known/celebrated/noted/renowned for/as/to-闻名构式
template: 主语 + be famous/known/celebrated/noted/renowned + for/as/to + 名词
coreWords: famous(a.), known(a.), celebrate(v.), note(v./n.), renowned(a.), for(prep.), as(prep.), to(prep.)
usageNote: be famous/known for(因……闻名)；be famous/known as(作为……闻名)；be known to(为……所知)。
example: He is famous for his novels. / He is known as a writer. / He is known to everyone. / The city is celebrated for its beauty.

name: Be patient with / Be impatient with-耐心构式
template: 主语 + be patient/impatient/angry/annoyed/furious + with + 人
coreWords: patient(a.), impatient(a.), angry(a.), annoyed(a.), furious(a.), with(prep.)
usageNote: be patient/impatient with sb.(对某人有耐心/不耐烦)；be angry/annoyed/furious with sb.(对某人生气)。
example: Be patient with children. / She is impatient with delay. / Don't be angry with me. / He was furious with his son.
variants: be angry at/about + 事(对事生气)

name: Be dependent on / Be independent of-依赖/独立构式
template: 主语 + be dependent/reliant/conditional/contingent + on/upon + 名词 / 主语 + be independent of + 名词
usageNote: be dependent/reliant on(依赖于)；be independent of(独立于)。
example: Children are dependent on their parents. / She is independent of her family. / Success is dependent on effort. / The plan is contingent on the weather.

name: break one's heart
example: It broke my heart to say goodbye to my best friend.

name: break the rules
usageNote: 半习语。字面意义与比喻意义接近
example: If you break the rules, you will be punished.

name: break the law
example: Anyone who breaks the law must pay the price.

name: break the news
usageNote: 习语。将消息首次告知他人
example: The doctor had to break the news to the family.

name: break a promise
example: You should never break a promise to a friend.

name: break a record
usageNote: 半习语。创造新的最高/最低成绩
example: She broke a record in the swimming competition.

name: break down
function: （机器）故障；（谈判）破裂；（人）情绪崩溃
usageNote: 短语动词，多义。非组成性
example: The car broke down on the highway. / He broke down in tears.

name: break in
usageNote: 短语动词，多义。非组成性
example: Thieves broke in while we were away. / Please don't break in when others are talking.

name: break into
usageNote: 短语动词。后接地点或活动
example: Someone broke into our house last night. / She broke into tears.

name: break off
usageNote: 短语动词。表示突然终止关系或动作
example: They broke off their engagement. / He broke off a piece of bread.

name: break out
usageNote: 短语动词，非组成性。主语常为灾难性事件
example: War broke out in 1939. / Fire broke out in the building.

name: break through
usageNote: 短语动词。克服障碍取得进展
example: Scientists have broken through in cancer research.

name: break up
function: 分手；（学期）结束；解散；打碎
usageNote: 短语动词，多义。非组成性
example: They broke up after three years. / School breaks up next week.

name: bring about
usageNote: 短语动词。后接变化/结果
example: The new policy brought about great changes.

name: bring back
usageNote: 短语动词。字面与比喻义并存
example: Please bring back the book tomorrow. / The song brings back memories.

name: bring down
usageNote: 短语动词。使某物/某人下降或失败
example: The scandal brought down the government.

name: bring forward
usageNote: 短语动词。将时间或议题前移
example: The meeting was brought forward to Monday.

name: bring in
usageNote: 短语动词。使进入或产生收益
example: The company brings in millions each year.

name: bring on
usageNote: 短语动词。常指引起不良后果
example: Stress can bring on headaches.

name: bring out
usageNote: 短语动词。使内在品质显露或产品上市
example: The new model will be brought out next month.

name: bring up
usageNote: 短语动词，多义。非组成性
example: She brought up three children alone. / Don't bring up that topic.

name: call back
example: I will call you back after the meeting.

name: call for
usageNote: 短语动词。表示需求或公开要求
example: The situation calls for immediate action.

name: call in
usageNote: 短语动词。请来专业人士或短暂拜访
example: We had to call in a doctor.

name: call off
usageNote: 短语动词。取消已计划的活动
example: The game was called off because of rain.

name: call on
usageNote: 短语动词。后接人表示拜访，接不定式表示要求
example: I called on my teacher yesterday. / The president called on citizens to save water.

name: call out
example: She called out for help when she saw the fire.

name: call up
function: 打电话；征召入伍；使回忆起
usageNote: 短语动词。美式英语中常用
example: I'll call you up tonight.

name: come about
usageNote: 短语动词。表示事情如何发生
example: How did this situation come about?

name: come across
example: I came across an old photo in the drawer.

name: come along
usageNote: 短语动词。催促或描述进展
example: Come along! We're going to be late.

name: come apart
example: The old chair came apart when I sat on it.

name: come around
function: 恢复知觉；改变看法；来访
usageNote: 短语动词。非组成性，多义
example: She came around after fainting. / He'll come around to our idea.

name: come back
usageNote: 短语动词。字面与比喻义并存
example: Come back soon! / It came back to me suddenly.

name: come by
example: Good jobs are hard to come by these days.

name: come down
usageNote: 短语动词。字面或比喻下降
example: Prices have come down recently.

name: come from
example: I come from a small village.

name: come in
example: Come in, please. / Short skirts came in last year.

name: come off
usageNote: 短语动词。非组成性多义
example: The button came off my coat. / The plan came off well.

name: come on
example: Come on! You can do it!

name: come out
usageNote: 短语动词。多义，非组成性
example: Her new book came out last week. / The truth came out.

name: come over
example: Come over to my house this evening.

name: come to
usageNote: 短语动词。非组成性多义
example: The bill came to fifty dollars. / She came to after ten minutes.

name: come true
example: Her dream of becoming a doctor came true.

name: come up
example: A new problem came up at the meeting.

name: come up with
example: She came up with a great idea for the project.

name: cut down
usageNote: 短语动词。减少数量或大小
example: We need to cut down expenses.

name: cut in
example: Don't cut in when others are speaking.

name: cut off
usageNote: 短语动词。使分离或停止供应
example: We were cut off during the phone call.

name: cut out
example: Cut out the picture from the paper.

name: cut up
example: Cut up the vegetables before cooking.

name: do away with
example: The government did away with the old law.

name: do without
usageNote: 短语动词。无需某物而应付
example: We can't do without water.

name: do one's best
example: I will do my best to help you.

name: do harm to
example: Smoking does harm to your health.

name: do good to
example: Reading does good to your mind.

name: do sb. a favor
example: Could you do me a favor and pass the salt?

name: fall asleep
usageNote: 习语。从清醒状态进入睡眠
example: He fell asleep during the boring lecture.

name: fall behind
example: Don't fall behind with your payments.

name: fall down
usageNote: 短语动词。字面或比喻失败
example: The old building fell down in the earthquake.

name: fall ill
example: She fell ill after getting caught in the rain.

name: fall in love
example: They fell in love at first sight.

name: fall off
example: The number of students fell off this year.

name: fall out
example: They fell out over money.

name: fall over
example: He fell over a stone in the path.

name: fall through
example: Our plans fell through at the last minute.

name: get across
usageNote: 短语动词。使想法被理解
example: I couldn't get my point across.

name: get ahead
example: Hard work helps you get ahead in life.

name: get along
example: Do you get along with your classmates?

name: get away
example: The thief got away in a stolen car.

name: get away with
example: He got away with cheating on the test.

name: get back
usageNote: 短语动词。返回或重新获得
example: When did you get back from your trip?

name: get by
example: I get by on my small salary.

name: get down
example: Get down the main points of the speech.

name: get in
example: We must get in the wheat before the rain.

name: get into
example: Don't get into trouble at school.

name: get off
example: I get off at the next stop.

name: get on
example: Get on the bus at the school gate.

name: get out
example: The news got out quickly.

name: get out of
example: He got out of doing his homework.

name: get over
example: She got over her illness quickly.

name: get rid of
example: Get rid of those old clothes.

name: get together
example: Let's get together this weekend.

name: get up
example: I get up at six every morning.

name: give away
example: Don't give away the ending of the story.

name: give back
example: Please give back the books by Friday.

name: give in
example: She finally gave in to their demands.

name: give off
usageNote: 短语动词。释放某种东西
example: The flowers give off a sweet smell.

name: give out
example: The teacher gave out the exam papers.

name: give up
example: Never give up on your dreams.

name: go ahead
usageNote: 短语动词。给予许可或主动进行
example: Go ahead and ask your question.

name: go away
example: Go away! I'm busy right now.

name: go by
example: As time goes by, things change.

name: go down
example: The ship went down in the storm.

name: go off
example: The bomb went off in the city center.

name: go on
example: What's going on here?

name: go out
example: The lights went out during the storm.

name: go over
example: Let's go over the plan again.

name: go through
example: She's going through a hard time.

name: go up
example: Prices have gone up again.

name: go wrong
example: Something went wrong with the machine.

name: hand in
example: Please hand in your homework tomorrow.

name: hand out
example: The teacher handed out the books.

name: hand over
example: The thief was handed over to the police.

name: hold back
example: She couldn't hold back her tears.

name: hold on
example: Hold on a minute, please.

name: hold out
example: We can hold out for a few more days.

name: hold up
example: Sorry, I was held up in traffic.

name: keep away
example: Keep away from the fire.

name: keep in mind
example: Keep in mind that time is limited.

name: keep off
example: Keep off the grass.

name: keep on
example: Keep on trying and you will succeed.

name: keep up
usageNote: 短语动词。维持某种状态
example: Keep up the good work!

name: keep up with
example: I can't keep up with the news.

name: look after
example: Please look after your little sister.

name: look at
example: Look at the blackboard, please.

name: look back
example: Looking back, I see many mistakes.

name: look down on
example: Don't look down on people who are poor.

name: look for
example: I'm looking for my lost keys.

name: look forward to
example: I look forward to seeing you again.

name: look into
example: The police are looking into the case.

name: look out
example: Look out! A car is coming!

name: look up
example: Look up the word in the dictionary.

name: look up to
example: Children should look up to their parents.

name: make out
example: I can't make out what he's saying.

name: make up
usageNote: 短语动词。多义，非组成性
example: Women make up half the workforce.

name: make up for
example: I will make up for lost time.

name: make up one's mind
example: I finally made up my mind to study abroad.

name: make a difference
example: Your help made a big difference.

name: make a living
example: He makes a living by selling fruit.

name: make friends
example: She finds it easy to make friends.

name: make fun of
example: Don't make fun of others' mistakes.

name: make progress
example: You have made great progress in English.

name: make sense
example: This sentence doesn't make sense.

name: make sure
example: Make sure you lock the door.

name: make the bed
example: I make the bed every morning.

name: make use of
example: We should make use of every chance.

name: put away
example: Put away your toys after playing.

name: put down
example: Put down your ideas on paper.

name: put forward
example: He put forward a good suggestion.

name: put off
example: Don't put off your homework.

name: put on
example: Put on your coat. It's cold outside.

name: put out
example: Put out the fire before you leave.

name: put up
example: They put up a tent by the lake.

name: put up with
example: I can't put up with the noise.

name: take away
example: The medicine will take away the pain.

name: take care of
example: Take care of yourself on the trip.

name: take in
example: I was taken in by his lies.

name: take off
example: The plane took off on time.

name: take on
example: The company is taking on new workers.

name: take over
example: The new manager took over last week.

name: take place
example: The concert will take place next Friday.

name: take pride in
example: She takes pride in her work.

name: take up
example: He took up painting last year.

name: turn around
usageNote: 短语动词。转向相反方向
example: Turn around and look at me.

name: turn down
example: She turned down the invitation.

name: turn into
example: The frog turned into a prince.

name: turn off
example: Turn off the TV before bed.

name: turn on
example: Turn on the light, please.

name: turn out
example: It turned out that she was right.

name: turn over
example: Turn over the page to continue.

name: turn to
example: When in trouble, turn to your parents.

name: turn up
example: Turn up the music! / He didn't turn up for work.

name: above all
usageNote: 插入语。用于强调最重要之点
example: Above all, you must be honest.

name: after all
usageNote: 插入语。表示提醒已知事实
example: He is a child, after all.

name: all of a sudden
example: All of a sudden, the lights went out.

name: as a matter of fact
example: As a matter of fact, I do know her.

name: as a result
example: He worked hard. As a result, he passed.

name: as for
example: As for me, I prefer tea.

name: as usual
usageNote: 插入语。描述习惯性行为
example: He arrived late, as usual.

name: as well
example: She speaks French as well.

name: as well as
example: She is clever as well as beautiful.

name: at all
usageNote: 插入语。用于否定/疑问加强语气
example: I don't like it at all.

name: at first
example: At first, I didn't understand.

name: at last
example: At last, the bus came.

name: at least
example: It costs at least ten dollars.

name: at once
example: Come here at once!

name: at present
example: At present, I am very busy.

name: at the same time
example: Don't speak at the same time.

name: because of
usageNote: 介词短语。后接名词/代词
example: He was late because of the rain.

name: before long
example: Before long, the rain stopped.

name: by accident
example: I found it by accident.

name: by all means
example: By all means, help yourself.

name: by chance
example: I met her by chance at the store.

name: by heart
example: Learn this poem by heart.

name: by mistake
example: I took your bag by mistake.

name: by no means
example: This is by no means easy.

name: by oneself
example: She lives by herself.

name: by the way
example: By the way, where is your sister?

name: day after day
example: Day after day, he did the same work.

name: day and night
example: They worked day and night.

name: except for
example: The room is empty except for a desk.

name: face to face
example: They talked face to face.

name: far from
example: The result is far from perfect.

name: first of all
example: First of all, let me introduce myself.

name: for example
example: Many fruits, for example, apples, are red.

name: for fear of
example: He spoke quietly for fear of waking her.

name: for free
example: I got this book for free.

name: for good
