usageNote: 词源：under-来自古英语，表'在下'；line来自古英语line（绳、线）。underline字面义'在下方划线'。作为'强调'义是18世纪后的隐喻用法。under-与名词结合能产性中等。
example: Please underline the new words in red so you can find them easily later.
variants: underline (n.下划线), underlined (adj.划线的), line→underline (对), emphasize (v.强调), highlight (v.突出显示)

name: underground
template: under- + N → 在下形容词/副词/名词
coreWords: under-（在下前缀）+ ground（地面，来自3500词）
function: 表示在地面以下的位置或活动
usageNote: 词源：under-表'在下'；ground来自古英语grund。underground字面义'在地面下'。该词可兼作形容词、副词和名词（如the underground地铁）。under-与名词结合表空间位置能产性高。
example: Some animals live underground to keep safe from the cold and from enemies.
variants: underground (n.地铁), under→over (反义), subway (n.地铁), overground (adj.地面上的)

name: underage
coreWords: under-（不足前缀）+ age（年龄，来自3500词）
function: 表示未达到法定或规定年龄的
usageNote: 词源：under-在此表'不足、低于'；age来自古法语aage。underage字面义'年龄不足'。under-表'不足'是重要语义分支：underpaid, undervalued, underweight等。该构式能产性高。
example: Underage children are not allowed to buy drinks in this shop.
variants: underage (adj.未成年的), underpaid (adj.工资过低的), underweight (adj.体重不足的), overage (adj.超龄的→underage反义)

name: combine
coreWords: com-（共同前缀）+ bine（来自拉丁语bini=两个两个地）
function: 表示将不同元素合为一体
usageNote: 词源：combine来自中古法语combiner，源自晚期拉丁语combinare（com-共同+bini两个两个地）。bine本身非独立英语词。15世纪进入英语。combine强调'融合为一体'，而非简单放在一起。该构式为半能产型。
example: We can combine flour and water to make a simple kind of bread.
variants: combination (n.组合), combined (adj.联合的), mix (v.混合), blend (v.融合), separate (v.分开→combine反义)

name: communicate
coreWords: com-（共同前缀）+ municate（来自拉丁语munus=公共服务）
function: 表示通过语言、文字或行为与他人交换信息
usageNote: 词源：communicate来自拉丁语communicare（com-共同+munus任务/服务），字面义'使共同分享'。经古法语进入英语。munus本身非独立英语词。该构式高度词汇化。
example: Students should communicate with each other to solve the problem as a team.
variants: communication (n.交流), communicator (n.沟通者), community (n.社区), talk (v.交谈), interact (v.互动)

name: compete
coreWords: com-（共同前缀）+ pete（来自拉丁语petere=寻求/追求）
function: 表示与他人争夺同一目标或资源
usageNote: 词源：compete来自晚期拉丁语competere（com-共同+petere寻求），字面义'共同寻求'，引申为'竞争'。1620年进入英语。pete本身非独立英语词。该构式高度词汇化。
example: All the students in the class compete for the top prize each year.
variants: competition (n.竞争), competitor (n.竞争者), compete→cooperate (对), rival (n.对手), contest (v.争夺)

name: connect
coreWords: con-（共同前缀）+ nect（来自拉丁语nectere=捆绑/连接）
function: 表示建立联系或使事物相连
usageNote: 词源：connect来自拉丁语connectere（con-共同+nectere捆绑）。nect本身非独立英语词。late 13世纪经古法语connecter进入英语。connect强调'连接'的物理或抽象关系。
example: Please connect the red wire to the right side of the machine.
variants: connection (n.连接), connected (adj.连接的), connect→disconnect (反义), link (v.连接), join (v.加入)

name: contain
coreWords: con-（共同前缀）+ tain（来自拉丁语tenere=握住/保持）
function: 表示某物内部装有其他事物，或控制某种情感
usageNote: 词源：contain来自古法语contenir，源自拉丁语continere（con-共同+tenere握住），字面义'共同握住/容纳'。late 13世纪进入英语。tain本身非独立英语词，但tenere是重要拉丁词根。
example: This small box can contain all the tools you need for the job.
variants: container (n.容器), content (n.内容), contain→exclude (反义), hold (v.容纳), include (v.包含)

name: international
coreWords: inter-（之间前缀，来自拉丁语）+ nation（国家，来自3500词）+ -al（形容词后缀）
function: 表示涉及两个或多个国家的事务或关系
usageNote: 词源：inter-来自拉丁语，表'在…之间'；nation来自拉丁语natio（出生）；-al来自拉丁语-alis。international为18世纪创造的新词（边沁提出）。构式为能产型，inter-可与多种名词+-al结合。
example: The airport is an important international centre for travel and business.
variants: internationally (adv.国际上), internationalism (n.国际主义), national (adj.国家的), global (adj.全球的), foreign (adj.外国的)

name: internet
coreWords: inter-（之间前缀）+ net（网络，来自3500词）
function: 表示连接计算机的全球性网络系统
usageNote: 词源：inter-表'在…之间'；net来自古英语nett。internet为1970年代由inter-（互相）+ network（网络）缩合创造，特指全球TCP/IP网络。inter-与名词结合的构式能产性中等。
example: The internet helps people find news and learn new things every day.
variants: internet (n.互联网), network (n.网络), online (adj.在线的), web (n.网), intranet (n.内部网)

name: interview
template: inter- + N/V → 互相动词/名词
coreWords: inter-（之间前缀）+ view（看，来自3500词）
function: 表示两人面对面交谈，一方提问一方回答
usageNote: 词源：inter-表'在…之间'；view来自古法语veue（看）。interview字面义'互相看'，引申为'面谈'。16世纪进入英语，最初指'正式会面'。
example: I have an interview for a summer job at the book shop tomorrow.
variants: interview (v.采访), interviewer (n.面试官), interviewee (n.被面试者), meeting (n.会面), conversation (n.对话)

name: automatic
coreWords: auto-（自动前缀，来自希腊语）+ matic（来自希腊语-matos=自行思考的）
function: 表示无需人工干预即可自行运作的
usageNote: 词源：automatic来自希腊语automatos（auto-自己+matos思考的，来自词根men-思考）。automaton为同根词。该构式为半能产型，auto-主要用于科技词汇。
example: The automatic door opens when you walk close to it.
variants: automatically (adv.自动地), automation (n.自动化), automaton (n.自动装置), manual (adj.手动的→automatic反义), self-working (adj.自动的)

name: automobile
coreWords: auto-（自动前缀）+ mobile（移动的，来自拉丁语mobilis）
function: 表示可自动移动的交通工具，即汽车
usageNote: 词源：automobile来自法语automobile（auto-自动+mobile移动的），19世纪末创造以区别于马车。mobile来自拉丁语mobilis（movere移动）。auto-表'自动'主要用于技术领域。该构式为半能产型。
example: The automobile was invented more than one hundred years ago and changed how people travel.
variants: automobile (n.汽车=car), automotive (adj.汽车的), mobile (adj.移动的), vehicle (n.车辆), motorcar (n.汽车)

name: bicycle
coreWords: bi-（双前缀，来自拉丁语）+ cycle（循环/轮子，来自希腊语kyklos）
function: 表示有两个轮子的骑行交通工具
usageNote: 词源：bi-来自拉丁语bis（两次）；cycle来自希腊语kyklos（圆圈）。bicycle字面义'两个轮子'，19世纪中期创造。bi-表'双'能产性高：bicycle, bilingual, biweekly等。
example: He rides his bicycle to school every morning to keep healthy.
variants: bicycle (v.骑自行车), bicyclist (n.骑自行车的人), bike (n.自行车=口语), cycle (n.循环), tricycle (n.三轮车)

name: bilingual
coreWords: bi-（双前缀）+ lingual（语言的，来自拉丁语lingua=舌头/语言）
function: 表示能够说或使用两种语言的
usageNote: 词源：bi-表'双'；lingual来自拉丁语lingua（舌头、语言）。bilingual字面义'双语的'。该构式为半能产型，-lingual主要用于语言学语境：monolingual, bilingual, multilingual等。
example: A bilingual teacher can speak both English and Chinese in class.
variants: bilingualism (n.双语能力), lingual (adj.语言的), monolingual (adj.单语的), multilingual (adj.多语的), language (n.语言)

name: television
coreWords: tele-（远前缀）+ vision（视觉，来自拉丁语visio）
function: 表示通过电波传送远距离图像和声音的装置
usageNote: 词源：tele-表'远'；vision来自拉丁语visio（看），源自videre（看）。television为20世纪初混合构词（tele-希腊+vision拉丁），描述'远距离视觉'。该构式为半能产型。
example: Television brings news and stories from all over the world into our homes.
variants: television (n.电视=TV), televise (v.电视播放), vision (n.视觉), viewer (n.观众), broadcast (v.广播)

name: transport
coreWords: trans-（跨越前缀）+ port（搬运/携带，来自拉丁语portare）
function: 表示将人或货物从一个地方运送到另一个地方
usageNote: 词源：transport来自拉丁语transportare（trans-跨越+portare搬运）。14世纪进入英语。port本身在英语中为'港口'义，与拉丁portare（搬运）同源但语义分化。该构式为半能产型。
example: Buses are used to transport students from home to school each day.
variants: transportation (n.运输), transporter (n.运输工具), transport (n.运输), carry (v.携带), deliver (v.递送)

name: transparent
coreWords: trans-（穿越前缀）+ parent（出现的，来自拉丁语parere）
function: 表示光线可以穿透的，或动机容易看透的
usageNote: 词源：transparent来自拉丁语transparens（trans-穿越+parere出现），字面义'穿越而显现'，即'透明'。15世纪进入英语。parent在此非'父母'义，而是来自parere（出现）。该构式高度词汇化。
example: Clean water is transparent, so you can see the bottom of the glass.
variants: transparency (n.透明度), transparently (adv.透明地), clear (adj.清澈的), opaque (adj.不透明的→transparent反义), see-through (adj.透明的)

name: supermarket
coreWords: super-（超级前缀，来自拉丁语）+ market（市场，来自3500词）
function: 表示大型自助式零售商店
usageNote: 词源：super-来自拉丁语super（在上、超过）；market来自拉丁语mercatus。supermarket为1930年代美国创造，描述'超级规模的市场'。super-表'超级'在20世纪商业用语中很能产。
example: My mother goes to the supermarket every Saturday to buy food for the family.
variants: supermarket (n.超市), superstore (n.大卖场), market (n.市场), shop (n.商店), grocery (n.杂货店)

name: superman
coreWords: super-（超级前缀）+ man（人，来自3500词）
function: 表示拥有超凡能力的虚构英雄或能力超群的人
usageNote: 词源：super-表'超级'；man来自古英语mann。superman为尼采哲学概念Ubermensch的英译（1890年代），后成为漫画角色名。super-与名词结合表'超级'能产性中等：superstar, superpower等。
example: In the story, the superman could fly faster than any bird in the sky.
variants: superman (n.超人), superhero (n.超级英雄), superwoman (n.超级女性), superstar (n.巨星), ordinary man (n.普通人→superman反义)

name: preview
coreWords: pre-（在前前缀，来自拉丁语）+ view（看，来自3500词）
function: 表示在正式放映/出版前提前观看
usageNote: 词源：pre-来自拉丁语prae（在前）；view来自古法语veue。preview字面义'提前看'。20世纪初期用于电影行业。pre-表'在前'能产性极高：prepare, predict, preview等。
example: The teacher gave us a preview of what we will learn in the next unit.
variants: preview (v.预习/预演), pre-view (n.预先观看), view (v.看), review (v.复习→preview对), foresight (n.远见)

name: prepare
coreWords: pre-（在前前缀）+ pare（准备，来自拉丁语parare）
function: 表示事先做好安排或准备工作
usageNote: 词源：prepare来自拉丁语praeparare（pre-在前+parare准备），字面义'提前准备'。15世纪经古法语parer进入英语。pare本身非独立英语词（英语中pare意为'削皮'，来源不同）。该构式高度词汇化。
example: Please prepare your books and pens before the class begins.
variants: preparation (n.准备), prepared (adj.准备好的), prepare→unprepared (反义), ready (v.准备), arrange (v.安排)

name: subway
coreWords: sub-（在下前缀，来自拉丁语）+ way（道路，来自3500词）
function: 表示在地下运行的大众交通系统
usageNote: 词源：sub-来自拉丁语sub（在下）；way来自古英语weg。subway字面义'下面的道路'。该词在19世纪末期用于描述地下铁路。sub-与名词结合表'在下'能产性中等。
example: The subway is the fastest way to travel across the busy city in the morning.
variants: subway (n.地铁=underground/metro), underpass (n.地下通道), way (n.道路), surface road (n.地面道路→subway反义)

name: suburb
coreWords: sub-（在下前缀）+ urb（城市，来自拉丁语urbs）
function: 表示城市边缘的居住区
usageNote: 词源：suburb来自古法语suburbre，源自拉丁语suburbium（sub-近+urbs城市），字面义'城市附近'。14世纪进入英语。urb本身非独立英语词，但urban（城市的）为同根词。该构式为半能产型。
example: Many families live in the suburb because houses there are cheaper and quieter.
variants: suburban (adj.郊区的), suburbanite (n.郊区居民), suburb→city centre/downtown (反义), outskirts (n.郊区), neighbourhood (n.社区)

name: minimum
coreWords: mini-（小前缀，来自拉丁语）+ mum（来自拉丁语minimus=最小）
function: 表示可达到的最小数量、程度或限度
usageNote: 词源：minimum直接来自拉丁语minimum（最小），为minimus的中性形式。mini-表'小'在英语中很能产（minibus, miniskirt等），但minimum本身为拉丁词直接借用。该构式为半能产型。
example: The minimum age for driving a car in this country is eighteen years old.
variants: minimum (adj.最小的), minimize (v.使最小化), minimal (adj.极小的), maximum (n.最大值→minimum反义), least (adj.最少的)

name: multiply
coreWords: multi-（多前缀，来自拉丁语）+ ply（折叠/层，来自拉丁语plicare）
function: 表示数量增加或数学上的乘法运算
usageNote: 词源：multiply来自拉丁语multiplicare（multi-多+plicare折叠），字面义'多层折叠'，引申为'倍增'。12世纪经古法语进入英语。ply本身非独立英语词。multi-表'多'能产性高。
example: If you multiply six by seven, you will get forty-two as the answer.
variants: multiplication (n.乘法), multiple (adj.多重的), multiply→divide (反义), increase (v.增加), add (v.加)

name: multimedia
coreWords: multi-（多前缀）+ media（媒体，medium的复数，来自拉丁语）
function: 表示使用多种媒体形式（文字、图像、音频、视频）的内容或系统
usageNote: 词源：multi-表'多'；media为拉丁语medium（中间）的复数。multimedia为1960年代创造的计算机术语。multi-与名词结合的构式能产性高。
example: This is a multimedia classroom with a computer, a screen, and speakers.
variants: multimedia (n.多媒体), media (n.媒体), multi-purpose (adj.多用途的), single-media (adj.单媒体的→multimedia反义)

name: exchange
coreWords: ex-（出前缀，来自拉丁语）+ change（改变/交换，来自3500词）
function: 表示将一物换为另一物，或双方互换物品
usageNote: 词源：ex-来自拉丁语，表'出、向外'；change来自古法语changier。exchange字面义'换出'。14世纪经古法语进入英语。ex-表'出'是重要前缀。该构式能产性中等。
example: The students had an exchange program with a school in Japan last summer.
variants: exchange (n.交换), exchangeable (adj.可交换的), change (v.改变), swap (v.交换), trade (v.交易)

name: express
coreWords: ex-（出前缀）+ press（压，来自拉丁语pressare）
function: 表示用语言表达思想或情感，或快速传递
usageNote: 词源：express来自拉丁语exprimere（ex-出+premere压），字面义'挤压出来'，引申为'表达'。14世纪进入英语。press为英语本族词。ex-表'出'与动词结合构式能产性中等。
example: She wrote a letter to express her thanks for all the help she had received.
variants: expression (n.表达), expressive (adj.有表现力的), express (adj.快速的), press (v.压), say (v.说), state (v.陈述)

name: export
coreWords: ex-（出前缀）+ port（搬运/携带，来自拉丁语portare）
function: 表示将商品从本国运往国外销售
usageNote: 词源：export来自拉丁语exportare（ex-出+portare搬运）。15世纪进入英语。port在英语中为'港口'，与portare（搬运）同源但语义不同。该构式为半能产型。
example: This factory exports cars to many countries in Europe and Asia.
variants: export (n.出口), exporter (n.出口商), export→import (反义), trade (n.贸易), ship (v.船运)

name: enjoy
coreWords: en-（使动前缀）+ joy（欢乐，来自古法语joie）
function: 表示从某事中获得快乐或满足感
usageNote: 词源：enjoy来自古法语enjoir（en-使+joie欢乐）。joy来自古法语joie，最终来自拉丁语gaudia。14世纪进入英语。en-与名词结合构成使动动词是英语中的能产构式。
example: I enjoy reading story books in my free time after school.
variants: enjoyment (n.享受), enjoyable (adj.令人愉快的), joy (n.欢乐), enjoy→suffer (反义), like (v.喜欢), love (v.热爱)

name: encourage
coreWords: en-（使动前缀）+ courage（勇气，来自古法语corage）
function: 表示给予他人信心或勇气去做某事
usageNote: 词源：encourage来自古法语encoragier（en-使+corage勇气）。courage来自古法语corage（cuir心+-age名词后缀），源自拉丁语cor（心）。字面义'使有心'即'鼓励'。15世纪进入英语。
example: Teachers should encourage students to ask questions in class.
variants: encouragement (n.鼓励), encouraging (adj.鼓舞人心的), courage (n.勇气), encourage→discourage (反义), support (v.支持), inspire (v.激励)

name: decrease
coreWords: de-（向下前缀）+ crease（增长，来自拉丁语crescere）
function: 表示数量、大小或程度的减少
usageNote: 词源：decrease来自古法语decreistre，源自拉丁语decrescere（de-向下+crescere增长）。crease本身非独立英语词（英语crease意为'皱褶'，来源不同）。14世纪进入英语。de-表'向下'能产性中等。
example: The number of students in the class began to decrease after the holiday.
variants: decrease (n.减少), decreasing (adj.减少的), decrease→increase (反义), reduce (v.减少), decline (v.下降), grow (v.增长)

name: describe
coreWords: de-（向下前缀）+ scribe（写，来自拉丁语scribere）
function: 表示用语言描绘或写出某人或某物的特征
usageNote: 词源：describe来自拉丁语describere（de-向下+scribere写），字面义'写下'，引申为'描述'。15世纪进入英语。scribe本身非独立英语词，但scribere是重要拉丁词根。
example: Can you describe your best friend so that I know what he looks like?
variants: description (n.描述), descriptive (adj.描述性的), describe→describe (对), write (v.写), picture (v.描绘), explain (v.解释)

name: destroy
coreWords: de-（去除前缀）+ stroy（建造，来自拉丁语struere）
function: 表示彻底破坏或毁灭某物
usageNote: 词源：destroy来自古法语destruire，源自拉丁语destruere（de-去除+struere建造），字面义'去除建造'即'拆毁'。13世纪进入英语。stroy/struere本身非独立英语词。该构式为半能产型，但destroy语义已高度词汇化。
example: The fire destroyed the old house, but no one was hurt.
variants: destruction (n.破坏), destructive (adj.破坏性的), destroy→create/build (反义), ruin (v.毁坏), damage (v.损坏)

name: forehead
coreWords: fore-（在前前缀，来自古英语）+ head（头，来自3500词）
function: 表示面部位于眼睛上方的部位
usageNote: 词源：fore-来自古英语foran/for-，表'在前'；head来自古英语heafod。forehead字面义'前面的头'。fore-为英语本族前缀，在古英语复合词中很常见。现代英语中fore-主要用于固定词汇。
example: She pushed the hair away from her forehead because it was very hot.
variants: forehead (n.前额), forearm (n.前臂), foreleg (n.前腿), head (n.头), face (n.脸)

name: forecast
coreWords: fore-（在前前缀）+ cast（投掷/推算，来自古挪威语kasta/古英语）
function: 表示根据现有信息对未来事件进行预测
usageNote: 词源：forecast由fore-（在前）+ cast（投掷/推算）构成。cast来自古挪威语kasta（投掷）或古英语同源词。forecast字面义'提前推算'。15世纪进入英语。fore-与动词结合表'提前'能产性中等。
example: The weather forecast says it will rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella.
variants: forecast (n.预报), forecaster (n.预报员), predict (v.预测), foresee (v.预见), forecast→review (反义)

name: midnight
coreWords: mid-（中间前缀，来自古英语）+ night（夜晚，来自3500词）
function: 表示一天中夜晚的中间时刻，即12点钟
usageNote: 词源：mid-来自古英语midd，表'中间'；night来自古英语niht。midnight字面义'夜的中间'。古英语中mid-是非常能产的前缀：midday, midsummer, midwinter等。现代英语中mid-仍保持一定能产性。
example: The clock struck twelve at midnight and the new year began.
variants: midnight (n.午夜), midday (n.正午), midsummer (n.仲夏), night (n.夜晚), noon (n.中午)

name: midsummer
coreWords: mid-（中间前缀）+ summer（夏天，来自3500词）
function: 表示夏季的中间时段，通常指6月下旬
usageNote: 词源：mid-表'中间'；summer来自古英语sumer。midsummer字面义'夏之中'。莎士比亚名剧《仲夏夜之梦》（A Midsummer Night's Dream）使该词广为人知。mid-与名词结合表'中间'能产性中等。
example: In midsummer, the sun stays bright until very late in the evening.
variants: midsummer (n.仲夏), midwinter (n.仲冬), mid-autumn (n.中秋), summer (n.夏天), winter (n.冬天)

name: nonsense
coreWords: non-（非前缀，来自拉丁语）+ sense（意义，来自3500词）
function: 表示无意义、荒谬或愚蠢的话语或行为
usageNote: 词源：non-来自拉丁语non（不）；sense来自拉丁语sensus（感觉）。nonsense字面义'无意义'。17世纪进入英语。non-与名词结合表'非'在英语中非常能产，尤其是学术和正式用语中。
example: The story was full of nonsense, so nobody believed what he said.
variants: nonsense (n.胡说), nonsensical (adj.荒谬的), sense (n.意义), meaningless (adj.无意义的), sense→nonsense (反义)

name: non-smoker
coreWords: non-（非前缀）+ smoker（吸烟者，来自3500词smoke）
usageNote: 词源：non-表'非'；smoker为smoke+er（施事者）。non-smoker为20世纪公共卫生运动推广的词，带连字符。non-与施事名词结合构式能产性高：non-drinker, non-believer, non-member等。
example: This room is for non-smokers only, so you cannot smoke here.
variants: non-smoker (n.不吸烟者), non-smoking (adj.禁烟的), smoker (n.吸烟者), non-drinker (n.不饮酒者), non-member (n.非成员)

name: crossroads
coreWords: cross（交叉，来自3500词）+ roads（道路，road的复数，来自3500词）
function: 表示两条或多条道路交叉的地方
usageNote: 词源：crossroads为英语本族复合词。cross来自古法语crois/拉丁语crux；roads来自古英语rad。crossroads字面义'交叉的道路'。该复合词在英语中历史悠久，既指实际路口也用于隐喻义'关键时刻'。
example: You will see a bank at the crossroads if you turn left at the next corner.
variants: crossroads (n.十字路口=十字路口), intersection (n.交叉路口), turning point (n.转折点), road (n.道路), crossing (n.交叉口)

name: stepfather
coreWords: step（继前缀，来自古英语steop-=丧失）+ father（父亲，来自3500词）
function: 表示母亲再婚后的丈夫，非生父
usageNote: 词源：step-来自古英语steop-，表'丧失的'（即丧失亲生父母而由他人代养）。steopchild原指'孤儿'。stepfather字面义'代父'。该构式在现代英语中受限，仅用于几个亲属词：stepmother, stepbrother, stepsister, stepchild等。
example: My stepfather has lived with us since I was five years old.
variants: stepfather (n.继父), stepmother (n.继母), stepbrother (n.继兄), stepsister (n.继姐), father (n.父亲), foster father (n.养父)

name: selfish
coreWords: self（自己，来自3500词）+ -ish（形容词后缀，来自古英语）
function: 表示只关心自己而不考虑他人的
usageNote: 词源：self来自古英语self；ish来自古英语-ish（…似的）。selfish为17世纪创造，字面义'像自己的'即'只顾自己'。-ish后缀在英语本族词中很能产：childish, foolish, selfish等。
example: A selfish person does not like to share toys or food with other children.
variants: selfishness (n.自私), selfishly (adv.自私地), selfless (adj.无私的→selfish反义), childish (adj.孩子气的), foolish (adj.愚蠢的)

name: action
coreWords: act（行动，来自3500词）+ -ion（名词后缀，来自拉丁语）
function: 表示行动或行为的过程、结果或实例
usageNote: 词源：action来自拉丁语actio（act-行动+-io名词后缀）。英语中动词act去t加-ion。拉丁源动词常通过-ion/-ation构成名词。该构式能产性极高，是英语名词化的核心手段之一。
example: The quick action of the doctor saved the sick boy's life.
variants: action (n.行动), active (adj.活跃的), act (v.行动), activity (n.活动), inaction (n.不行动→action反义)

name: education
function: 表示教育的过程、体系或结果
usageNote: 词源：education来自拉丁语educatio（educare养育+-io后缀）。英语中动词educate去e加-ion。 educate来自拉丁语educare（e-出+ducare引导），字面义'引导出来'。该构式为能产型，大量-ate动词通过此方式名词化。
example: Education is the key to a better future for every child.
variants: educational (adj.教育的), educator (n.教育家), educate (v.教育), learning (n.学习), ignorance (n.无知→education反义)

name: information
function: 表示通过告知而获得的知识、数据或消息
usageNote: 词源：information来自拉丁语informatio（informare赋予形态+-io后缀）。英语中动词inform去m加-ation（因拉丁词干为informat-）。14世纪进入英语。-ation是-ion的扩展形式，用于拉丁源动词。
example: You can find a lot of useful information on the school website.
variants: informative (adj.提供信息的), inform (v.通知), knowledge (n.知识), data (n.数据), misinformation (n.错误信息)

name: invitation
function: 表示邀请他人参加某事的请求或请柬
usageNote: 词源：invitation来自拉丁语invitatio（invitare邀请+-io后缀）。英语中动词invite去e加-ation。 invite来自拉丁语invitare。该构式为能产型，大量动词通过-ation名词化。
example: I received an invitation to my friend's birthday party last week.
variants: invite (v.邀请), inviting (adj.诱人的), invitation card (请柬), guest (n.客人), host (n.主人)

name: pollution
function: 表示环境被有害物质污染的状态或过程
usageNote: 词源：pollution来自拉丁语pollutio（polluere污染+-io后缀）。英语中动词pollute去e加-ion。pollute来自拉丁语polluere（污染、玷污）。该构式为能产型。
example: Air pollution in big cities can cause health problems for old people.
variants: pollutant (n.污染物), polluted (adj.被污染的), pollute (v.污染), clean (adj.干净的→pollution反义), purity (n.纯净)

name: suggestion
function: 表示向他人提出的想法、意见或建议
usageNote: 词源：suggestion来自拉丁语suggestio（suggerere提出+-io后缀）。英语中动词suggest直接加-ion。 suggest来自拉丁语suggerere（sub-从下+gerere携带）。14世纪进入英语。
example: The teacher made a suggestion about how we could improve our project.
variants: suggest (v.建议), suggestive (adj.暗示的), suggestion (n.建议), advice (n.劝告), proposal (n.提议)

name: achievement
function: 表示通过努力而完成或获得的成绩、成果
usageNote: 词源：achievement来自古法语achevement（achever完成+-ment后缀）。英语中动词achieve去e加-ment。 achieve来自古法语achever（a-到+chief头/结束）。-ment后缀来自拉丁语-mentum，是英语名词化的重要手段。
example: Winning the race was a great achievement for the young runner.
variants: achieve (v.达到), achievable (adj.可达成的), achievement (n.成就), success (n.成功), failure (n.失败→achievement反义)

name: agreement
function: 表示双方意见一致的状态或书面协议
usageNote: 词源：agreement由动词agree去e加-ment构成。-ment来自古法语-ment，源自拉丁语-mentum（工具/结果后缀）。agree本身来自古法语agreer（a-到+gré愉快）。该构式能产性高。
example: After a long talk, the two sides finally reached an agreement on the plan.
variants: agree (v.同意), agreeable (adj.令人愉快的), agreement (n.协议), disagree (v.不同意), contract (n.合同)

name: development
function: 表示成长、进步或建设的过程和结果
usageNote: 词源：development由develop加-ment构成。develop来自古法语desveloper（des-解除+voloper包裹），原义'展开'。-ment是英语中动词名词化的核心后缀之一，能产性极高。
example: The development of new roads will help people travel more easily.
variants: develop (v.发展), developing (adj.发展中的), developed (adj.发达的), development (n.发展), growth (n.增长), decline (n.衰退→development反义)

name: government
function: 表示管理国家的机构、体系或行为
usageNote: 源：government来自古法语gouvernement（gouverner治理+-ment后缀）。英语中govern直接加-ment。 govern来自拉丁语gubernare（掌舵、管理）。-ment后缀在此表示'行为/结果/机构'。
example: The government made new rules to keep the roads safe for all people.
variants: govern (v.治理), governor (n.州长), government (n.政府), governable (adj.可统治的), politics (n.政治), citizen (n.公民)

name: movement
function: 表示位置变化、身体活动或社会运动
usageNote: 词源：movement由move去e加-ment构成。move来自古法语movoir，源自拉丁语movere。-ment后缀使动词名词化，表'动作/过程/结果'。该构式能产性极高。
example: The movement of the train made it hard to stand without holding on.
variants: move (v.移动), moving (adj.感人的), movement (n.运动), motion (n.运动), stillness (n.静止→movement反义)

name: punishment
function: 表示因犯错而受到的处罚或痛苦
usageNote: 词源：punishment由punish加-ment构成。punish来自古法语puniss-，源自拉丁语punire（惩罚）。-ment后缀表'结果/手段'。该构式为能产型。
example: The punishment for being late to class was to clean the room after school.
variants: punish (v.惩罚), punishable (adj.可惩罚的), punishment (n.惩罚), penalty (n.处罚), reward (n.奖励→punishment反义)

name: happiness
coreWords: happy（幸福的，来自3500词）+ -iness（名词后缀）
function: 表示幸福、快乐的状态或感觉
usageNote: 词源：happiness由happy去y变i加-ness构成。-ness是英语本族名词后缀，源自古英语-ness/-nis，将形容词名词化。happy来自hap（运气）+-y。该构式能产性极高。
example: Her face was full of happiness when she heard the good news.
variants: happy (adj.幸福的), happily (adv.快乐地), happiness (n.幸福), joy (n.欢乐), sadness (n.悲伤→happiness反义)

name: kindness
coreWords: kind（善良的，来自3500词）+ -ness（名词后缀）
function: 表示善良、友好和乐于助人的品质
usageNote: 词源：kindness由kind加-ness构成。kind源自古英语gecynde（自然的、天生的），后引申为'和善的'。-ness是英语形容词名词化的核心后缀，能产性极高。
example: The kindness of strangers helped him find his way home in the dark.
variants: kind (adj.善良的), kindly (adv.亲切地), kindness (n.善良), goodness (n.善行), cruelty (n.残忍→kindness反义)

name: darkness
coreWords: dark（黑暗的，来自3500词）+ -ness（名词后缀）
function: 表示缺乏光线的状态或黑暗的抽象概念
usageNote: 词源：darkness由dark加-ness构成。dark源自古英语deorc（黑暗的）。-ness后缀使形容词名词化，表'状态/性质'。这是英语中最能产的构式之一。
example: The room was in complete darkness because all the lights were turned off.
variants: dark (adj.黑暗的), darken (v.使变暗), darkness (n.黑暗), night (n.夜晚), light (n.光→darkness反义), brightness (n.明亮)

name: weakness
coreWords: weak（虚弱的，来自3500词）+ -ness（名词后缀）
function: 表示力量不足、身体虚弱或性格上的缺点
usageNote: 词源：weakness由weak加-ness构成。weak源自古英语wac（弱的）。-ness后缀将形容词转化为抽象名词。该构式为高度能产型。
example: His only weakness is that he eats too many sweet foods before bed.
variants: weak (adj.虚弱的), weaken (v.削弱), weakness (n.弱点), fault (n.缺点), strength (n.力量→weakness反义)

name: illness
coreWords: ill（生病的，来自3500词）+ -ness（名词后缀）
function: 表示身体不适或患有疾病的状态
usageNote: 词源：illness由ill加-ness构成。ill源自古诺斯语illr（坏的、生病的）。-ness使形容词名词化。illness为抽象名词，表'疾病状态'。该构式为能产型。
example: She had to stay in bed for a week because of a serious illness.
variants: ill (adj.生病的), ill (adv.不好地), illness (n.疾病), sick (adj.生病的), sickness (n.疾病), health (n.健康→illness反义)

name: carelessness
coreWords: careless（粗心的，来自3500词）+ -ness（名词后缀）
function: 表示缺乏注意或关心的状态，导致出错
usageNote: 词源：carelessness由careless加-ness构成。careless本身是care+-less（无…的）。再加-ness形成双重派生：care→careless→carelessness。这种多层派生在英语中常见。
example: The fire was caused by carelessness with a candle left near paper.
variants: careless (adj.粗心的), carefully (adv.仔细地), carelessness (n.粗心), care (n.小心), carefulness (n.细心→carelessness反义)

name: ability
coreWords: able（能够的，来自3500词）+ -ity（名词后缀）
function: 表示做某事所需的能力、技能或才干
usageNote: 词源：ability由able去e加-ity构成。-ity来自古法语-ite，源自拉丁语-itas，将形容词名词化（常表'性质/状态'）。able来自拉丁语habilis（易处理的）。该构式能产性高。
example: She has the ability to speak three languages very well.
variants: able (adj.能够的), unable (adj.不能的→able反义), ability (n.能力), capability (n.能力), disability (n.残疾)

name: reality
coreWords: real（真实的，来自3500词）+ -ity（名词后缀）
function: 表示事物真实存在的状态，与想象或幻想相对
usageNote: 词源：reality由real加-ity构成。real来自古法语reel，源自拉丁语realis（物的、实际的）。-ity将形容词转化为抽象名词。该构式能产性高。
example: In reality, the story was not as simple as people thought at first.
variants: real (adj.真实的), really (adv.真正地), reality (n.现实), actual (adj.实际的), dream (n.梦想→reality反义), fantasy (n.幻想)

name: possibility
coreWords: possible（可能的，来自3500词）+ -ity（名词后缀）
function: 表示某事可能发生或存在的机会或潜力
usageNote: 词源：possibility由possible去e加-ity构成。possible来自拉丁语possibilis（posse能够+bilis可…的）。拉丁源-ble形容词常通过-ity名词化。该构式为能产型。
example: There is a strong possibility of rain this afternoon, so take a coat.
variants: possible (adj.可能的), possibly (adv.可能地), possibility (n.可能性), probability (n.概率), impossibility (n.不可能→possibility反义)

name: responsible去e + -ity
template: 形容词去e + -ity → 抽象名词
function: 将形容词转化为抽象名词，表示性质、状态或程度
usageNote: 词源：-ity源自拉丁语后缀-itas（经由古法语-ité进入英语）。responsible来自拉丁语respondere（回答、承担责任）。去e规则：形容词以-e结尾时，加-ity需先去掉-e。responsible→responsibility。能产性：极高，-ity是英语中最高产的抽象名词后缀之一。
example: It is your responsibility to finish the work on time.
variants: responsible（形容词）, respond（动词）, response（名词）; 反义词：irresponsibility

name: teach + -er
function: 表示执行该动作的人（施事者）
usageNote: 词源：-er源自古英语后缀-ere，与德语-er同源。teach源自古英语tǣcan（展示、教导）。这是英语中最高产的派生后缀之一，能将几乎所有动词转化为施事者名词。能产性：极高。
example: My teacher helps me learn English every day.
variants: teaching（动名词）, teachable（形容词）; 同类：worker, farmer, writer, driver

name: work + -er
function: 表示执行该动作的人（施事者）
usageNote: 词源：-er源自古英语-ere。work源自古英语weorc（劳动、作品）。动词work加-er构成施事者名词worker，表示工作的人。能产性：极高，几乎所有表示活动的动词都可以加-er构成施事者。
example: The worker builds houses for people in the city.
variants: working（形容词/动名词）, workable（形容词）; 同类：teacher, farmer, driver

name: write去e + -er
template: 动词去e + -er → 施事者名词
function: 表示执行该动作的人（施事者）
usageNote: 词源：write源自古英语wrītan（刻写）。以不发音-e结尾的动词加-er时需去e，这是英语拼写的常规规则。write→writ-er。能产性：极高，但现代英语中部分动词已直接用-er而不去e（如love→lover）。
example: The writer wrote a story about a brave boy.
variants: writing（动名词）, written（过去分词）, rewrite（动词）; 同类：driver, rider

name: act + -or
template: 动词 + -or → 施事者名词（拉丁源）
function: 表示执行该动作的人（施事者），源自拉丁语
usageNote: 词源：-or源自拉丁语后缀-tor/-sor（经由古法语-eur进入英语）。act源自拉丁语agere（做）的过去分词actus。英语中-or后缀多用于拉丁源动词，与-er功能相同但语体更正式。能产性：中等，主要用于拉丁源词汇。
example: The actor played the role of a kind doctor in the film.
variants: action（名词）, active（形容词）, actress（阴性形式）; 同类：doctor, inventor, conductor, visitor

name: conduct + -or
template: 动词 + -or → 施事者名词（拉丁源）
function: 表示执行该动作的人或物（施事者/工具）
usageNote: 词源：conduct源自拉丁语conducere（引导、带领），con-（一起）+ ducere（引导）。-or为拉丁源施事者后缀。conductor可指人（指挥、售票员）也可指物（导体），体现了施事者名词向工具/功能的扩展。
example: The conductor checked everyone's ticket on the train.
variants: conduct（动词/名词）, conductor（导体义）, conductress（阴性，罕用）; 近义：director

name: visit + -or
template: 动词 + -or → 施事者名词（拉丁源）
function: 表示执行该动作的人（施事者）
usageNote: 词源：visit源自拉丁语visitare（经常去、拜访），来自videre（看）的反复形式。-or后缀在此构成施事者。值得注意的是，visit本身在英语中既是动词也是名词（零派转），再加-or构成施事者名词。
example: A visitor came to see our school yesterday.
variants: visit（动词/名词）, visitation（名词）, revisiting（动名词）; 同类：actor, doctor, editor

name: art + -ist
template: 名词 + -ist → 从业者/专家名词
function: 表示从事某领域活动的人（从业者、专家或信徒）
usageNote: 词源：-ist源自法语-iste，来自拉丁语-ista，最终源自希腊语-istes。-ist后缀在英语中多用于表示专业人士、信徒或主义者。art源自拉丁语ars（技艺）。artist原指技艺高超之人，后特指艺术家。能产性：高。
example: The artist painted a beautiful picture of the lake.
variants: art（名词）, artistic（形容词）, artistry（名词）; 同类：scientist, musician, novelist, tourist

name: science去e + -ist
template: 名词去e + -ist → 从业者名词
function: 表示从事某学科研究的人（专家）
usageNote: 词源：science源自拉丁语scientia（知识），来自scire（知道）。science以-ce结尾，其中的e是字母组合的一部分，加-ist时去e：scienc-e→scient-ist。scientist是近代造词（1834年，由William Whewell创造）。
example: The scientist did many tests in the lab.
variants: science（名词）, scientific（形容词）, scientifically（副词）; 同类：artist, musician, biologist

name: tour + -ist
template: 名词/动词 + -ist → 从业者/参与者名词
function: 表示从事旅游活动的人
usageNote: 词源：tour源自法语tour（转圈、巡回），来自拉丁语tornus（车床、圆圈）。-ist后缀在此表示从事该活动的人。tourist与traveler的区别在于：tourist更强调以观光为目的的旅行，而traveler范围更广。
example: Many tourists visit the Great Wall every year.
variants: tour（名词/动词）, tourism（名词）, touristy（形容词，口语）; 近义：traveler, visitor, sightseer

name: free + -dom
template: 形容词 + -dom → 抽象状态名词
function: 表示某种状态、领域或管辖范围
usageNote: 词源：-dom源自古英语-dom（判断、管辖），与拉丁语dominus（主人）无关，而是来自日耳曼语*domaz（判断）。原义为管辖区域或权力范围，后引申为状态。free源自古英语frēo（自由的）。freedom=freedom。
example: Freedom is important for all people in the world.
variants: free（形容词）, freely（副词）; 同类：wisdom, kingdom, boredom, officialdom

name: king + -dom
template: 名词 + -dom → 领域/状态名词
function: 表示某人统治的领域或管辖范围
usageNote: 词源：king源自古英语cyning（国王），来自日耳曼语*kuningaz。-dom原指管辖范围或权力领域。kingdom字面义为'国王的统治区域'。在英语中，-dom构成的词多为抽象名词，如freedom, wisdom, boredom。
example: The king ruled his kingdom with kindness and wisdom.
variants: king（名词）, kingdom（生物学界）, kingly（形容词）; 同类：freedom, wisdom, earldom

name: friend + -ship
template: 名词 + -ship → 抽象关系/状态名词
function: 表示两者之间的关系、状态或品质
usageNote: 词源：-ship源自古英语-scipe（状态、性质），与德语-schaft同源。friend源自古英语frēond（朋友）。-ship将具体关系抽象化，friendship不是指朋友本身，而是指'朋友之间的关系'。能产性：中高。
example: Their friendship began when they were in primary school.
variants: friend（名词）, friendly（形容词）, friendless（形容词）; 同类：leadership, relationship, membership, ownership

name: leader + -ship
template: 名词 + -ship → 抽象能力/状态名词
function: 表示某种身份、能力或集体状态
usageNote: 词源：leader源自古英语lǣdan（引导）+ -er。leadership由leader加-ship构成，表示领导能力或领导地位。-ship在此将具体的人（leader）抽象化为'领导这一属性'。注意： leader（人）→leadership（能力/地位）。
example: Good leadership helps a team work together well.
variants: leader（名词）, lead（动词）, leading（形容词）; 同类：friendship, relationship, ownership, citizenship

name: relation + -ship
template: 名词 + -ship → 抽象关系名词
function: 表示人或事物之间的关联状态
usageNote: 词源：relation源自拉丁语referre（带回）的过去分词relatus，re-（回）+ latum（携带）。relationship=relation + -ship。relation本身已含'关系'义，加-ship后语义更加抽象和固化。relationship与relation的区别在于前者更强调情感联系。
example: They have a close relationship with their neighbors.
variants: relation（名词）, relate（动词）, relative（名词/形容词）; 近义：connection, link, tie

name: child + -hood
template: 名词 + -hood → 时期/状态名词
function: 表示某生命阶段或状态
usageNote: 词源：-hood源自古英语-hād（状态、等级），与德语-heit同源。child源自古英语cild（儿童）。childhood将具体的人（child）抽象化为'童年这一阶段'。能产性：中，常见词包括adulthood, motherhood, fatherhood, neighborhood。
example: I had a happy childhood in the small town.
variants: child（名词）, childish（形容词）, childlike（形容词）; 同类：adulthood, motherhood, fatherhood, neighborhood

name: neighbor + -hood
template: 名词 + -hood → 区域/集体名词
function: 表示某一人群所在的区域或社区
usageNote: 词源：neighbor源自古英语nēahgebur（附近的居住者），nēah（近）+ gebur（居住者）。neighborhood字面义为'邻居们所在的区域'，语义从人转向了地理空间。在美式英语中常简写为neighborhood。
example: There is a nice park in our neighborhood.
variants: neighbor（名词）, neighboring（形容词）, neighborly（形容词）; 同类：brotherhood, adulthood, falsehood

name: short + -age
template: 形容词 + -age → 状态/结果名词
function: 表示某种状态、数量或费用
usageNote: 词源：-age源自古法语-age，来自拉丁语-aticum（状态名词后缀）。short源自古英语sceort（短）。shortage=short + -age，表示'短缺的状态'。-age后缀在英语中还可表示费用（postage）或数量（voltage）。
example: There is a shortage of clean water in that area.
variants: short（形容词）, shorten（动词）, shortly（副词）; 同类：breakage, mileage, postage, storage, voltage

name: teen + -age
template: 名词 + -age → 形容词/状态名词
function: 表示与某年龄段相关的属性
usageNote: 词源：teen在20世纪初从-teen（表示13-19的数字后缀，如thirteen, fourteen）独立出来，指13-19岁的青少年。-age在此将teen这一年龄段概念转化为属性形容词/名词。teenage作为形容词出现于1920年代，由teen + age构成。
example: Teenage students often face much pressure at school.
variants: teen（名词）, teenager（名词）, teenaged（形容词）; 近义：adolescent, youthful

name: beauty去y + -iful
function: 表示'充满……的'、'具有……性质的'
usageNote: 词源：-ful源自古英语-full（充满的）。beauty源自拉丁语bellus（美丽）经由法语beaute进入英语。beauty以-y结尾，变形容词时先去y加-i再加-ful：beauty→beaut-i-ful。注意中间连接元音-i-的作用：避免辅音簇并标示词基转换。
example: The garden is full of beautiful flowers in spring.
variants: beauty（名词）, beautify（动词）, beautifully（副词）; 反义词：ugly, plain; 同类：plentiful, merciful, wonderful

name: care + -ful
function: 表示'充满……的'、'具有……性质的'
usageNote: 词源：care源自古英语caru（忧虑、关心）。-ful源自古英语-full。careful字面义为'充满关心的'，引申为'小心的、仔细的'。careful与careless构成反义对。注意：-ful作后缀时通常只写一个l，但与full（完全）词区别。
example: Be careful when you cross the busy street.
variants: care（名词/动词）, carefully（副词）, careless（反义）; 同类：hopeful, useful, helpful, powerful

name: use + -ful
function: 表示'具有……用途的'、'有益的'
usageNote: 词源：use源自古法语us，来自拉丁语ūsus（使用）。-ful在此将名词use转化为形容词useful，表示'有用的'。useful与useless构成直接反义对，展示了-ful与-less的对立关系。能产性：高，几乎所有表示功能的名词都可加-ful。
example: This book is very useful for my English study.
variants: use（名词/动词）, useless（反义）, usage（名词）, user（名词）; 同类：helpful, harmful, meaningful

name: hope + -ful
function: 表示'充满……的'、'具有……性质的'
usageNote: 词源：hope源自古英语hopa（希望）。-ful将抽象名词hope转化为形容词hopeful，表示'充满希望的'。hopeful既可作定语（a hopeful sign），也可作表语（I am hopeful）。
example: We are hopeful that the weather will be fine tomorrow.
variants: hope（名词/动词）, hopefully（副词）, hopeless（反义）; 同类：careful, cheerful, peaceful, successful

name: success + -ful
function: 表示'具有……结果的'、'达到……的'
usageNote: 词源：success源自拉丁语succedere（跟随、接替）的过去分词successus，suc-（sub-变体，下）+ cedere（走）。successful=success + -ful，表示'达到成功状态的'。注意：success以两个s结尾，加-ful时不需要任何拼写变化。
example: She was successful in passing the important exam.
variants: success（名词）, succeed（动词）, successfully（副词）; 反义：unsuccessful; 同类：powerful, wonderful, beautiful

name: care + -less
function: 表示'缺乏……的'、'没有……的'（否定）
usageNote: 词源：-less源自古英语-lēas（没有），与德语-los同源。careless=care + -less，字面义为'没有关心的'，即'粗心的'。-less与-ful构成系统的对立关系：careful（细心的）vs careless（粗心的）。能产性：极高。
example: A careless driver may cause a serious accident.
variants: care（名词/动词）, carefully（副词）, careful（反义）; 同类：homeless, hopeless, useless, fearless, endless

name: home + -less
usageNote: 词源：home源自古英语hām（家、住所）。-less在此构成否定形容词，表示'没有家的'。homeless与homesick（想家的）形成有趣对比：-less表缺乏，-sick表渴望。
example: The city built new houses for the homeless people.
variants: home（名词）, homesick（形容词）, homeless（名词化指群体）; 同类：careless, hopeless, motherless, fatherless

name: hope + -less
function: 表示'缺乏……的'、'没有……的'（否定）
usageNote: 词源：hope + -less构成hopeless，表示'没有希望的'。hopeful与hopeless构成完美反义对。注意：hopeless在口语中可表示'差劲的、无可救药的'（a hopeless student），语义比字面义更强。
example: Don't be hopeless; there is always a way out.
variants: hope（名词/动词）, hopefully（副词）, hopeful（反义）; 同类：careless, useless, helpless, restless

name: use + -less
function: 表示'没有……的'、'缺乏用途的'（否定）
usageNote: 词源：use + -less构成useless，表示'没有用处的'。useful与useless构成直接反义对。注意useless在口语中也可表示'差劲的'（口语化用法，如useless advice），语义有贬值趋势。
example: This old pen is useless because it has no ink.
variants: use（名词/动词）, useful（反义）, usage（名词）, used（形容词）; 同类：careless, hopeless, worthless, harmless

name: worth + -less
usageNote: 词源：worth源自古英语weorþ（价值）。-less在此构成否定形容词。worthless=worth + -less。worth本身在现代英语中多作介词（worth $10），但其名词属性在复合词中保留。worthy（值得的）是另一个相关派生词。
example: The broken watch is worthless now, but I still keep it.
variants: worth（名词/介词）, worthy（形容词）, worthwhile（形容词）; 反义：valuable, precious; 同类：valueless, meaningless

name: accept + -able
usageNote: 词源：-able源自拉丁语-abilis（可……的），经由古法语-able进入英语。accept源自拉丁语accipere（拿取），ac-（向）+ capere（拿）。acceptable=accept + -able，表示'可被接受的'。-able主要加于及物动词。
example: Your plan is acceptable to most of us.
variants: accept（动词）, acceptance（名词）, accepted（过去分词）; 同类：comfortable, valuable, readable, washable

name: comfort + -able
function: 表示'能够提供……的'、'令人……的'
usageNote: 词源：comfort源自古法语conforter（加强），来自拉丁语confortare（使强壮）。comfortable=comfort + -able，原义为'能给予安慰的'，后转为'身体舒适的'。注意拼写：comfort后直接加-able，不需要变化。
example: This chair is very comfortable to sit on.
variants: comfort（名词/动词）, comfortably（副词）, discomfort（名词）; 同类：enjoyable, reasonable, fashionable

name: value去e + -able
function: 表示'具有……价值的'、'可被……的'
usageNote: 词源：value源自拉丁语valēre（强壮、有价值）。value以不发音-e结尾，加-able时去e：valu-e→valu-able。注意：-able前的连接元音-a-是后缀的组成部分。valuable既可作定语（valuable advice），也可作名词化用（valuables，贵重物品）。
example: This ring is very valuable because it is made of gold.
variants: value（名词/动词）, valueless（反义）, invaluable（极有价值的）, valuation（名词）; 同类：believable, desirable, advisable

name: vis + -ible
usageNote: 词源：-ible源自拉丁语-ibilis，是-able在拉丁词根后的变体。vis-是拉丁语videre（看）的变体词根，同根词包括vision, visit, visual。visible=vis + -ible，表示'可被看见的'。-ible与-able功能相同，但-ible主要接拉丁词根。
example: The star is visible to the naked eye on clear nights.
variants: vision（名词）, visit（动词）, visual（形容词）, invisible（反义）; 同类：audible, feasible, terrible, horrible

name: terr + -ible
usageNote: 词源：terr-是拉丁语terrēre（使恐惧）的词根，来自terreo（我害怕）。terrible原义为'令人恐惧的'，后语义弱化，在现代英语中常表示'糟糕的'或'程度极强的'（a terrible headache）。-ible在此接拉丁词根terr-。
example: The weather was terrible during the whole holiday.
variants: terror（名词）, terrific（原义可怕的，现常表极好的，口语）, terribly（副词）; 同类：horrible, visible, feasible

name: danger + -ous
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'充满……的'
usageNote: 词源：-ous源自古法语-ous/-eus，来自拉丁语-ōsus（充满……的）。danger源自古法语dongier（权力、管辖），来自拉丁语dominium（所有权）。dangerous=danger + -ous，表示'充满危险的'。能产性：高。
example: It is dangerous to swim in the deep river alone.
variants: danger（名词）, dangerously（副词）, endanger（动词）; 同类：famous, courageous, nervous, glorious

name: fame + -ous
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'闻名的'
usageNote: 词源：fame源自拉丁语fama（传言、名声），来自farī（说）。famous=fame + -ous。注意拼写：fame以-e结尾，加-ous时去e（fame→fam-ous）。famous的语义从'被谈论的'发展为'闻名的、著名的'。
example: The writer became famous after his first book came out.
variants: fame（名词）, famously（副词）, infamous（臭名昭著的）; 同类：dangerous, nervous, curious, various

name: curi + -ous
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'充满……的'
usageNote: 词源：curi-来自拉丁语cura（关心、照料），curiosus原义为'仔细的、谨慎的'，后发展为'好奇的'（因为好奇者总是关心地打探）。curious进入英语后保留了'好奇的'和'奇怪的'两个义项。注意cura也是cure（治愈）的词源。
example: The child is curious about everything around him.
variants: curiosity（名词）, curiously（副词）, cure（动词/名词）; 同类：dangerous, nervous, glorious, various

name: nerv + -ous
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'与……相关的'
usageNote: 词源：nerv-来自拉丁语nervus（筋、神经、力量）。nervous原义为'有力的'（现已罕用），后发展为'神经紧张的'。nervous system（神经系统）保留了拉丁源的科学语义。在现代英语中，nervous主要指'紧张的、焦虑的'。
example: I felt nervous before speaking in front of the class.
variants: nerve（名词）, nervously（副词）, nervousness（名词）, unnerving（形容词）; 同类：dangerous, curious, anxious, cautious

name: act + -ive
function: 表示'具有……倾向的'、'倾向于……的'
usageNote: 词源：-ive源自拉丁语-ivus（经由古法语-if进入英语）。act源自拉丁语agere（做）的过去分词actus。active=act + -ive，表示'倾向于行动的'。注意：以-t结尾的动词加-ive时，常需加连接元音-i-（如act→act-ive）。
example: She takes an active part in school sports.
variants: act（动词）, activity（名词）, actively（副词）, activate（动词）; 同类：creative, expensive, attractive, effective

name: expense去se + -ive
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'需要……的'
usageNote: 词源：expense源自拉丁语expendere（支付、花费），ex-（出）+ pendere（称重）。expense以-se结尾，其中se是拉丁派生词的特征字母组合。加-ive时去se：expens-e→expens-ive。注意：expense的形容词是expensive，名词化形式是expensiveness。
example: This restaurant is too expensive for a student.
variants: expense（名词）, spend（动词）, expenditure（名词）; 同类：creative, attractive, effective, massive

name: create去e + -ive
function: 表示'具有……能力的'、'倾向于……的'
usageNote: 词源：create源自拉丁语creāre（创造、产生）。create以不发音-e结尾，加-ive时去e：creat-e→creat-ive。creative表示'有创造力的'。注意：-ive在此不仅改变词性，还增加了'能力/倾向'的语义层次。
example: The creative boy always comes up with new ideas.
variants: create（动词）, creation（名词）, creativity（名词）, creator（名词）; 同类：active, attractive, effective, impressive

name: talk + -ative
usageNote: 词源：-ative是-ive的扩展形式，源自拉丁语-ativus。talkative=talk + -ative，表示'爱说话的'。-ative通常加于拉丁源动词（如informative），但talk是本土英语词，加-ative属于能产性扩展。能产性：中，主要用于表示倾向性。
example: The talkative girl speaks to everyone in class.
variants: talk（动词/名词）, talkatively（副词）, talkativeness（名词）; 同类：informative, argumentative, imaginative

name: nation + -al
usageNote: 词源：-al源自拉丁语-alis（经由古法语-el进入英语）。nation源自拉丁语nātiō（出生、民族），来自nāscī（出生）。national=nation + -al，表示'与民族/国家相关的'。注意：-al是英语中最高产的形容词后缀之一。
example: The national flag has five stars on it.
variants: nation（名词）, nationally（副词）, nationality（名词）, international（形容词）; 同类：natural, cultural, educational, traditional

name: nature去e + -al
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'与……相关的'
usageNote: 词源：nature源自拉丁语nātūra（出生、本性），来自nāscī（出生）。nature以不发音-e结尾，加-al时去e：natur-e→natur-al。natural表示'自然的、天生的'。注意：nature中的e在音系上是沉默的，属于拼写历史遗留。
example: It is natural for children to love playing games.
variants: nature（名词）, naturally（副词）, unnatural（反义）; 同类：national, cultural, educational, personal

name: culture去e + -al
function: 表示'与……相关的'、'具有……性质的'
usageNote: 词源：culture源自拉丁语cultūra（耕作、培养），来自colere（耕作）。culture以-e结尾，加-al时去e：cultur-e→cultur-al。cultural表示'文化的'。注意：culture的动词形式是cultivate，与cultural的派生路径不同。
example: We should respect the cultural traditions of other nations.
variants: culture（名词）, culturally（副词）, agriculture（名词）, cultivate（动词）; 同类：national, natural, educational, traditional

name: education + -al
function: 表示'与……相关的'、'具有……性质的'
usageNote: 词源：education源自拉丁语ēducātiō（培养、教育），来自ēdūcere（引出），ē-（出）+ dūcere（引导）。educational=education + -al。注意：education以-ion结尾，加-al时不需要拼写变化，直接连接。
example: The museum has great educational value for students.
variants: education（名词）, educate（动词）, educator（名词）, educational（副词：educationally）; 同类：national, cultural, traditional, functional

name: differ + -ent
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'处于……状态的'
usageNote: 词源：-ent源自拉丁语-entem（现在分词后缀）。differ源自拉丁语differre（不同），dis-（分开）+ ferre（携带）。different=differ + -ent，原义为'被区分开的'。注意：-ent与-ant功能相同，-ent多用于拉丁-e-词干动词。
example: These two books are different in style but both good.
variants: differ（动词）, difference（名词）, differently（副词）; 同类：excellent, confident, patient, urgent

name: excel + -lent
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'处于……状态的'
usageNote: 词源：excel源自拉丁语excellere（超越、突出），ex-（出）+ cellere（升起）。excellent不是简单的excel加-ent，而是直接借自拉丁语excellens（excellere的现在分词）。这是一个拉丁直接借用词，不是英语本土派生。
example: She did an excellent job on the school project.
variants: excel（动词）, excellence（名词）, excellently（副词）; 同类：different, confident, patient, urgent

name: pleas + -ant
function: 表示'具有……性质的'、'处于……状态的'
usageNote: 词源：pleasant不是please直接加-ant，而是借自古法语plaisant（plaisir的现在分词），来自拉丁语placēre（使高兴）。pleasant的拼写经过了英语化改造：plaisant→pleasant。注意pleasant作定语和表语皆可。
example: We had a pleasant walk in the park on Sunday.
variants: please（动词）, pleasure（名词）, pleasantly（副词）, unpleasant（反义）; 同类：important, constant, distant, patient

name: quick + -ly
function: 将形容词转化为副词，表示方式或程度
usageNote: 词源：-ly源自古英语-līce（方式副词后缀），与德语-lich同源。quick源自古英语cwic（活的、活跃的）。quickly=quick + -ly，表示'以快速的方式'。-ly是英语中最高产的副词后缀。
example: He ran quickly to catch the early bus.
variants: quick（形容词）, quickness（名词）, quicken（动词）; 同类：slowly, happily, easily, carefully

name: happy去y + -ily
function: 将形容词转化为副词（以-y结尾的形容词专用）
usageNote: 词源：happy源自古英语hæpic（幸运的），来自hap（运气）。以辅音+y结尾的形容词变副词时，需先将y改为i再加-ly：happy→happ-i-ly。这是英语拼写的常规规则。注意：-ily中的i既来自y的变体，也起连接作用。
example: The children played happily in the garden all afternoon.
variants: happy（形容词）, happiness（名词）, unhappy（反义）; 同类：easily, luckily, heavily, busily

name: easy去y + -ily
function: 将形容词转化为副词（以-y结尾的形容词专用）
usageNote: 词源：easy源自古法语aisie（舒适的），来自aisier（使舒适）。以-y结尾的形容词变副词的规则：去y加-ily。easy→eas-i-ly。注意：easy的比较级和最高级也遵循类似规则：easier, easiest。
example: You can easily solve this problem if you think carefully.
variants: easy（形容词）, ease（名词/动词）, uneasy（反义）; 同类：happily, luckily, heavily, readily

name: careful + -ly
function: 将形容词转化为副词，表示方式
usageNote: 词源：careful源自古英语caru + -ful。careful以-ful结尾，加-ly时直接连接：careful + ly→carefully。注意：-ful结尾的词变副词不需要任何拼写变化，因为-ful以l结尾，而-ly也以l开头，但双写l并非必要（英式英语中通常单写）。
example: Please listen carefully to the teacher in class.
variants: careful（形容词）, careless（反义）, care（名词/动词）; 同类：slowly, suddenly, finally, really

name: hard + -ly
function: 表示程度（几乎不）或方式（努力地）
usageNote: 词源：hard源自古英语heard（坚硬的、困难的）。hardly原义为'努力地'（hard + -ly，如work hardly），但语义经历了重大转移，现代义为'几乎不'。这一语义变化发生在16-17世纪，属于典型的语义漂移。注意hardly的否定语义常引起学习者困惑。
example: I can hardly hear what you are saying.
variants: hard（形容词/副词）, harden（动词）, hardness（名词）; 近义：scarcely, barely; 反义：easily

name: gold + -en
usageNote: 词源：-en源自古英语-en（由……制成），但golden中的-en功能已扩展为'像……的/具有……性质的'。gold源自古英语gold。golden不仅指'金制的'，更常用于比喻义（golden age, golden rule）。注意gold本身也可作形容词（a gold watch），但golden更强调品质。
example: The golden sun sank below the sea at sunset.
variants: gold（名词/形容词）, golden（比喻用法）, gild（动词）; 同类：wooden, woolen, earthen

name: wood + -en
usageNote: 词源：wood源自古英语wudu（木头）。wooden=wood + -en，表示'木制的'。-en后缀在现代英语中已不具能产性，仅保留在少数固定词中（golden, wooden, woolen等）。wooden还发展出比喻义'僵硬的、不灵活的'（a wooden smile）。
example: The old house has a strong wooden door.
variants: wood（名词）, woody（形容词）, woodland（名词）; 同类：golden, woolen, earthen, leaden

name: strength + -en
usageNote: 词源：strength源自古英语strengþu（力量），来自strang（强壮）。strengthen=strength + -en，表示'使强壮'。注意：strength中的th是古英语名词后缀-th（与-ness同源），不是词根的一部分。strengthen不是'去h加-en'，而是名词strength直接加动词后缀-en。
example: Exercise can strengthen your body and mind.
variants: strength（名词）, strong（形容词）, strongly（副词）; 反义：weaken; 同类：shorten, deepen, widen, broaden

name: real + -ize
function: 表示'使成为……'、'以……方式处理'
usageNote: 词源：-ize源自希腊语-izein，经由拉丁语-izare进入英语。real源自古法语reel，来自拉丁语realis（实际的）。realize=real + -ize，表示'使成为现实/认识到现实'。realize有两义：(1)实现（梦想）；(2)意识到。能产性：极高。
example: I hope to realize my dream of becoming a doctor.
variants: real（形容词）, reality（名词）, really（副词）, realization（名词）; 同类：organize, apologize, modernize, recognize

name: organ + -ize
function: 表示'使成为……'、'以……方式安排'
usageNote: 词源：organ源自拉丁语organum（工具、器官），来自希腊语organon。organize源自中世纪拉丁语organizare（安排），organum + -izare。organize表示'使有条理/组织'。注意英式拼写为organise，但-ize是更古老的拼法。
example: We need to organize a class meeting next week.
variants: organ（名词）, organization（名词）, organizer（名词）, organizational（形容词）; 同类：realize, apologize, recognize, modernize

name: apology + -ize
function: 表示'做出……行为'、'以……方式表达'
usageNote: 词源：apology源自希腊语apologia（辩护），apo-（远离）+ logia（说话）。 apologize=apology + -ize，出现于1596年，表示'做出道歉的行为'。注意：apology原义为'辩护'，16世纪才发展出'道歉'义。英式拼写为apologise。
example: You should apologize to your friend for being late.
variants: apology（名词）, apologetic（形容词）, apologetically（副词）; 同类：realize, organize, sympathize, criticize

name: simple去e + -ify
usageNote: 词源：-ify源自拉丁语-ficare（经由古法语-fier进入英语），来自facere（做）。simple源自拉丁语simplex（单一的），sim-（一）+ plex（折叠）。simplify=simple + -ify，表示'使简单'。simple以-e结尾，加-ify时去e。
example: Can you simplify this long sentence for me?
variants: simple（形容词）, simply（副词）, simplicity（名词）, simplification（名词）; 同类：beautify, clarify, classify, purify

name: beauty去y + -ify
usageNote: 词源：beauty源自拉丁语bellus经由法语。beautify=beauty + -ify，表示'使美丽'。以-y结尾的名词加-ify时去y：beauty→beaut-i-fy。注意：-ify要求前面的音节为单音节或重读音节，这是-ify与-ize的区别之一。
example: They plan to beautify the school garden this spring.
variants: beauty（名词）, beautiful（形容词）, beautification（名词）; 同类：simplify, clarify, classify, terrify

name: for(e) + -ward
template: 前缀/词根 + -ward → 方向副词/形容词
usageNote: 词源：-ward源自古英语-weard（朝向），来自日耳曼语*wert（转）。forward=fore（前）+ -ward。注意拼写：fore-缩略为for-，这是古英语中常见的元音弱化现象。forward既可作副词（move forward），也可作形容词（a forward movement），还可作动词（forward a letter）。
example: Please step forward when your name is called.
variants: forward（动词：转寄）, forwardness（名词）; 同类：backward, upward, downward, inward, outward, eastward

name: after + -ward(s)
template: 介词/副词 + -ward(s) → 时间副词
function: 表示'在……之后'的时间方向
usageNote: 词源：after源自古英语æfter（在……之后）。-ward表示方向，-s是古英语副词后缀的残留。afterward(s)=after + -ward + -(s)。注意：美式英语常用afterward（无s），英式英语常用afterwards（有s）。两者语义完全相同。
example: We went for a walk afterward(s) and talked about the film.
variants: after（介词/副词）, afterward（美式）, afterwards（英式）; 同类：backward(s), toward(s), homeward(s), onward(s)

name: other + -wise
usageNote: 词源：-wise源自古英语-wīse（方式、方法），与德语-weise同源。other源自古英语ōþer（第二、另一个）。otherwise=other + -wise，原义为'以其他方式'，后发展为连词用法（否则）。otherwise的语义演变展示了方式副词向条件连词的语法化过程。
example: You must study hard; otherwise, you may fail the exam.
variants: other（形容词/代词）, likewise（副词）, clockwise（副词）, likewise（副词）; 近义：or else, if not

name: water
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：water源自古英语wæter，与德语Wasser同源。零派转（conversion/zero-derivation）是英语中最古老的构词方式之一。名词water（水）在古英语时期就已有动词用法（浇水），两义共享同一形式。语义关联为'用N来实施与N相关的动作'。
example: Please water the flowers when the soil is dry.
variants: watery（形容词）, watered（过去分词）, watering（动名词）; 同类：hand, face, picture, smoke

name: hand
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：hand源自古英语hond，与德语Hand同源。名词hand（手）通过零派转动词化，表示'用手传递/交给'（hand me the book）。这一语义引申路径为'身体部位→用该部位执行的动作'，是零派转的典型模式。
example: Please hand me that red pen on the desk.
variants: handful（名词）, handed（形容词：left-handed）, handy（形容词）; 同类：water, face, picture

name: face
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：face源自古法语face，来自拉丁语facies（外形、面容）。名词face（脸）通过零派转产生动词义'面对'（面对困难）。语义引申路径为'身体部位→朝向该部位的动作'。注意：face还可与副词小品词搭配（face up to）。
example: We must face the problem and find a solution soon.
variants: facial（形容词）, faced（复合形容词：red-faced）, facing（动名词/名词）; 同类：hand, water, picture

name: picture
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：picture源自拉丁语pictūra（绘画），来自pingere（画）的过去分词pictus。名词picture（图画）通过零派转产生动词义'描绘/想象'（picture yourself in college）。语义从'图像'扩展到'在心中形成图像'。
example: I can picture the happy life we will have together.
variants: pictural（罕用）, pictured（过去分词）, picturing（动名词）; 同类：water, hand, face

name: walk
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：walk源自古英语wealcan（卷、弯曲）和walken（醒来、走动），词源较复杂。walk作动词（走）和名词（散步）完全同形。零派转在此体现为'动作↔该动作的实例'的相互转换，这是名动零派转的最常见类型。
example: I take a walk in the park after dinner every day.
variants: walker（施事者名词）, walking（动名词/形容词）, walkable（形容词）; 同类：talk, run, swim, dance

name: talk
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：talk源自古英语talian（数数、讲述），与tell同源。talk作动词（谈话）和名词（谈话/演讲）完全同形。注意：talk的语义比speak更口语化、随意。零派转使talk可以灵活地在句中承担不同句法功能。
example: We had a long talk about our plans for the future.
variants: talkative（形容词）, talker（名词）, talking（动名词）; 同类：walk, look, run, swim

name: look
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：look源自古英语lōcian（看），来自日耳曼语*lōkōjaną。look作动词（看）和名词（样子/一瞥）完全同形。名词义'样子'（a sad look）是从'看的动作'引申到'被看的样子'，体现了转喻认知机制。
example: She has a kind look in her eyes when she smiles.
variants: looking（动名词）, looked（过去式）, lookout（复合名词）, looking-glass（复合名词）; 同类：walk, talk, smell, taste

name: smoke
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：smoke源自古英语smoca，与德语Schmauch同源。名词smoke（烟）通过零派转产生动词义'吸烟/冒烟'。语义引申路径为'物质→产生该物质的动作'。注意：smoke的派生词smoky（多烟的）需去e加-y。
example: It is not allowed to smoke in the school building.
variants: smoky（形容词）, smoking（动名词）, smoked（过去分词/形容词：smoked fish）, nonsmoker（复合名词）; 同类：water, dust, steam

name: study
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，无需形态变化
usageNote: 词源：study源自拉丁语studium（热情、勤奋），来自studēre（热心于）。study作名词有两个主要义项：(1)学习；(2)书房。动词义为'学习/研究'。注意：名词'书房'是语义引申结果，与动词'学习'的关联为'学习的场所'。
example: I need a quiet room for my study every evening.
variants: student（施事者名词）, studied（过去分词/形容词）, studious（形容词）, studiously（副词）; 同类：use, practice, experience

name: use
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，但发音不同
usageNote: 词源：use源自古法语us，来自拉丁语ūsus（使用）。use作名词时读/juːs/，作动词时读/juːz/，这是英语中名动异读的典型例子。语义关联为'用途（名词）↔使用（动词）'。注意：used to（过去习惯）的读音为/juːst/。
example: What's the use of this old tool? Can we still use it?
variants: useful（形容词）, useless（反义）, user（施事者名词）, usage（名词）, used（过去分词）, using（动名词）; 同类：record, present, refuse

name: record
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式既可作名词也可作动词，但重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：record源自拉丁语recordārī（回忆），re-（再）+ cor（心）。名词record读/ˈrekɔːd/（重音在第一音节），动词record读/rɪˈkɔːd/（重音在第二音节）。这是英语名动异读的典型模式：名轻动重。语义关联为'记录的结果↔做出记录的动作'。
example: The singer plans to record a new song and break the old record.
variants: recorder（施事者名词）, recording（动名词）, recorded（过去分词）; 同类：present, export, import, increase

name: present
template: 名词 ↔ 动词 ↔ 形容词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词、动词和形容词，发音不同
usageNote: 词源：present源自拉丁语praessēns（在场的），来自praeesse（在前面），prae-（前）+ esse（是）。三性异读：形容词/名词present读/ˈpreznt/（在场的/礼物），动词present读/prɪˈzent/（呈现/赠送）。这是英语中最复杂的三性零派转词之一。
example: He was not present at the meeting, so I will present his ideas for him.
variants: presence（名词）, presentation（名词）, presently（副词）; 同类：perfect（形动异读）

name: perfect
template: 形容词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作形容词和动词，发音不同
usageNote: 词源：perfect源自拉丁语perfectus（完成的），per-（完全）+ facere（做）。形容词perfect读/ˈpɜːfɪkt/（完美的），动词perfect读/pəˈfekt/（使完美）。这是典型的形动异读（名轻动重模式的变体）。注意perfect作为动词在现代英语中较正式。
example: Practice is the best way to perfect your English skills.
variants: perfection（名词）, perfectly（副词）, imperfect（反义）, imperfectly（副词）; 同类：present, record, conflict

name: refuse
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读异义）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，但发音不同且语义关联弱
usageNote: 词源：动词refuse读/rɪˈfjuːz/，源自古法语refuser，来自拉丁语refundere（倒回）；名词refuse读/ˈrefjuːs/，源自古法语refusé（被拒绝的东西）。两词虽然拼写相同，但词源路径不同，属于偶然的拼合（convergence）。这是异读异义的最极端例子。
example: The workers refuse to touch the refuse in the street.
variants: refusal（名词）, refused（过去分词）, refusing（动名词）; 同类：lead（铅/领导）, wind（风/缠绕）, tear（眼泪/撕裂）

name: progress
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：progress源自拉丁语prōgressus（前进），pro-（向前）+ gradi（走）。名词progress读/ˈprəʊɡres/（进步），动词progress读/prəˈɡres/（前进）。名轻动重模式。注意：progress的语义始终围绕'向前移动'的核心意象。
example: We have made great progress. We hope to progress even further next year.
variants: progressive（形容词）, progression（名词）, progressively（副词）; 同类：record, present, export, import

name: increase
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：increase源自拉丁语incrēscere（生长），in-（在内）+ crēscere（生长）。名词increase读/ˈɪnkriːs/（增加），动词increase读/ɪnˈkriːs/（使增加）。名轻动重模式。注意：increase与decrease的派生模式完全相同。
example: There has been a big increase in prices. I hope they do not increase again.
variants: increasing（形容词/动名词）, increasingly（副词）, increased（过去分词/形容词）; 同类：decrease, progress, record, present

name: decrease
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：decrease源自拉丁语dēcrēscere（减少），dē-（向下）+ crēscere（生长）。名词decrease读/ˈdiːkriːs/（减少），动词decrease读/dɪˈkriːs/（使减少）。名轻动重模式。decrease与increase构成完美的反义对，两者共享同一拉丁词根crēscere。
example: We noticed a sharp decrease in sales during the winter months.
variants: decreasing（形容词/动名词）, decreased（过去分词/形容词）, decreasingly（副词）; 同类：increase, progress, record, present

name: export
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：export源自拉丁语exportāre（运出），ex-（出）+ portāre（携带）。名词export读/ˈekspɔːt/（出口物），动词export读/ɪkˈspɔːt/（出口）。名轻动重模式。注意：port是'携带'的词根，同根词包括import, transport, report, portable。
example: The country plans to export more rice this year to increase export income.
variants: exporter（施事者名词）, exportation（名词）, exported（过去分词）; 同类：import, transport, report, progress

name: import
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：import源自拉丁语importāre（运入），im-（向内）+ portāre（携带）。名词import读/ˈɪmpɔːt/（进口物），动词import读/ɪmˈpɔːt/（进口）。名轻动重模式。import与export共享词根portāre，前缀im-/ex-表示相反方向。
example: The company decided to import oil because local import was not enough.
variants: importer（施事者名词）, importation（名词）, imported（过去分词/形容词）, important（形容词，语义转移）; 同类：export, transport, report, progress

name: conduct
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：conduct源自拉丁语conducere（引导），con-（一起）+ ducere（引导）。名词conduct读/ˈkɒndʌkt/（行为），动词conduct读/kənˈdʌkt/（引导/指挥）。名轻动重模式。注意：conductor（MC-273）是同根施事者名词。
example: His good conduct at school made his parents proud. He knows how to conduct himself well.
variants: conductor（施事者名词）, conduction（名词）, conductive（形容词）; 同类：record, present, export, import

name: conflict
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：conflict源自拉丁语confligere（碰撞），con-（一起）+ fligere（打击）。名词conflict读/ˈkɒnflɪkt/（冲突），动词conflict读/kənˈflɪkt/（冲突）。名轻动重模式。注意：conflict作动词时较正式，口语中更常用clash。
example: There is a conflict between his work and his family life.
variants: conflicting（形容词）, conflicted（过去分词/形容词）; 同类：conduct, record, present, perfect

name: close
template: 形容词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作形容词和动词，发音不同
usageNote: 词源：close（形容词/动词）源自拉丁语clausus（关闭的），来自claudere（关闭）。形容词close读/kləʊs/（近的/亲密的），动词close读/kləʊz/（关闭）。注意：close的名词形式closure是独立借词，而非close加后缀。形容词close从'关闭的'引申为'近的'。
example: Please close the door. The shop is close to our school.
variants: closely（副词）, closing（动名词/形容词）, closed（过去分词/形容词）, closure（名词）; 同类：separate, present, perfect

name: separate
template: 形容词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作形容词和动词，重音位置不同
usageNote: 词源：separate源自拉丁语sēparātus（分开的），sē-（分开）+ parāre（准备）。形容词separate读/ˈseprət/（分开的），动词separate读/ˈsepəreɪt/（使分开）。注意：形动异读在此不仅体现为重音位置，还涉及元音弱化（形容词尾音为/ət/，动词尾音为/eɪt/）。
example: Keep these two boxes separate. We need to separate the red pens from the blue ones.
variants: separation（名词）, separated（过去分词/形容词）, separately（副词）, separateness（名词）; 同类：close, present, perfect

name: lead
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读异义）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，发音不同且语义完全不同
usageNote: 词源：动词lead读/liːd/，源自古英语lǣdan（引导），与德语leiten同源；名词lead（铅）读/led/，源自古英语lēad（铅）。两词词源完全无关，属于历史拼写偶然重合。这是英语中最著名的同形异源词（homograph with different etymologies）之一。
example: Lead pipes can be dangerous. Good leaders know how to lead their teams well.
variants: leader（施事者名词）, leadership（名词）, leading（形容词/动名词）; 同类：refuse, wind, tear, minute

name: wind
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，发音不同
usageNote: 词源：名词wind（风）读/wɪnd/，源自古英语wind；动词wind（缠绕）读/waɪnd/，源自古英语windan（转动、缠绕）。两词古英语形式不同（wind vs windan），但在现代英语中拼写合并。注意：wound是wind（缠绕）的过去式，不要与wound（伤口）混淆。
example: The wind blew hard, so we had to wind the rope around the post.
variants: windy（形容词）, winding（形容词：蜿蜒的）, window（相关词）; 同类：lead, tear, refuse, minute

name: tear
template: 名词 ↔ 动词（零派转/异读异义）
function: 同一形式可作名词和动词，发音不同且语义不同
usageNote: 词源：名词tear（眼泪）读/tɪə/，源自古英语tēar，与德语Zähre同源；动词tear（撕裂）读/teə/，源自古英语teran（撕），与德语zerren同源。两词古英语拼写和发音都不同，在现代英语中拼写合并但发音保持区别。
example: A tear ran down her face as she watched him tear the old letter.
variants: tearful（形容词）, tearing（动名词）, torn（过去分词）; 同类：lead（铅/领导）, wind（风/缠绕）, refuse（垃圾/拒绝）, minute（分钟/微小的）

name: minute
template: 名词 ↔ 形容词（零派转/异读）
function: 同一形式可作名词和形容词，发音不同
usageNote: 词源：名词minute（分钟）读/ˈmɪnɪt/，源自拉丁语minūta（小部分），来自minūtus（小的）；形容词minute（微小的）读/maɪˈnjuːt/，同源但保留了拉丁语的小词发音。两义共享'小'的核心语义：分钟是'一小部分时间'，微小的也是'极小的'。注意：这是名形异读而非名动异读。
example: Wait a minute! I can see some minute differences between the two photos.
variants: minutely（副词）, minuteness（名词）; 同类：lead（铅/领导）, wind（风/缠绕）, tear（眼泪/撕裂）, refuse（垃圾/拒绝）

name: can表能力
usageNote: 表示主语具备某种能力或技能
example: She can speak English very well.

name: can/could表许可
usageNote: Could比Can更委婉客气，用于正式场合
example: You can use my pen. / Could I borrow your book?

name: can/could表请求
usageNote: Could you...为高中最常用礼貌请求句型
example: Could you please open the window?

name: can/could表可能性
usageNote: could表示的可能性比can更弱、更不确定
example: It can get very cold here at night. / It could rain tomorrow.

name: can't表否定推测
usageNote: 表示对某事的否定推断，语气较肯定
example: That can't be true. / He can't be at home now.

name: can用于感叹
usageNote: can用于否定疑问句表惊讶
example: How can you be so careless?

name: cannot help but
usageNote: 双重否定表肯定，意为忍不住做某事
example: I cannot help but admire her courage.

name: may表许可
usageNote: may表许可较can更正式，常用于书面规则
example: You may leave now. / Students may use the library.

name: may well
usageNote: well加强may的语气，表可能性很大
example: He may well win the competition.

name: may as well
usageNote: 建议做某事，因无更好选择
example: We may as well walk home; it's not far.

name: must表必须
usageNote: 表示说话人认为必要或有义务做的事
example: You must finish your homework first.

name: must表推测
usageNote: 表示有把握的肯定推断，只用于肯定句
example: He must be tired after such a long walk.

name: mustn't表禁止
example: You mustn't park here. / You mustn't be late.

name: must用于修辞问
usageNote: 表示对某人行为的不满或厌烦
example: Must you make so much noise?

name: shall表建议/规定
usageNote: shall主要用于第一人称疑问句中
example: Shall we start the meeting?

name: shall表规定/命令
usageNote: 用于法律条文、规则、条约中
example: Candidates shall remain silent during the exam.

name: should表建议
example: You should eat more vegetables.

name: should表预期
usageNote: 表示某事按道理应该发生
example: The train should arrive at 9 o'clock.

name: shouldn't have done
usageNote: 表示过去不该做而实际上做了的事
example: You shouldn't have said that to her.

name: will表将来
usageNote: 表示将来发生的动作或状态
example: I will call you tomorrow.

name: will表意愿/承诺
usageNote: 表示主语自愿或承诺做某事
example: I will help you with your work.

name: will表习惯/特性
usageNote: 表示人或事物的固有特性或习惯
example: Fish will die without water.

name: will you表请求
usageNote: will you用于请求他人做某事
example: Will you please pass me the salt?

name: would表过去将来
usageNote: 从过去看将要发生的事
example: He said he would come the next day.

name: would表意愿/坚持
usageNote: 表示过去的意愿或习惯
example: She wouldn't listen to my advice.

name: would表过去习惯
usageNote: 描述过去反复发生的动作，带有感情色彩
example: When I was young, I would play in the park every day.

name: Would you mind
usageNote: 后接动名词，为非常客气的请求
example: Would you mind closing the door?

name: would rather
usageNote: 后接动词原形，表示偏好
example: I would rather stay at home tonight.

name: would rather sb did
usageNote: 表示与现在/将来事实相反的愿望
example: I'd rather you didn't smoke here.

name: would like
example: I would like a cup of coffee.

name: need作情态动词
usageNote: need作情态动词主要用于否定句和疑问句
example: Need I say more? / You needn't worry.

name: needn't have done
usageNote: 表示过去没有必要做的事却做了
example: You needn't have bought milk; we have plenty.

name: need doing
example: The flowers need watering. = need to be watered

name: dare作情态动词
usageNote: dare作情态动词用于否定句、疑问句
example: Dare he jump from the wall? / I dare not try.

name: How dare you
usageNote: 表示对某人行为的愤怒或震惊
example: How dare you speak to me like that!

name: dare say
usageNote: 固定表达，表示推测或看法
example: I dare say you are right.

name: ought to
example: You ought to respect your parents.

name: used to
usageNote: 表示过去常常做而现在不再做的事
example: I used to get up early when I was young.

name: used to疑问/否定
usageNote: 借助did构成疑问和否定，use用原形
example: Did you use to play basketball? / I didn't use to like tea.

name: be used to
usageNote: be used to后接名词或动名词，表已适应的状态
example: I am used to getting up early now.

name: be used to do
usageNote: use的被动语态，to为不定式符号
example: Wood is used to make paper.

name: have to
usageNote: 强调客观外部条件导致的必须
example: I have to go now; it's getting late.

name: don't have to
usageNote: 表示没有必要做某事，与mustn't不同
example: You don't have to wait for me.

name: 一般现在时-习惯动作
usageNote: 常与always, usually, often, sometimes, never等频率副词连用
example: She usually walks to school.

name: 一般现在时-客观真理
usageNote: 表示永恒不变的自然规律和普遍真理
example: The sun rises in the east.

name: 一般现在时-时刻表
usageNote: 用于列车、航班、课程等固定时刻表
example: The train leaves at 8:00 tomorrow.
variants: 现在进行时表将来安排

name: 一般现在时-引述/评论
usageNote: 用于戏剧评论、图片说明等
example: Here comes the bus! / There goes the bell!

name: 一般现在时-零条件句
usageNote: 表示条件和结果总是同时成立
example: If you heat ice, it melts.

name: 一般过去时-已完成动作
usageNote: 表示在过去某个时间发生的动作或状态
example: I visited Beijing last summer.

name: 一般过去时-过去习惯
usageNote: would强调重复动作，used to还可指状态
example: She would often read late into the night.

name: 一般过去时-委婉请求
usageNote: 用过去时使请求更礼貌、更不直接
example: I wondered if you could help me.

name: will表将来
usageNote: 表示将来发生的动作或状态
example: I will graduate next year.

name: be going to
usageNote: 表示事先打算或有迹象表明即将发生
example: Look at those clouds; it is going to rain.

name: be about to
usageNote: 表示即将发生的动作，时间非常近
example: The movie is about to start.

name: be to
usageNote: 表示正式的安排、命令或计划
example: We are to meet at the station at 10.

name: will条件句
template: If + S + V, S + will + V
usageNote: 表示真实可能发生的情况
example: If you study hard, you will pass the exam.

name: 现在进行时-正在进行
usageNote: 表示说话时正在发生或现阶段正在进行的动作
example: She is reading a book now.

name: 现在进行时-现阶段
usageNote: 不一定说话时刻正在进行，指现阶段
example: I am learning French this term.

name: 现在进行时-表将来
template: Subj + am/is/are + V-ing + 将来时间
usageNote: 表示已计划或安排好的近期将来
example: We are meeting them tomorrow.

name: 现在进行时-表渐变
example: It is getting dark. / He is growing older.

name: 现在进行时-表重复/批评
usageNote: 与always连用表反复出现的行为，常带感情色彩
example: He is always leaving the door open.

name: 现在进行时-状态动词限制
template: Subj + be + Adj/状态V（不可用进行）
usageNote: know, believe, love, have等表状态的动词一般不用进行时
example: I know him well.（不说I am knowing）

name: 过去进行时-被打断的动作
template: Subj + was/were + V-ing when S + V-ed
usageNote: 描述一个过去正在进行的动作被另一动作打断
example: I was doing homework when the phone rang.

name: 过去进行时-背景描写
usageNote: 用于叙述中交代过去事件的背景
example: The wind was blowing and the birds were singing.

name: 过去进行时-过去并行
template: While + S + was/were + V-ing, S + was/were + V-ing
function: 两个过去同时进行的动作
usageNote: while连接两个并行的过去进行动作
example: While I was cooking, he was watching TV.

name: 过去进行时-礼貌请求
usageNote: 用过去进行时使请求更间接、礼貌
example: I was wondering if you could lend me some money.

name: 将来进行时-将来进行
usageNote: 表示将来某时刻正在进行的动作
example: This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Paris.

name: 将来进行时-礼貌询问
usageNote: 用将来进行时询问比will you do更客气
example: Will you be needing anything else?

name: 将来进行时-将来安排
usageNote: 表示预料将来会发生的事
example: I will be seeing him at the meeting next week.

name: 现在完成时-经历
usageNote: 表示过去某时发生过的事（不强调何时）
example: Have you ever been to Japan?

name: 现在完成时-结果
function: 过去动作对现在的影响
usageNote: 强调过去动作造成现在可见的结果
example: I have lost my keys.（现在仍找不到）

name: 现在完成时-持续
function: 从过去持续到现在的状态
usageNote: 常与for, since连用表示持续时间
example: I have lived here for ten years.

name: 现在完成时-近期动作
usageNote: just, already, yet常与现在完成时连用
example: She has just left. / I have already finished.

name: have been to
template: Subj + have/has + been to + 地点
usageNote: 表示某人有去过某地的经历
example: I have been to Shanghai twice.

name: have gone to
template: Subj + have/has + gone to + 地点
usageNote: 表示人已去某地，现在不在说话现场
example: He has gone to the library.

name: 过去完成时-过去的过去
usageNote: 表示在过去某个时间点之前已经完成的动作
example: When I arrived, the train had left.

name: 将来完成时
template: Subj + will have + p.p. + by + 时间
usageNote: 表示到将来某一时间为止已经完成的动作
example: By next year, I will have graduated.

name: 现在完成进行时
function: 从过去持续到现在并可能继续
usageNote: 强调动作的持续性和未完成性
example: I have been waiting for two hours.

name: 现在完成进行时-重复
usageNote: 表示最近一段时间内反复发生的动作
example: Lately, he has been working late every night.

name: 过去完成进行时
function: 过去某时前一直在进行
usageNote: 表示在过去某一时刻之前一直持续的动作
example: She had been studying for hours before the exam.

name: 一般现在时被动
usageNote: 表示现在经常性或习惯性的被动
example: English is spoken in many countries.

name: 一般过去时被动
usageNote: 表示过去某个时间发生的被动动作
example: The bridge was built in 1950.

name: 一般将来时被动
usageNote: 表示将来将要发生的被动
example: The meeting will be held tomorrow.

name: 现在进行时被动
usageNote: 表示说话时正在被进行的被动动作
example: The house is being painted.

name: 现在完成时被动
usageNote: 表示到说话时已经完成的被动动作
example: The book has been translated into many languages.

name: 情态动词被动
usageNote: 情态动词后的被动语态
example: This problem must be solved at once.

name: 情态完成被动
coreWords: can, may, must, should, have, be
usageNote: 对过去被动动作的情态推测
example: The letter should have been sent yesterday.

name: 带双宾语的被动
usageNote: 间接宾语或直接宾语均可作主语
example: I was given a book. / A book was given to me.

name: 过去将来时
usageNote: 表示从过去某一时刻看将要发生的事
example: He promised he would come back soon.

name: 过去将来进行时
function: 过去看将来某时正进行
usageNote: 表示从过去看将来某一时刻正在进行的动作
example: He said he would be working at 8.

name: 将来完成进行时
function: 到将来某时前一直在进行
usageNote: 表示到将来某一时间为止一直在进行的动作
example: By May, I will have been working here for 3 years.

name: be supposed to
usageNote: 表示他人对主语的期望或被规定做的事
example: You are supposed to wear a uniform.

name: be expected to
usageNote: 表示预期或期望某人做某事
example: Students are expected to arrive on time.

name: 现在完成时与过去时间区别
function: 不能与确定的过去时间连用
usageNote: 现在完成时不能与yesterday, last week等明确过去时间连用
example: I have seen the film.（不说I have seen it yesterday）

name: 瞬间动词的完成体
function: 瞬间动词不与时段连用
usageNote: die, leave, arrive等瞬间动词不能与for/since连用表持续
example: He has died.（不说He has died for three years）

name: be动词否定
usageNote: be动词后直接加not构成否定
example: She is not happy with the result.

name: 实义动词否定
usageNote: 借助助动词do/does/did + not构成否定
example: I do not like coffee. / He doesn't go there.

name: 情态动词否定
coreWords: can, may, must, will, shall, not
usageNote: 情态动词后直接加not构成否定
example: You should not give up. / I cannot agree.

name: 完成时否定
example: I have not finished yet. / She hasn't called me.

name: 进行时否定
example: He is not listening to me.

name: no + 名词
usageNote: no后接名词，表示完全否定
example: There is no water in the bottle.

name: not any
usageNote: not...any表示完全否定，用于否定句
example: I don't have any money.

name: not a/an + 名词
example: Not a student was late for class.

name: never位置
usageNote: 频率副词never位于实义动词前、助动词后
example: I have never seen such a beautiful sight.

name: seldom/rarely/hardly否定副词
usageNote: 否定副词置于实义动词前、助动词后
example: I hardly know him. / She rarely complains.

name: neither...nor
usageNote: 连接两个并列成分，动词与靠近的主语一致
example: Neither John nor Mary likes spicy food.

name: not...at all
example: I don't like it at all.
variants: not in the least/not a bit

name: not...until
example: I didn't go to bed until midnight.

name: no longer/no more
usageNote: no longer置于助动词后实义动词前，no more常置于句末
example: He no longer lives here. / He doesn't live here any more.

name: none of
usageNote: none of后接复数名词或代词，动词可用单/复数
example: None of the students passed the exam.

name: nothing/nobody/nowhere+肯定动词
usageNote: 否定代词/副词本身含否定意义，后接肯定式动词
example: Nobody knows the answer.

name: 双重否定表肯定
usageNote: 两个否定词连用表示肯定意义
example: You cannot succeed without working hard.

name: 部分否定-not all
usageNote: not与all/every/both连用表部分否定，非全部否定
example: Not all birds can fly.

name: 全部否定-none
usageNote: none表示三者及以上的全部否定
example: None of my friends smoke.

name: 全部否定-all...not
example: All that glitters is not gold.

name: can't...too/enough
usageNote: 表面否定实则肯定，表越...越好
example: You cannot be too careful.

name: There be no doing
usageNote: there is no后接动名词表不可能
example: There is no knowing what will happen.

name: without + doing
usageNote: 介词without后接动名词，表条件否定
example: He left without saying goodbye.

name: instead of + doing
usageNote: 介词短语后接动名词表替代
example: Instead of going out, I stayed at home.

name: rather than do
example: I prefer to walk rather than take a bus.

name: too...to否定
example: He is too young to go to school.

name: far from + doing
usageNote: far from后接动名词或形容词
example: The result is far from satisfactory.

name: anything but
usageNote: anything but表面肯定实则否定
example: The film was anything but boring.

name: not to mention
usageNote: 用于补充说明，表示前面已说的已足够
example: He can't walk, not to mention running.

name: let alone
example: I can't afford a bike, let alone a car.

name: 基数词-个位数
coreWords: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine
usageNote: 基本数词，构成其他数字的基础
example: I have three books.

name: 基数词-十几
coreWords: eleven, twelve, thirteen, fourteen, fifteen, sixteen, seventeen, eighteen, nineteen
example: She is sixteen years old.

name: 基数词-整十
coreWords: twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety
example: My father is fifty years old.

name: 基数词-复合数字
template: 整十 + 连字符 + 个位
coreWords: twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety
usageNote: 十位与个位间用连字符连接
example: There are twenty-three students in the class.

name: 基数词-百
example: Two hundred people attended the meeting.

name: 基数词-千
example: The population is about five thousand.

name: 基数词-百万
example: The city has two million people.

name: 基数词-十亿
example: China has over one billion people.

name: hundreds/thousands/millions of
example: Hundreds of birds flew across the sky.

name: 序数词-基本
usageNote: 序数词表示顺序和位置
example: She won first place in the race.

name: 序数词-第4-第19
coreWords: four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, eleven, twelve, thirteen, fourteen, fifteen, sixteen, seventeen, eighteen, nineteen
usageNote: 多数序数词在基数词后加th
example: December is the twelfth month.

name: 序数词-整十序数
coreWords: twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety
usageNote: 整十序数词把y改为ie再加th
example: Today is her twentieth birthday.

name: 序数词-复合序数
coreWords: twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety, one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine
usageNote: 只把最后的个位数变成序数词
example: This is the twenty-first century.

name: 序数词前加the
usageNote: 序数词通常与定冠词the连用
example: He was the first to arrive.

name: 分数表达
coreWords: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten
usageNote: 分子用基数词，分母用序数词
example: One-third of the students are girls.

name: half分数
example: Half of the cake is gone.

name: quarter分数
example: A quarter of an hour is 15 minutes.

name: 倍数-twice
template: twice + as + Adj + as / the + N + of
example: This room is twice as big as that one.

name: 倍数-three times
template: 基数词 + times + as + Adj + as
example: His house is three times larger than mine.

name: double
usageNote: 可作形容词、动词或名词
example: I ordered a double coffee.

name: 小数表达
template: 基数词 + point + 基数词
example: The price is three point five dollars.

name: percent百分比
example: Fifty percent of the students passed.

name: dozen
example: I bought two dozen eggs.

name: score
example: Four score years = eighty years

name: couple
usageNote: a couple of表示两个或少量
example: I need a couple of minutes.

name: pair
usageNote: a pair of用于成对出现的物品
example: I bought a pair of shoes.

name: about/around/roughly/approximately
example: About two hundred people came.

name: or so
usageNote: or so置于数字后表示约数
example: It will take an hour or so.

name: more than/over
usageNote: more than和over表示超过某一数量
example: More than fifty students joined.

name: less than/under/below
example: Less than twenty people were there.

name: both...and
usageNote: 连接两个并列成分（主语、宾语、表语等）
example: Both Tom and Jerry came to the party.

name: either...or
usageNote: 连接两个并列成分，动词就近一致
example: Either you or he is wrong.

name: neither...nor
usageNote: 连接两个并列成分，动词就近一致
example: Neither my father nor my mother likes cats.

name: as...as同级比较
template: as + Adj/Adv + as + 比较对象
example: She is as tall as her mother.

name: not as/so...as
usageNote: 表示否定意义的同级比较
example: He is not as/so tall as his brother.

name: if条件句-第一类
template: If + S + V, S + will + V
usageNote: 表示可能发生的条件及结果
example: If you try hard, you will succeed.

name: if条件句-第二类
template: If + S + V-ed, S + would + V
usageNote: 表示与现在事实相反的假设
example: If I were you, I would study harder.

name: if条件句-第三类
template: If + S + had + p.p., S + would have + p.p.
usageNote: 表示与过去事实相反的假设
example: If I had known, I would have helped you.

name: 人称代词-主格
template: I, you, he, she, it, we, they
coreWords: I, you, he, she, it, we, they
usageNote: 人称代词主格在句中作主语
example: We are students.
variants: me, him, her, us, them（宾格）

name: 人称代词-宾格
template: me, you, him, her, it, us, them
coreWords: me, you, him, her, it, us, them
usageNote: 人称代词宾格在句中作宾语
example: She invited me to dinner.

name: 物主代词-形容词性
template: my, your, his, her, its, our, their
coreWords: my, your, his, her, its, our, their
usageNote: 形容词性物主代词后必须接名词
example: This is my book.

name: 物主代词-名词性
template: mine, yours, his, hers, its, ours, theirs
coreWords: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs
usageNote: 名词性物主代词独立使用，后不跟名词
example: This book is mine.

name: 反身代词
template: myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves
coreWords: myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves
usageNote: 反身代词指代主语自身
example: He taught himself English.

name: 反身代词-强调
coreWords: myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves
usageNote: 反身代词作主语同位语表强调
example: I myself will do it.

name: 反身代词-固定搭配
coreWords: enjoy, help, teach, look, after, oneself
usageNote: 某些动词后必须接反身代词
example: Enjoy yourself at the party!

name: by oneself
coreWords: by, myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves
example: She lives by herself.

name: 相互代词-each other
usageNote: each other用于两者之间的相互关系
example: They love each other.

name: 相互代词-one another
usageNote: one another传统上用于三者及以上
example: The team members help one another.

name: 指示代词-this/that
usageNote: this指近处或下文，that指远处或上文
example: This is my friend and that is hers.

name: 指示代词-these/those
example: Those are my books.

name: such
example: Such a beautiful day! / I've never seen such kindness.

name: 疑问代词-who/whom
example: Who is coming? / Whom did you see?

name: 疑问代词-whose
example: Whose pen is this?

name: 疑问代词-which/what
usageNote: which指有范围的选择，what指无范围
example: Which do you prefer, tea or coffee? / What is your name?

name: 关系代词-that
template: 先行词（人或物） + that + V
usageNote: that可指人或物，不能用于介词后
example: This is the house that Jack built.

name: 不定代词-some/any
usageNote: some用于肯定，any用于否定/疑问
example: Someone is at the door. / Is anyone there?

name: 不定代词-every/no
coreWords: every, no, body, one, thing, where
example: Everyone makes mistakes. / Nothing is impossible.

name: 复合不定代词+形容词
usageNote: 复合不定代词后的形容词必须后置
example: There is something important to tell you.

name: it指时间/天气/距离
template: It + be + 时间/天气/距离
usageNote: it作形式主语指时间、天气、距离等
example: It is ten o'clock. / It is raining.

name: it takes...
template: It + takes/took + sb + 时间 + to do
usageNote: it takes表做某事花费某人多少时间
example: It takes me an hour to get to school.

name: a/an-首次提及
usageNote: 第一次提到某人或某物时用不定冠词
example: I saw a dog in the park.

name: a/an-泛指一类
usageNote: 不定冠词+可数名词单数可泛指一类
example: A dog is a loyal animal.
variants: the + 单数名词（泛指）/零冠词复数

name: a/an-职业身份
example: She is a doctor. / He is an engineer.
variants: 零冠词（表独一无二职位时）

name: a/an-数量one
example: I need a pen. (= one pen)

name: a/an-单位量词
usageNote: 表示重量、数量、容积等单位
example: a cup of tea / a piece of cake / a bottle of water

name: the-特指
usageNote: 指说话双方都知道的人或物
example: Close the door, please.

name: the-独一无二
function: 世界上独一无二的事物
usageNote: the用于表示独一无二的事物前
example: the sun, the moon, the earth, the sky

name: the-最高级
function: 形容词最高级前必须加the
usageNote: 形容词和副词最高级前用定冠词the
example: He is the tallest student in our class.

name: the-序数词前
usageNote: 序数词表示顺序时前面加the
example: This is the first time I've been here.

name: the-乐器前
usageNote: 演奏乐器时乐器名称前加定冠词
example: She can play the piano.

name: 零冠词-球类棋类
usageNote: play后接球类或棋类运动时不加冠词
example: They play football every Sunday.

name: 零冠词-三餐
usageNote: breakfast, lunch, dinner前通常不加冠词
example: I usually have breakfast at 7.

name: 零冠词-学科语言
usageNote: 表示学科和语言的名词前一般不加冠词
example: I like English and math.

name: 零冠词-专有名词
usageNote: 人名、国名、城市名等专有名词前一般不加冠词
example: China is a large country.

name: the + 形容词=一类人
usageNote: the+形容词表示具有某种特征的一类人
example: the rich, the poor, the old, the young, the sick

name: the + 姓氏复数
usageNote: 姓氏复数前加the表示全家人
example: The Smiths are coming to dinner.

name: a + 人名
usageNote: 不定冠词+人名表示某个叫此名的人
example: A Mr. Wang called you this morning.

name: 零冠词-抽象名词
usageNote: 抽象名词泛指时不加冠词
example: Love is blind. / Health is wealth.

name: 零冠词-物质名词
usageNote: 泛指的物质名词前不加冠词
example: Water is essential for life.

name: the + 江河海洋
usageNote: 江、河、海、洋、山脉等地理名称前加the
example: the Yangtze River / the Pacific Ocean

name: the + 某些国名
usageNote: 复数国名、含普通名词的国名前加the
example: the United States / the United Kingdom

name: 零冠词-称呼语
function: 称呼语前一般不加冠词
usageNote: 称呼语直接用于人名前不加冠词
example: Mr. Smith is our teacher.

name: 零冠词-节假日
function: 大多数节日前不加冠词
usageNote: 传统节日前一般不加冠词
example: We celebrate Christmas in December.

name: by + 交通工具
template: by + 零冠词 + 交通工具
coreWords: by, bus, car, train, plane, bike
usageNote: by后接交通工具不加冠词
example: I go to school by bus.

name: 零冠词-名词所有格
template: 名词所有格 + 零冠词 + 名词
function: 名词所有格后不加冠词
usageNote: 名词所有格限定了名词，前面不再加冠词
example: This is Tom's book.

name: 问候-Hello/Hi
usageNote: 最常用的英语问候语，可用于各种场合
example: Hello! How are you doing today?

name: 问候-Good morning
usageNote: 根据一天中的不同时段使用不同问候
example: Good morning! Did you sleep well?

name: 问候-How do you do
usageNote: 用于初次见面的正式场合，回答也用同样的话
example: How do you do? — How do you do?

name: 问候-How are you
usageNote: 用于熟人之间询问近况
example: How are you? — I'm fine, thank you.
variants: How's it going/How have you been

name: 告别-Goodbye
example: Goodbye! See you tomorrow.

name: 告别-See you
usageNote: see you系列为较随意的告别
example: See you later! Take care!

name: 告别-Take care
template: Take care / Take care of yourself
usageNote: 告别时表达关心和祝愿
example: Take care on your way home.

name: 感谢-Thank you
template: Thank you / Thanks / Thank you very much
usageNote: thank you为最基本的感谢用语
example: Thank you for your help.

name: 感谢-I appreciate
usageNote: 比thank you更正式，表达更深的感谢
example: I really appreciate your kindness.

name: 感谢-You shouldn't have
usageNote: 用于收到礼物时表达惊喜和感谢
example: Oh, you shouldn't have! Thank you so much!

name: 道歉-Sorry
example: I'm sorry for being late.

name: 道歉-I apologize
example: I sincerely apologize for my mistake.

name: 道歉-Excuse me
usageNote: excuse me用于引起注意、请人让路等
example: Excuse me, where is the station?

name: 请求-Could you
usageNote: could you为最常用的礼貌请求句型
example: Could you please open the window?

name: 请求-Would you mind
example: Would you mind closing the door?

name: 请求-Would you like
usageNote: would you like用于礼貌地提供某物或邀请
example: Would you like a cup of tea?

name: 邀请-Would you like to
