template: 主语 + find/think/consider/make/feel + it + 形容词/名词 + to do/that从句
coreWords: find(v.), think(v.), consider(v.), make(v.), feel(v.), it(pron.)
function: it作形式宾语，代替后面的不定式或从句
usageNote: 当不定式或从句作宾语且后面有宾补时，常用it作形式宾语。
example: I find it easy to learn English. / He thinks it important that we study hard. / She made it a rule to get up early. / I feel it my duty to help.
variants: depend on it that...：You may depend on it that he will come.

name: It is/was + the first/second...time + that从句(完成时)
template: It is/was + the first/second/last/best...time + that + 主语 + have/had done
coreWords: it(pron.), first(num.), second(num.), last(a.), best(a.), time(n.), that(conj.)
usageNote: It is the first time that...从句用现在完成时；It was the first time that...从句用过去完成时。
example: It is the first time that I have been here. / It was the second time that he had lied to me. / This is the best film I have ever seen.
variants: This/That is the first time + that从句

name: 疑问词+ever引导名词性从句
template: 疑问词 + ever + 从句(陈述语序)
coreWords: whoever, whatever, whichever, whenever, wherever, however
function: 疑问词+ever引导名词性从句，相当于anyone who/anything that
usageNote: whoever, whatever, whichever, whenever, wherever, however可引导名词性从句、让步状语从句。引导名词性从句时=any...that。
example: Whoever breaks the rule will be punished. / You can take whatever you like. / I will talk to whoever is in charge. / Choose whichever you prefer.

name: 疑问词+to do-名词性短语构式
template: 疑问词 + to do(不定式)
coreWords: what, which, who, whom, where, when, how, whether
function: 疑问词+不定式相当于名词性从句
usageNote: 疑问词+to do在句中可作主语、宾语、表语。相当于一个名词性从句。
example: I don't know what to do. / Can you tell me how to get there? / The question is when to start. / I wonder whether to go.
variants: which to choose / where to go / who to ask

name: Not...until-直到……才
template: 主语 + not + 谓语 + until + 时间/从句
usageNote: not...until表示'直到……时候才……'，可与倒装和强调句型转换。
example: I didn't know the truth until yesterday. / He won't come until you call him. / She didn't stop working until midnight.
variants: Not until yesterday did I know the truth.(倒装) / It was not until yesterday that I knew.(强调)

name: It looks/seems/appears as if/though-表观构式
template: It looks/seems/appears as if/though + 从句
coreWords: look(v.), seem(v.), appear(v.), as(conj.), if(conj.), though(conj.)
usageNote: it looks/seems/appears as if/though表示'看起来/似乎'，后可接陈述语气或虚拟语气。
example: It looks as if it is going to rain. / It seems as though he were tired.(虚拟) / It appears as if nothing had happened.
variants: It seems that... / It appears that...

name: It is said/reported/believed/known that...-报道构式
coreWords: say(v.), report(v.), believe(v.), know(v.), think(v.), consider(v.), expect(v.), hope(v.)
usageNote: it is said/reported/believed等结构表示传闻、报道或普遍看法。可转换为主语+be+过去分词+不定式。
example: It is said that he is honest. / It is reported that they won. / He is said to be honest. (= It is said that he is honest.)

name: The+比较级...the+比较级-越……越……
template: The + 比较级 + 主语 + 谓语 + , + the + 比较级 + 主语 + 谓语
usageNote: the more...the more表示比例关系。前面的分句相当于条件状语从句，后面的相当于主句。
example: The more you read, the more you know. / The harder you work, the more progress you make. / The earlier you start, the sooner you finish.
variants: The more, the better. / The sooner, the better.

name: Do you mind/Would you mind + doing-请求构式
template: Do you mind/Would you mind + doing / if从句
usageNote: mind后接动名词或if从句。回答不介意时用No/Of course not/Certainly not；介意时用Sorry/I'm afraid。
example: Do you mind my opening the window? / Would you mind if I open the window? / Would you mind helping me?
variants: 回答：No, not at all. / Of course not. / I'm sorry, but...

name: There being no/other+noun-独立主格构式
template: There being + no/other + 名词 + , + 主句
usageNote: there being + 名词构成独立主格，常作原因状语。
example: There being no bus, we had to walk. / There being nothing else to do, we went home. / There being no time left, we hurried.
variants: With no bus available, we had to walk.

name: 名词/形容词+as/though+主语+谓语-让步倒装构式
template: 名词(无冠词)/形容词/副词/动词原形 + as/though + 主语 + 谓语
function: '尽管/虽然'，as/though引导让步状语从句的倒装
usageNote: as/though引导让步从句必须倒装：将表语(形容词/不带冠词的名词)、状语(副词)或动词原形置于句首。
example: Child as he is, he knows a lot. / Hard as he tried, he failed. / Try as he might, he couldn't open it. / Much as I like him, I can't trust him.

name: So+adj./adv.+that-结果状语从句(正常语序)
template: 主语 + 谓语 + so + adj./adv. + that + 从句
usageNote: so...that引导结果状语从句。so后接形容词或副词。
example: He was so tired that he fell asleep. / She speaks so fast that I can't follow. / The box is so heavy that I can't carry it.
variants: So + many/much/few/little + 名词 + that：So many people came that we had no room.

name: Such+(a/an)+adj.+n.+that-结果状语从句
template: 主语 + 谓语 + such + (a/an) + adj. + n. + that + 从句
usageNote: such...that引导结果状语从句。such后接(a/an)+adj.+名词。
example: It was such a boring film that I fell asleep. / They are such good students that teachers love them. / He told such funny stories that we all laughed.
variants: such + 不可数名词/复数名词(不加a/an)：such fine weather that...

name: A is to B what C is to D-比喻构式
template: A + is to + B + what + C + is to + D
usageNote: what引导比较状语从句，表示类比。
example: Air is to man what water is to fish. / Reading is to the mind what exercise is to the body.

name: The reason why...is that...-原因构式
template: The reason why + 从句 + is that + 从句
usageNote: the reason why...is that...是固定句型，表语从句用that引导，不用because。
example: The reason why I was late is that I missed the bus. / The reason why he left is that he found a better job. / Do you know the reason why she cried?

name: It/That/This is because...-原因构式
usageNote: it/that/this is because表示'这是因为'，because引导表语从句。
example: That's because he didn't study hard. / It is because I care about you. / This is because we need more time.
variants: The reason is that...(不用because) / That is why...(结果)

name: 否定转移(I don't think/suppose/believe/imagine/expect)
template: I don't think/suppose/believe/imagine/expect/fancy + that + 肯定从句
coreWords: not(ad.), think(v.), suppose(v.), believe(v.), imagine(v.), expect(v.)
function: 否定转移到主句，从句用肯定
usageNote: think, suppose, believe, imagine, expect等动词后的宾语从句否定常转移到主句。
example: I don't think he is right.(我认为他不对) / I don't suppose he will come. / I don't believe she knows. / I don't imagine they will agree.
variants: Do you think he is right? / Yes, I think so. / No, I don't think so.

name: Whether...or not-是否构式
template: 主语 + 谓语 + whether + 从句 + or not
usageNote: whether...or not表示'是否'。whether可与or not连用；if不能与or not直接连用(分开可)。whether可引导主语从句、表语从句、同位语从句，if不能。
example: I don't know whether he will come or not. / Whether he comes or not doesn't matter. / The question is whether we should go. / I wonder whether to go.
variants: whether or not可置于句首：Whether or not he comes, we will start.

name: It is/has been + 时间段 + since...-时间构式
template: It is/has been + 时间段 + since + 从句(一般过去时)
usageNote: it is/has been + 时间段 + since + 一般过去时。表示从过去某时到现在的时间。
example: It is ten years since I left school. / It has been three days since he came. / It is a long time since we met.

name: The first/second/last/best time + that从句(完成时)
template: This/It/That is/was + the first/second/last/best/worst/only...time + that + 从句(完成时)
coreWords: first(num.), second(num.), last(a.), best(a.), worst(a.), only(a.), time(n.), that(conj.)
usageNote: is + the first time that...从句用现在完成时；was + the first time that...从句用过去完成时。
example: This is the first time I have been here. / It was the second time he had lied. / That is the best film I have ever seen.
variants: the first time可作连词：The first time I saw her, I liked her.

name: By the time + 从句-时间完成构式
template: By the time + (that) + 从句 + , + 主句(完成时)
usageNote: by the time引导时间状语从句，主句常用完成时态。
example: By the time he arrived, the meeting had started. / By the time you read this, I will have left. / By the time she finishes, it will be dark.
variants: By the time + 一般现在时, 主句 + 将来完成时

name: Ought to / Should have done-义务虚拟构式
template: 主语 + ought to/should + have done
usageNote: ought to/should have done表示过去本应该做某事但未做，含有责备或遗憾意味。
example: You ought to have told me earlier. / You should have come on time. / He should have studied harder.
variants: oughtn't to have done / shouldn't have done(本不该做却做了)

name: Happened to do / Happen to be doing-碰巧构式
template: 主语 + happen to + do / happen to be doing / happen to have done
usageNote: happen to do(碰巧做)；happen to be doing(碰巧正在做)；happen to have done(碰巧已经做了)。
example: I happened to meet him yesterday. / I happened to be out when you called. / He happened to have left before I arrived.

name: It happened that...-形式主语碰巧构式
usageNote: it happened/chanced/occurred that...为形式主语句型，表示'碰巧/偶然'。
example: It happened that I knew the answer. / It chanced that we met. / It occurred to me that I had forgotten my key.

name: Sb. occur to...-想起构式
template: It occurs to sb. that + 从句 / It occurs to sb. to do
usageNote: it occurs to sb. that...表示'某人突然想起'。occur不能用被动语态。
example: It occurred to me that I had forgotten my key. / It never occurred to me to ask. / Did it occur to you that he might be wrong?

name: Make + it + adj./n. + for sb. to do-形式宾语构式
template: 主语 + make/think/find/consider + it + adj./n. + for sb. + to do
usageNote: 当不定式或从句作宾语且后面有宾补时，常用it作形式宾语。
example: I find it easy to learn English. / She made it a rule to get up early. / He thinks it important for us to study hard.

name: It is up to sb. to do-取决于某人构式
template: It is up to + sb. + to do / 从句
usageNote: it is up to sb.表示'由某人决定/取决于某人'。
example: It is up to you to decide. / It is up to him whether we go. / It is up to the teacher to choose.

name: Be likely to / Be probable that-可能性构式
template: 主语 + be likely to do / It is probable/likely/possible that + 从句
usageNote: likely可作形容词(人/物作主语：sb./sth. be likely to do)；probable/possible常用it作形式主语。
example: He is likely to come. / It is probable that he will come. / It is possible for him to come. / Rain is likely this afternoon.

name: Be said/reported/known/believed/thought to do-传闻构式
template: 主语 + be said/reported/known/believed/thought/considered + to do/to be doing/to have done
coreWords: say(v.), report(v.), know(v.), believe(v.), think(v.), consider(v.)
function: '据说/据报道/据认为某人做/正在做/已经做了'
usageNote: be said/reported/known等+to do(动作发生在谓语之后/同时)；+to be doing(动作正在进行)；+to have done(动作发生在谓语之前)。
example: He is said to be honest. / He is said to be studying abroad. / He is said to have won the prize.

name: break the ice
usageNote: 习语，非组成性。字面义为破冰，比喻义为打破沉默或尴尬气氛
example: At the party, Tom told a joke to break the ice.

name: even if/though
usageNote: 连词短语。引导让步状语从句
example: Even if it rains, I'll go.

name: now that
usageNote: 连词。引导原因状语从句
example: Now that you are here, let's start.

name: No pain, no gain.
example: Study hard! No pain, no gain.

name: You can't have your cake and eat it too.
template: You + can't + have + your + cake + and + eat + it + too
example: You must choose. You can't have your cake and eat it too.

name: subway
usageNote: sub-源自拉丁语sub（下面），way源自古英语weg（道路）。subway最早记录于1820-1830年代，原义为'地下管道通道'，后转义为'地下铁路'。美式英语中subway=地铁，英式英语中subway=地下人行通道（地铁用underground/tube）。该构式是拉丁前缀+本土词根的混合复合词。
example: I take the subway to school because it is fast and cheap.
variants: underground（地铁，英式）, tube（地铁，伦敦）, metro（地铁，法语借词）, underground passage（地下通道）, overpass（过街天桥）

name: supermarket
function: 复合名词，表示大型自助零售商店
usageNote: 由super-（拉丁语前缀，意为'在……之上、超越'，来自superus）与market（市场，源自古英语market/market，经由古法语来自拉丁语mercatus）复合而成。supermarket一词最早记录于1933年的美国英语，指一种大型自助式食品零售商店。该词体现了20世纪消费社会的兴起。能产性中等。
example: My mother goes to the supermarket every Saturday morning.
variants: superstore（大型超市）, hypermarket（大卖场）, grocery store（杂货店）

name: cooperate
coreWords: co-（共同前缀，来自拉丁语com-）+ operate（操作/运转，来自3500词）
function: 表示与他人一起工作以达成共同目标
usageNote: 词源：co-是拉丁语com-的变体，表'共同'；operate来自拉丁语operari（工作、劳动）。cooperate经拉丁语cooperari进入英语，17世纪确立。co-表'共同'是正式用语中的常见前缀。
example: The two schools decided to cooperate and share their books and teachers.
variants: cooperation (n.合作), cooperative (adj.合作的), operate→cooperate (对), collaborate (v.协作), coordinate (v.协调)

name: telephone
coreWords: tele-（远前缀，来自希腊语）+ phone（声音，来自希腊语phone）
function: 表示通过电信号进行远距离语音通话的装置
usageNote: 词源：tele-来自希腊语tele（远）；phone来自希腊语phone（声音）。telephone字面义'远音'，19世纪中期创造。tele-是科技词汇的重要前缀，能产性高。
example: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell in 1876.
variants: telephone (v.打电话), telephonic (adj.电话的), phone (n.电话=口语), call (v.呼叫), mobile phone (n.手机)

name: translate
coreWords: trans-（跨越前缀，来自拉丁语）+ late（搬运，来自拉丁语latus）
function: 表示将一种语言转换为另一种语言
usageNote: 词源：translate来自拉丁语translatus（trans-跨越+latus搬运，ferre的过去分词），字面义'搬运到另一边'。14世纪进入英语，最初也指'转移'。trans-是重要的跨语言前缀。
example: Can you translate this letter from French into English for me?
variants: translation (n.翻译), translator (n.译者), translate→interpret (近义), transfer (v.转移), transform (v.转变)

name: microphone
coreWords: micro-（微小前缀，来自希腊语）+ phone（声音，来自希腊语phone）
function: 表示将微小声音放大传输的装置
usageNote: 词源：micro-来自希腊语mikros（微小）；phone来自希腊语phone（声音）。microphone字面义'微小的声音'，19世纪后期发明。micro-是科技词汇的重要前缀，表'微小'能产性高。
example: The speaker held the microphone close to his mouth so everyone could hear.
variants: microphone (n.麦克风=mic), megaphone (n.扩音器), phone (n.电话), loudspeaker (n.扬声器), amplifier (n.扩音器)

name: enlarge
coreWords: en-（使动前缀，来自古法语）+ large（大的，来自3500词）
usageNote: 词源：en-来自古法语en-，表'使成为、进入'；large来自古法语large。enlarge字面义'使变大'。14世纪进入英语。en-表'使动'是英语中重要的派生构式，能产性高：enable, enrich, enlarge等。
example: The school plans to enlarge the library so it can hold more books.
variants: enlargement (n.扩大), enlarge→reduce (反义), expand (v.扩大), grow (v.增长), shrink (v.收缩)

name: may/might表可能性
usageNote: might比may可能性更小，常用于虚拟语气
example: She may be busy now. / It might snow later.

name: may表祝愿
usageNote: 倒装结构，用于正式祝愿
example: May you succeed in your exam! / May you be happy!

name: might have done
usageNote: 虚拟语气，表示与过去事实相反的推测
example: You might have told me earlier.

name: must have done
usageNote: 表示对过去事情的较有把握的推断
example: She must have forgotten the meeting.

name: should have done
usageNote: 虚拟语气，表过去应做而实际未做的事
example: You should have told me the truth.

name: would表虚拟
template: If + S + V, S + would + V
usageNote: 第二、三类条件句的主句谓语
example: If I were you, I would take that chance.

name: ought to have done
usageNote: 与should have done同义，语义稍强
example: You ought to have studied harder.

name: 一般现在时-代替将来时
function: 时间/条件从句中表将来
usageNote: 时间/条件状语从句中用一般现在代替一般将来
example: If it rains, we will stay at home.

name: 一般过去时-虚拟语气
template: If + S + V-ed, S + would + V
usageNote: 表示与现在事实相反的假设
example: If I knew the answer, I would tell you.

name: 过去完成时-间接引语
usageNote: 过去完成时常用于间接引语中
example: He said he had finished his work.

name: 过去完成时-第三类条件句
template: If + S + had + p.p., S + would have + p.p.
function: 与过去事实相反的假设
usageNote: 第三类条件句：表示过去本可发生但未发生的事
example: If I had known, I would have told you.

name: 过去完成时-hardly...when
template: Hardly had + S + p.p. when + S + V-ed
example: Hardly had I arrived when it started raining.

name: since从句
template: It is/has been + 时段 + since + S + V-ed
example: It has been three years since I last saw him.

name: for/since时间段
template: Subj + have/has + p.p. + for/since + 时间
usageNote: for接时间段，since接时间点或从句
example: I have studied English for five years. / since 2019.

name: This/That/It is the first time
template: This/It is the first time + (that) + S + have + p.p.
example: This is the first time I have visited Paris.

name: hardly...when倒装
template: Hardly had + S + p.p. when + S + V-ed
usageNote: hardly置于句首引起部分倒装
example: Hardly had I got home when it began to rain.

name: no sooner...than倒装
template: No sooner had + S + p.p. than + S + V-ed
usageNote: no sooner置于句首引起部分倒装
example: No sooner had she arrived than she started crying.

name: scarcely...when
template: Scarcely had + S + p.p. when + S + V-ed
usageNote: scarcely置于句首引起部分倒装
example: Scarcely had the game started when it rained.

name: never句首倒装
usageNote: never置于句首引起部分倒装
example: Never have I felt so happy.

name: seldom句首倒装
usageNote: seldom置于句首引起部分倒装
example: Seldom does he go to the cinema.

name: neither句首倒装
usageNote: 表示前面否定的情况也适用于另一主语
example: I don't like coffee. Neither does he.

name: not only...but also
template: not only + X + but also + Y
usageNote: 连接两个并列成分，可倒装加强语气
example: Not only is he smart, but he is also kind.

name: not only句首倒装
template: Not only + aux + S + V, but S + also + V
usageNote: not only置于句首引起部分倒装
example: Not only did he finish first, but he also broke the record.

name: not until强调倒装
template: Not until + 时间 + did + S + V
usageNote: not until置于句首引起倒装
example: Not until then did I realize my mistake.

name: 否定转移
usageNote: 主句否定转移到从句，从句用肯定式
example: I don't think he is right.（= I think he is not right）

name: not only...but also
template: not only + X + but also + Y
usageNote: 连接两个并列成分，可倒装
example: She not only sings well but also dances beautifully.

name: whether...or
usageNote: whether可引导名词性从句，or not可省略
example: I don't know whether he will come or not.

name: whether...or选择
example: Whether it rains or shines, I will go out.

name: so...that
example: He was so tired that he fell asleep immediately.

name: such...that
template: such + (a/an) + Adj + N + that + 从句
example: It was such a beautiful day that we went for a picnic.

name: so that目的
template: Subj + V + so that + 目的从句
usageNote: so that引导目的状语从句
example: I spoke slowly so that everyone could hear.

name: so that结果
template: Subj + V + so that + 结果从句
usageNote: so that引导结果状语从句
example: It was very cold, so that the river froze.

name: such that
usageNote: such后接名词，that引导结果从句
example: His behaviour was such that everyone disliked him.

name: because引导原因
usageNote: because引导原因状语从句，语气最强
example: I stayed at home because it was raining.

name: since引导原因
usageNote: since引导的原因从句置于句首，表已知原因
example: Since you are busy, I will leave now.

name: as引导原因
usageNote: as引导原因从句语气较弱
example: As it was getting late, we decided to go home.

name: for引导原因
usageNote: for为并列连词，引导的分句不能置于句首
example: He must be ill, for he looks pale.

name: now that
usageNote: now that引导原因从句，常表新情况
example: Now that you are grown up, you should be independent.

name: although/though
usageNote: 引导让步状语从句，不与but连用
example: Although it rained, we still went hiking.

name: even though
template: Even though + 从句, + 主句
example: Even though he is poor, he is happy.

name: while表对比
example: While I like coffee, she prefers tea.

name: unless
usageNote: unless = if...not，引导条件状语从句
example: Unless you hurry, you will miss the bus.

name: provided that
usageNote: provided that引导条件从句，语气较正式
example: I will go, provided that you go too.

name: as if/though
template: Subj + V + as if/though + 从句
usageNote: as if/though引导方式状语从句，常用虚拟
example: He talks as if he knew everything.

name: even if
template: Even if + 从句, + 主句
usageNote: even if引导让步条件从句
example: Even if it rains, I will go out.

name: when时间从句
example: When I got home, my mother was cooking.

name: while时间从句
usageNote: while引导的时间段常与进行时连用
example: While I was reading, he was watching TV.

name: before时间从句
example: Think twice before you act.

name: after时间从句
example: After the rain stopped, we went outside.

name: since时间从句
template: Subj + have + p.p. + since + S + V-ed
usageNote: since引导时间从句，主句常用完成时
example: I have lived here since I was born.

name: until时间从句
template: Subj + V + until + 时间/从句
example: Wait here until I come back.

name: as soon as
template: As soon as + 从句, + 主句
usageNote: as soon as引导时间状语从句
example: As soon as I heard the news, I called her.

name: the moment
template: The moment + 从句, + 主句
example: The moment I saw him, I knew something was wrong.

name: every/each time
template: Every/Each time + 从句, + 主句
usageNote: every/each time引导时间状语从句
example: Every time I see her, she is smiling.

name: once
usageNote: once引导时间/条件状语从句
example: Once you start, you must finish.

name: where地点从句
example: Put the book where it belongs.

name: wherever
example: Wherever you go, I will follow.

name: 关系代词-who
usageNote: who在定语从句中作主语，指人
example: The girl who is singing is my sister.

name: 关系代词-whom
template: 先行词（人） + whom + S + V
usageNote: whom在定语从句中作宾语，可省略
example: The man (whom) I met yesterday is kind.

name: 关系代词-whose
usageNote: whose在定语从句中作定语，指人或物
example: I know the student whose father is a doctor.

name: 关系代词-which
usageNote: which在定语从句中作主语或宾语
example: The book which is on the table is mine.

name: it作形式主语
template: It + be + Adj/N + to do/that从句
usageNote: it代替后面的不定式或从句作形式主语
example: It is important to study hard.

name: it作形式宾语
template: Subj + V + it + Adj + to do/that从句
usageNote: it代替后面的不定式或从句作形式宾语
example: I find it difficult to learn French.

name: it强调句
template: It is/was + 被强调部分 + that/who + 其他
usageNote: it is/was...that/who为强调句型，可强调除谓语外任何成分
example: It was yesterday that I met her.

name: it seems/appears/happens
usageNote: it作形式主语，后接that从句或不定式
example: It seems that he is ill. / It happened to rain.

name: 祝愿-Hope you
usageNote: hope后接宾语从句表达愿望
example: I hope you have a great time.

name: 递进-Furthermore/Moreover
usageNote: 用于增加额外信息，语气较正式
example: The price is reasonable; furthermore, the quality is good.

name: 转折-Nevertheless/Nonetheless
example: It was raining; nevertheless, we went out.

name: 因果-Therefore/Thus
template: ..., therefore/thus, + 句子 / Therefore/Thus + 句子
example: He was ill; therefore, he stayed at home.

name: 因果-Consequently
usageNote: consequently表因果关系，语气较正式
example: She didn't study hard; consequently, she failed.

name: 因果-That's why
example: I was tired. That's why I went to bed early.

name: There is no doubt that
template: There is no doubt that + 从句
example: There is no doubt that he will succeed.

name: owing to/due to/because of
usageNote: 后接名词或动名词，不接从句
example: The game was cancelled because of the rain.

name: in spite of/despite
template: In spite of/Despite + N/V-ing, + 主句
usageNote: 后接名词或动名词，不接从句
example: In spite of the rain, we went out.

name: abandon oneself to
usageNote: 后接情感名词或动名词，含消极义
example: He abandoned himself to despair after the failure.

name: abandon hope
example: Don't abandon hope; things will get better.

name: to the best of one's ability
template: to the best of + 所有格 + ability
usageNote: 固定介词短语，作方式状语
example: I will finish the task to the best of my ability.

name: over and above
example: Over and above his salary, he gets allowances.

name: be absorbed in
usageNote: 被动形式表状态，介词in后接活动
example: He was absorbed in reading a book.
variants: be lost in, be immersed in

name: accomplish a task
example: The team accomplished their mission ahead of schedule.

name: feel accomplished
usageNote: 形容词化的过去分词作表语
example: She felt accomplished after finishing the project.

name: take into account
usageNote: 动宾分离结构，也可说take account of
example: You should take his age into account.

name: on account of
usageNote: 介词短语，后接名词，表原因
example: The match was cancelled on account of rain.

name: on no account
usageNote: 置于句首时句子需部分倒装
example: On no account should you be absent from class.

name: accuse sb. of (doing) sth.
example: He accused her of lying about her academic background.

name: achieve a balance
example: It is important to achieve a balance between work and rest.

name: a sense of achievement
usageNote: sense of后接抽象名词表示某种感觉
example: Finishing the marathon gave him a sense of achievement.

name: get across
example: The teacher tried to get the point across to the students.

name: act on
example: I acted on my doctor's advice and had a rest.

name: in action
usageNote: 介词短语作表语或状语
example: The new machine is now in action.

name: play an active role in
template: play an active role in + n./doing
example: He plays an active role in community service.

name: deliver an address
example: The president delivered an address to the nation.
variants: give a speech, make a speech

name: address a problem
example: We need to address the issue of pollution.

name: adjust to
usageNote: to为介词，后接名词或动名词
example: It took him a while to adjust to the new school.

name: admit doing/having done
usageNote: admit后接动名词作宾语，不接不定式
example: He admitted making a mistake.

name: admit sb. to/into
example: Only ticket-holders will be admitted to the concert hall.

name: adopt an attitude
example: We should adopt a positive attitude toward life.

name: have an advantage over
example: She has an advantage over others in speaking French.

name: advise doing
example: I advise leaving early to avoid traffic.

name: come of age
example: He came of age on his eighteenth birthday.

name: come to one's aid
example: A stranger came to my aid when my car broke down.

name: with the aid of
example: With the aid of a dictionary, I understood the article.

name: take aim
example: The soldier took aim at the target.

name: on/off the air
example: The show will be on the air at 8 p.m.

name: come alive
function: 活跃起来，变得生气勃勃
example: The city comes alive at night.

name: think aloud
function: 自言自语，把想法说出来
example: Sorry, I was just thinking aloud.

name: It amazes sb. that...

variants: society at large / the world at large

usageNote: 总结性用语，用于概括观点

usageNote: 用于说明主要原因或构成部分

name: let alone
template: let + alone + 名词/动词原形
usageNote: 用于强调后述情况更不可能发生
example: I can't afford a bike, let alone a car.

name: let out
example: She let out a scream when she saw the spider.

name: lie in
example: The problem lies in our lack of preparation.

name: risk one's life
example: He risked his life to save the child from the fire.

name: come to light
usageNote: 尤指隐藏的信息被揭露
example: New evidence has come to light in the investigation.

name: in the light of
template: in + the + light + of + 名词
usageNote: 也可写作 in light of（美式）
example: In the light of new evidence, we changed our plan.

name: see the light
usageNote: 多义短语，需根据语境理解
example: The book finally saw the light in 2020.

name: something like
example: The building is something like 50 meters tall.

name: limit to
template: limit + 宾语 + to + 数量/范围
usageNote: limit 作动词时双宾语结构
example: We need to limit our spending to $100.

name: within limits
example: You can borrow my car, but within limits.

name: in line with
usageNote: 表示符合某种标准或期望
example: Your proposal is in line with our policy.

name: draw the line
usageNote: 常用于表明容忍的极限
example: I don't mind jokes, but I draw the line at insults.
variants: draw the line at sth/doing sth

name: on the line
example: Hundreds of jobs are on the line.

name: live up to
usageNote: 后常接 expectation 等名词
example: He lived up to his reputation as a great speaker.

name: within living memory
example: It's the worst storm within living memory.

name: long for
usageNote: long 在此为动词，非形容词
example: She longs for the day when she can return home.

name: take a long look at
example: We need to take a long look at our spending.

name: look into
example: The police are looking into the case.

name: look down on/upon
usageNote: 与 look up to 相对
example: She looks down on people who haven't been to university.

name: lose face
usageNote: face 前无冠词，源于中文概念

name: lose track of
function: 失去…的线索；不了解…的动态

name: the lot
example: I'll take the lot - how much is it?

name: draw lots
example: They drew lots to decide who would go first.

name: make love
example: The movie scene implies they made love.

name: for the love of
template: for + the + love + of + 名词
example: For the love of God, stop making that noise!

name: like mad
example: He ran like mad to catch the bus.

name: in the main
example: In the main, the students are hardworking.

name: major in
example: She majored in computer science at university.

name: in the majority
example: Women are in the majority in the nursing profession.

name: make out
example: I can't make out what he's saying.
variants: make out sth / make out that...

name: make up for
example: Nothing can make up for the loss of a child.

name: make use of
example: We should make good use of our time.

name: all manner of
example: The shop sells all manner of goods.

name: in a manner of speaking
function: 不妨说；从某种意义上说

variants: in a way, so to speak

usageNote: map 原为名词，此处作动词

name: mark down
example: All winter clothes have been marked down by 30%.

name: mark the beginning of
example: The ceremony marked the beginning of a new era.
variants: mark the end of / mark a turning point

example: His guess was wide of the mark.

name: market share
example: The company has a 25% market share.

usageNote: into 强调进入某个家庭

name: meet a need

function: 收支平衡；勉强维持生计

example: The proposal met with strong opposition.

usageNote: 置于句首时句子需倒装

template: a + means + of + 名词/动名词

name: in memory of
example: The statue was built in memory of the heroes.

name: mend one's ways

name: get the message

example: I have to burn the midnight oil to finish this report.

usageNote: mind 在此为不可数名词

usageNote: 名词作连词用，引导时间状语从句

example: I think I'll give the party a miss.

name: a case of mistaken identity
example: It turned out to be a case of mistaken identity.

example: If I'm not mistaken, you owe me fifty dollars.

name: model on/after
usageNote: model A on B 结构
example: The school is modeled on European examples.

name: the moment (that)
example: The moment he arrived, the party began.

name: mourn for
example: The whole country mourned for the victims.
variants: mourn the loss of / grieve for

name: get a move on
example: Get a move on or we'll miss the train!

name: on the move
example: I've been on the move all day.

name: make a move
example: We should make a move soon - it's getting late.

name: commit murder
example: He was accused of committing murder.

name: get away with murder

name: face the music

name: nail down
example: We need to nail down the details of the contract.

example: We need to narrow down the list of candidates.

name: network with

name: break the news
example: The doctor broke the news to the family gently.

name: under normal circumstances

name: nose around

name: nowhere to be found

example: They are building offshore wind farms.

name: hostile to

name: exposed to
usageNote: 语域：正式；形容词+介词搭配为雅思口语和写作高频构式

name: vulnerable to
usageNote: 语域：正式；形容词+介词搭配为雅思口语和写作高频构式

name: susceptible to
usageNote: 语域：正式；形容词+介词搭配为雅思口语和写作高频构式

variants: immune to, impervious to, opposed to

name: immune to
usageNote: 语域：正式；形容词+介词搭配为雅思口语和写作高频构式

variants: resistant to, impervious to, protected from

variants: accepting of, patient with, open to

name: proficient in

variants: skilled at, competent in, adept at

name: skilled at
usageNote: 语域：半正式；形容词+介词搭配为雅思口语和写作高频构式

variants: proficient in, adept at, accomplished in

name: adept at
usageNote: 语域：正式；形容词+介词搭配为雅思口语和写作高频构式

variants: skilled at, proficient in, expert at

name: competent in
usageNote: 语域：正式；形容词+介词搭配为雅思口语和写作高频构式

variants: proficient in, capable in, skilled in

name: consistent with
usageNote: 语域：正式；形容词+介词搭配为雅思口语和写作高频构式
example: The experimental results are consistent with the predictions made by the theoretical model.
variants: in line with, compatible with, congruent with

name: a range of
template: a range of + 复数名词/不可数名词
function: 学术量化：表示范围广泛的事物
usageNote: 后接可数名词复数或不可数名词，用于正式写作中表达多样性；谓语动词可用单数或复数
example: A wide range of perspectives should be considered when formulating public policy.
variants: a broad range of, a whole range of

name: a variety of
template: a variety of + 复数名词
function: 学术量化：表示多样化的事物
usageNote: 强调事物的多样性而非单一性；variety前可加wide/great等修饰
example: Students should be exposed to a variety of learning materials to enhance their comprehension skills.
variants: a wide variety of, a great variety of, a diverse variety of

name: a number of
template: a number of + 复数名词
function: 学术量化：表示若干、一些（强调数量）
usageNote: 谓语动词用复数；区别于'the number of'；用于正式和半正式语境
example: A number of studies have demonstrated the link between sleep quality and academic performance.
variants: a large number of, a considerable number of, a small number of

name: the number of
template: the number of + 复数名词
usageNote: 中心词为number，谓语动词用单数；常接has increased/has decreased等
example: The number of international students enrolled in UK universities has increased significantly over the past decade.
variants: the total number of, the growing number of, the declining number of

name: an amount of
template: an amount of + 不可数名词
function: 学术量化：表示不可数名词的数量
usageNote: 仅用于不可数名词；常接large/small/vast等修饰语
example: A substantial amount of research funding is allocated to renewable energy projects each year.
variants: a large amount of, a considerable amount of, a vast amount of

name: a quantity of
function: 学术量化：表示数量（较正式）
usageNote: 可用于可数和不可数名词；比amount更正式
example: A considerable quantity of data was collected from over 5,000 survey respondents.
variants: a large quantity of, a small quantity of, vast quantities of

name: a large proportion of
usageNote: 用于描述部分与整体的关系；proportion前可用large/small/significant等修饰
example: A significant proportion of urban dwellers report experiencing high levels of stress on a daily basis.
variants: a small proportion of, a high proportion of, the proportion of

name: a high percentage of
function: 学术量化百分比：高比例
usageNote: 强调百分比；常与high/low/small等搭配
example: A high percentage of respondents indicated that they would support stricter environmental regulations.
variants: a low percentage of, a small percentage of, the percentage of

name: a growing number of
template: a growing/increasing number of + 复数名词
function: 学术量化趋势：越来越多的
usageNote: 强调数量的增长趋势；谓语用复数
example: A growing number of employers now recognize the value of remote working arrangements.
variants: an increasing number of, a rising number of, a steadily growing number of

name: a declining number of
template: a declining/decreasing number of + 复数名词
function: 学术量化趋势：越来越少的
usageNote: 强调数量的下降趋势；谓语用复数
example: A declining number of young people are choosing to pursue careers in traditional manufacturing industries.
variants: a decreasing number of, a falling number of, a dwindling number of

name: the majority of
template: the majority of + 复数名词/不可数名词
usageNote: 谓语动词可用单数或复数（英式英语多用复数）；比most更正式
example: The majority of experts agree that climate change poses an existential threat to human civilization.
variants: the vast majority of, the overwhelming majority of, a clear majority of

name: the minority of
template: the minority of + 复数名词
usageNote: 与majority相对；较为正式的表达
example: Only a small minority of participants expressed opposition to the proposed healthcare reforms.
variants: a small minority of, the tiny minority of

name: a handful of
template: a handful of + 复数名词
usageNote: 字面意思为'一把'，引申为少量；口语和半正式语境均可
example: A handful of pioneering companies have successfully implemented zero-waste production systems.
variants: a small handful of, a mere handful of

name: a wide range of
template: a wide/broad range of + 名词
usageNote: 雅思写作Task 2高频表达；强调多样性和覆盖面
example: The university offers a wide range of extracurricular activities designed to develop students' leadership skills.
variants: a broad range of, an extensive range of, a comprehensive range of

name: a broad spectrum of
template: a broad/wide spectrum of + 名词
function: 学术量化：广泛的光谱/范围
usageNote: spectrum原指光谱，引申为范围；比range更学术化
example: The research covers a broad spectrum of social issues, from poverty alleviation to educational inequality.
variants: a wide spectrum of, the full spectrum of, across the spectrum

name: a vast array of
template: a vast array of + 名词
function: 学术量化：大量的、排列整齐的事物
usageNote: array原指排列，引申为大量；非常正式的表达
example: The digital revolution has created a vast array of opportunities for entrepreneurs in emerging markets.
variants: a wide array of, an impressive array of, a bewildering array of

name: a significant amount of
template: a significant amount of + 不可数名词
function: 学术量化：大量的（强调重要性）
usageNote: amount专接不可数名词；significant强调统计学意义上的显著性
example: A significant amount of evidence supports the hypothesis that bilingualism enhances cognitive flexibility.
variants: a considerable amount of, a substantial amount of, a large amount of

name: a considerable degree of
template: a degree of + 不可数名词
example: Effective leadership requires a considerable degree of emotional intelligence and interpersonal sensitivity.
variants: a certain degree of, a high degree of, to some degree

name: a substantial level of
template: a level of + 不可数名词
example: Maintaining a substantial level of investment in basic research is essential for long-term economic growth.
variants: a high level of, a low level of, an adequate level of

name: to a large extent
function: 学术程度表达：在很大程度上
usageNote: 常用作句首状语或句末状语；表示程度
example: To a large extent, the success of any educational reform depends on the commitment of teaching staff.
variants: to a certain extent, to some extent, to a great extent

name: in large part
template: in large part / in part / for the most part
function: 学术部分表达：在很大程度上
usageNote: 常用作状语；for the most part意为'总体上'
example: The economic recovery was due in large part to government stimulus packages and monetary easing.
variants: in part, for the most part, in no small part

name: for the most part
function: 学术部分表达：总体而言、在大多数情况下
usageNote: 置于句首或句中；表示总体情况
example: For the most part, the findings of this study are consistent with previous research in this field.
variants: by and large, on the whole, in general

name: the role of
usageNote: 常与play/assume/fulfill等动词搭配；后可接in短语
example: The role of government in regulating financial markets remains a subject of intense debate.
variants: play a role in, an important role, the crucial role of

name: the importance of
template: the importance of + N / the importance of doing
usageNote: 雅思写作开头段常用；后可接lies in等
example: The importance of early childhood education cannot be overstated in shaping future academic outcomes.
variants: the significance of, the relevance of, the value of

name: the significance of
function: 学术领属：...的重要性/意义
usageNote: 比importance更正式；常用于研究结论中
example: This study highlights the significance of cultural factors in determining consumer behavior patterns.
variants: the importance of, the relevance of, the implications of

name: the impact of
usageNote: impact后常接on/upon；可与have/exert/assess等动词搭配
example: The impact of social media on adolescent mental health has attracted considerable scholarly attention.
variants: the effect of, the influence of, the consequence of

name: the effect of
usageNote: cause and effect关系中的常用表达；effect前可用positive/negative/significant等修饰
example: Researchers have extensively studied the effect of exercise on cognitive function in older adults.
variants: the positive effect of, the adverse effect of, the cumulative effect of

name: the influence of
usageNote: 强调潜移默化的影响；常与under/exert等搭配
example: The influence of Western consumer culture on developing nations warrants critical examination.
variants: the profound influence of, the far-reaching influence of, under the influence of

name: the consequence of
usageNote: 常暗示负面后果；可与face/suffer/accept等动词搭配
example: Climate change is the direct consequence of decades of unchecked industrial expansion.
variants: the direct consequence of, the unintended consequence of, far-reaching consequences

name: the development of
usageNote: 描述发展过程；可用于科技、经济、个人等各领域
example: The rapid development of artificial intelligence has raised profound ethical questions.
variants: the sustainable development of, the further development of, the historical development of

name: the growth of
usageNote: 常用于经济、人口、组织等领域；可与stimulate/promote/facilitate搭配
example: The growth of the gig economy has fundamentally altered traditional employment relationships.
variants: the rapid growth of, the economic growth of, the exponential growth of

name: the expansion of
usageNote: 强调规模或范围的扩大；常用于商业和地理语境
example: The expansion of online education platforms has democratized access to higher learning.
variants: the rapid expansion of, the continued expansion of, the global expansion of

name: the purpose of
template: the purpose of + N / the purpose of doing
usageNote: 介绍研究目的或行为动机；常与serve/achieve/fulfill搭配
example: The primary purpose of this study is to investigate the correlation between urban green spaces and public wellbeing.
variants: the main purpose of, the sole purpose of, the underlying purpose of

name: the aim of
template: the aim of + N / the aim of doing
usageNote: 常用于研究论文和报告中；比purpose更简洁
example: The aim of this report is to evaluate the effectiveness of current anti-smoking campaigns.
variants: the main aim of, the primary aim of, the ultimate aim of

name: the objective of
template: the objective of + N / the objective of doing
usageNote: 非常正式的表达；常用于商业计划和研究提案中
example: The objective of this policy initiative is to reduce carbon emissions by 40% within the next decade.
variants: the main objective of, the strategic objective of, the key objective of

name: the process of
usageNote: 描述某事发生或发展的过程；常与in/undergo等搭配
example: The process of globalization has accelerated dramatically since the advent of the internet.
variants: the gradual process of, the ongoing process of, in the process of

name: the method of
usageNote: 常用于学术写作中描述研究方法
example: The method of data collection employed in this study involved semi-structured interviews.
variants: the method of choice, the standard method of, the preferred method of

name: the approach of
usageNote: approach后常接to；可指方法或态度
example: The approach of this research differs from conventional methodologies in several important respects.
variants: the approach to, a holistic approach, a comprehensive approach to

name: the result of
usageNote: 常用于描述因果关系；比outcome更通用
example: The improvement in test scores was the direct result of targeted intervention programs.
variants: the direct result of, the end result of, as a result of

name: the outcome of
usageNote: 强调最终结果；常用于评估和项目中
example: The outcome of the negotiation will have significant implications for international trade relations.
variants: the desired outcome of, the successful outcome of, the eventual outcome of

name: the cause of
usageNote: 用于探讨原因和根源；常与identify/determine/investigate搭配
example: The precise cause of the economic downturn remains a matter of considerable debate among economists.
variants: the root cause of, the underlying cause of, the primary cause of

name: the source of
usageNote: 指事物的出处或起因；常与identify/trace/locate搭配
example: Air pollution has been identified as a major source of respiratory illness in urban populations.
variants: the main source of, the primary source of, a potential source of

name: the solution to
example: There is no simple solution to the complex problem of income inequality in modern societies.
variants: a viable solution to, an effective solution to, the only solution to

name: the key to
usageNote: key后接to（非of）；key可指方法、关键或答案
example: Investing in human capital is widely regarded as the key to sustainable economic development.
variants: the key to success, the key factor in, the key element of

name: the attitude toward
example: Public attitude toward immigration has shifted significantly over the past two decades.
variants: the positive attitude toward, the negative attitude toward, a changing attitude toward

name: the response to
example: The government's response to the housing crisis has been widely criticized as inadequate.
variants: in response to, the initial response to, a positive response to

name: the relationship between
template: the relationship between A and B
usageNote: 雅思写作Task 2和学术写作核心表达；用于探讨两者关系
example: Numerous studies have examined the relationship between socioeconomic status and educational attainment.
variants: the close relationship between, the complex relationship between, the causal relationship between

name: the connection between
template: the connection between A and B
usageNote: 比relationship更通用；可指任何类型的联系
example: There is a clear connection between poor diet and the rising incidence of chronic diseases.
variants: the direct connection between, the obvious connection between, a strong connection between

name: the link between
template: the link between A and B
usageNote: 常暗示因果关系；与establish/demonstrate/find搭配
example: Scientists have established a definitive link between smoking and lung cancer.
variants: a causal link between, a strong link between, the missing link between

name: the difference between
template: the difference between A and B
usageNote: 雅思写作比较类话题核心表达；常与make/tell/spot搭配
example: It is essential to recognize the difference between equality of opportunity and equality of outcome.
variants: the main difference between, a significant difference between, the fundamental difference between

name: the distinction between
template: the distinction between A and B
function: 学术领属：...之间的区别（正式）
usageNote: 比difference更正式；强调清晰的界限或分类
example: The distinction between voluntary and involuntary unemployment has important policy implications.
variants: the clear distinction between, the subtle distinction between, make a distinction between

name: the contrast between
template: the contrast between A and B
usageNote: 强调鲜明差异；常用于比较论证中
example: The contrast between urban prosperity and rural poverty has become increasingly pronounced.
variants: the sharp contrast between, the striking contrast between, in contrast to

name: the balance between
template: the balance between A and B
usageNote: 雅思写作常见话题；常与achieve/strike/maintain搭配
example: Achieving the balance between economic development and environmental protection remains a formidable challenge.
variants: the delicate balance between, the right balance between, strike a balance between

name: the conflict between
template: the conflict between A and B
usageNote: 描述利益、价值观或目标之间的对立
example: The conflict between short-term economic gains and long-term sustainability is a recurring theme in policy debates.
variants: the inherent conflict between, the potential conflict between, resolve the conflict between

name: a friend of mine
template: a N of + 物主代词
function: 学术双重所有格：我的一位朋友
usageNote: 双重所有格结构；常用于口语和半正式语境
example: A colleague of mine recently published a groundbreaking paper on behavioral economics.
variants: a colleague of mine, a professor of his, a student of hers

name: air pollution
function: 复合学术名词：空气污染
usageNote: 名词修饰名词结构，前名词起形容词作用；雅思环境类话题核心词汇
example: Air pollution is estimated to cause approximately seven million premature deaths worldwide each year.
variants: water pollution, noise pollution, light pollution, soil pollution

name: climate change
function: 复合学术名词：气候变化
usageNote: 雅思写作最高频话题词之一；常与combat/address/mitigate搭配
example: The scientific consensus on climate change has reached an unprecedented level of certainty.
variants: climate crisis, climate action, climate adaptation

name: global warming
function: 复合学术名词：全球变暖
usageNote: climate change的近义词；常与combat/cause/contribute to搭配
example: The primary cause of global warming is the accumulation of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere.

name: greenhouse effect
function: 复合学术名词：温室效应
usageNote: 固定搭配；常用于解释全球变暖的科学机制
example: The greenhouse effect is a natural phenomenon that has been intensified by human activities.

name: carbon emission
usageNote: 环境类话题高频词；常与reduce/cut/offset搭配
example: Many countries have pledged to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by 2050.
variants: carbon footprint, carbon trading, carbon neutral, carbon tax

name: fossil fuel
function: 复合学术名词：化石燃料
usageNote: 与renewable energy相对；环境话题核心概念
example: The continued reliance on fossil fuels is incompatible with global climate goals.
variants: fossil fuel subsidy, fossil fuel divestment, fossil fuel industry

name: information technology
function: 复合学术名词：信息技术
usageNote: 常缩写为IT；科技话题核心词汇
example: Advances in information technology have fundamentally transformed the nature of work.
variants: information age, information society, information superhighway

name: artificial intelligence
function: 复合学术名词：人工智能
usageNote: 常缩写为AI；雅思写作科技话题高频词
example: The rapid development of artificial intelligence raises fundamental questions about the future of employment.
variants: AI ethics, AI governance, machine intelligence

name: social media
function: 复合学术名词：社交媒体
usageNote: 媒体类话题核心词汇；常与use/influence/impact on搭配
example: The proliferation of social media has profoundly altered patterns of human communication and information consumption.
variants: social network, social platform, social networking site

name: public transport
function: 复合学术名词：公共交通
usageNote: 交通类话题核心词汇；雅思写作常见讨论主题
example: Investment in public transport infrastructure is essential for reducing urban congestion and pollution.
variants: public transportation, public transit, mass transit

name: private sector
function: 复合学术名词：私营部门
usageNote: 经济类话题核心词汇；与public sector相对
example: The private sector has a crucial role to play in driving innovation and creating employment opportunities.
variants: private enterprise, private company, private industry

name: public sector
function: 复合学术名词：公共部门
usageNote: 经济类话题核心词汇；常与government/public service互换
example: The public sector provides essential services that the market alone cannot adequately deliver.
variants: public service, public spending, public investment

name: higher education
function: 复合学术名词：高等教育
usageNote: 教育类话题核心词汇；指大学及以上教育
example: Access to higher education remains unequal across different socioeconomic groups.
variants: higher learning, tertiary education, postsecondary education

name: further education
function: 复合学术名词：继续教育/进修教育
usageNote: 英式英语常用；指中学毕业后非大学的教育
example: Further education colleges provide valuable vocational training for young people entering the workforce.
variants: adult education, lifelong education, continuing education

name: vocational training
function: 复合学术名词：职业培训
usageNote: 教育类话题高频词；强调实用技能培养
example: Vocational training programs should be strengthened to address the growing skills shortage in technical industries.
variants: vocational education, skills training, job training

name: distance learning
function: 复合学术名词：远程教育
usageNote: 近年高频话题；与online education密切相关
example: The COVID-19 pandemic accelerated the adoption of distance learning across all levels of education.
variants: online learning, remote learning, e-learning, virtual learning

name: job satisfaction
function: 复合学术名词：工作满意度
usageNote: 职场类话题核心词汇；常与level/degree of搭配
example: Research indicates a strong correlation between job satisfaction and overall productivity.
variants: career satisfaction, work fulfillment, employee satisfaction

name: career development
function: 复合学术名词：职业发展
usageNote: 职场类话题高频词；强调职业成长路径
example: Organizations that invest in employee career development tend to experience lower staff turnover rates.
variants: career progression, career advancement, professional development

name: work experience
function: 复合学术名词：工作经验
usageNote: 求职和职场话题核心词汇；常与gain/have/lack搭配
example: Relevant work experience is increasingly valued by employers alongside formal academic qualifications.
variants: practical experience, hands-on experience, professional experience

name: labor market
function: 复合学术名词：劳动力市场
usageNote: 经济类话题核心词汇；雅思写作Task 2高频词
example: The labor market has undergone significant transformation due to automation and globalization.
variants: job market, employment market, workforce market

name: living standard
function: 复合学术名词：生活水平
usageNote: 社会类话题核心词汇；复数为living standards
example: Economic growth does not automatically translate into improved living standards for all citizens.
variants: standard of living, quality of life, living conditions

name: quality of life
function: 复合学术名词：生活质量
usageNote: 社会类话题高频词；常与improve/enhance/measure搭配
example: Urban planners must prioritize quality of life considerations in designing sustainable cities.
variants: standard of living, wellbeing, life satisfaction

name: life expectancy
function: 复合学术名词：预期寿命
usageNote: 健康和社会类话题核心词汇
example: Life expectancy has increased dramatically in most parts of the world over the past century.
variants: average life expectancy, life span, longevity

name: birth rate
usageNote: 人口类话题核心词汇；常与declining/falling/low搭配
example: The declining birth rate in many developed nations poses significant challenges for future pension systems.
variants: fertility rate, mortality rate, death rate

name: health care
function: 复合学术名词：医疗保健
usageNote: 健康类话题最高频词之一；美式写作healthcare（一个词）
example: Access to affordable health care remains a pressing concern for policymakers worldwide.
variants: healthcare system, health care reform, medical care

name: mental health
function: 复合学术名词：心理健康
usageNote: 近年高频话题；常与awareness/stigma/support搭配
example: Mental health issues among young people have reached alarming proportions in recent years.
variants: mental wellbeing, mental illness, psychological health

name: crime rate
usageNote: 犯罪类话题核心词汇；常与high/low/rising/falling搭配
example: There is evidence to suggest that the crime rate is not solely determined by economic factors.
variants: violent crime rate, property crime rate, offense rate

name: human rights
usageNote: 法律和社会类话题核心词汇；常与violate/protect/defend搭配
example: The protection of fundamental human rights is a cornerstone of any democratic society.
variants: civil rights, individual rights, equal rights

name: economic growth
function: 复合学术名词：经济增长
usageNote: 经济类话题最高频词；常与stimulate/promote/sustainable搭配
example: Sustained economic growth requires continuous investment in education, infrastructure, and innovation.
variants: GDP growth, economic development, economic expansion

name: financial crisis
function: 复合学术名词：金融危机
usageNote: 经济类话题高频词；常与cause/trigger/recover from搭配
example: The global financial crisis of 2008 exposed the vulnerabilities of unregulated financial markets.
variants: economic crisis, banking crisis, debt crisis

name: market economy
function: 复合学术名词：市场经济
usageNote: 经济类话题核心概念；常与free/open/transition to搭配
example: China's transition to a market economy has lifted hundreds of millions of people out of poverty.
variants: free market economy, planned economy, mixed economy

name: free trade
function: 复合学术名词：自由贸易
usageNote: 经济类话题高频词；常与agreement/zone/promote搭配
example: Proponents of free trade argue that it leads to greater efficiency and lower consumer prices.
variants: fair trade, trade liberalization, open trade

name: urban planning
function: 复合学术名词：城市规划
example: Effective urban planning is essential for creating livable, sustainable, and inclusive cities.
variants: city planning, town planning, urban design

name: rural area
function: 复合学术名词：农村地区
usageNote: 城乡对比话题核心词汇；与urban area相对
example: Young people in rural areas often face limited educational and employment opportunities.

name: developing country
function: 复合学术名词：发展中国家
usageNote: 雅思写作高频词；注意与developed country的对比
example: Many developing countries struggle to balance economic modernization with environmental sustainability.
variants: developed country, emerging economy, low-income country, Third World country

name: developed country
function: 复合学术名词：发达国家
usageNote: 雅思写作高频词；常与developing country对比讨论
example: Developed countries bear a disproportionate responsibility for historical greenhouse gas emissions.
variants: industrialized country, advanced economy, high-income country

name: mother tongue
usageNote: 语言类话题核心词汇；较mother language更常用
example: Children who learn in their mother tongue tend to achieve better academic outcomes overall.
variants: first language, native language, native tongue

name: second language
function: 复合学术名词：第二语言
usageNote: 语言教育类话题高频词
example: The acquisition of a second language provides significant cognitive and professional advantages.
variants: foreign language, additional language, target language

name: body language
function: 复合学术名词：肢体语言
usageNote: 沟通和心理学话题词汇
example: Effective communicators understand the importance of body language in conveying meaning and building rapport.
variants: non-verbal communication, physical gestures, facial expressions

name: eye contact
function: 复合学术名词：眼神交流
usageNote: 沟通类话题词汇；常与maintain/avoid/establish搭配
example: Maintaining appropriate eye contact is essential for effective interpersonal communication.
variants: visual contact, direct gaze, eye movement

name: facial expression
function: 复合学术名词：面部表情
usageNote: 沟通和心理类话题词汇
example: Facial expressions play a crucial role in conveying emotions and facilitating social interaction.
variants: facial gesture, facial cue, emotional expression

name: verbal communication
function: 复合学术名词：口头交流
usageNote: 与non-verbal communication相对；沟通类话题核心词
example: While verbal communication transmits explicit information, non-verbal cues often convey deeper meaning.
variants: oral communication, spoken communication, linguistic communication

name: non-verbal communication
function: 复合学术名词：非言语交流
usageNote: 沟通类话题核心词汇；常与body language互换使用
example: Non-verbal communication accounts for a significant proportion of the meaning conveyed in face-to-face interaction.

name: renewable energy
function: 复合学术名词：可再生能源
usageNote: 环境类话题最高频词之一；常与invest in/develop/transition搭配
example: The transition to renewable energy sources is essential for mitigating the impacts of climate change.
variants: clean energy, green energy, sustainable energy

name: solar power
usageNote: 可再生能源话题具体词汇
example: The cost of solar power has decreased dramatically, making it competitive with fossil fuels in many regions.
variants: solar energy, solar panel, photovoltaic power

name: wind power
usageNote: 可再生能源话题具体词汇
example: Wind power has emerged as one of the most cost-effective sources of renewable electricity.
variants: wind energy, wind turbine, offshore wind

name: nuclear energy
usageNote: 能源话题争议性词汇；常与debate/controversy/safety搭配
example: The use of nuclear energy remains a contentious issue, with proponents citing its low carbon emissions and opponents emphasizing safety concerns.
variants: nuclear power, atomic energy, nuclear fission

name: natural resource
function: 复合学术名词：自然资源
usageNote: 环境和经济类话题核心词汇
example: The depletion of natural resources poses a long-term threat to global economic stability.
variants: natural capital, mineral resources, renewable resources

name: data analysis
function: 复合学术名词：数据分析
usageNote: 科技和研究类话题核心词汇
example: Advances in data analysis techniques have enabled researchers to identify patterns that were previously undetectable.
variants: statistical analysis, data mining, big data analytics

name: research method
function: 复合学术名词：研究方法
usageNote: 学术写作核心词汇；常与employ/use/adopt搭配
example: The choice of research method depends on the nature of the research question and the available resources.
variants: research methodology, scientific method, empirical method

name: case study
function: 复合学术名词：案例研究
usageNote: 学术写作和研究类话题词汇
example: The case study approach allows for in-depth examination of complex phenomena within their real-life context.
variants: detailed case study, comparative case study, longitudinal case study

name: government policy
function: 复合学术名词：政府政策
usageNote: 社会和政治类话题核心词汇
example: Government policy should be evidence-based and subject to rigorous evaluation of its effectiveness.
variants: public policy, fiscal policy, monetary policy, social policy

name: public opinion
function: 复合学术名词：公众舆论
usageNote: 媒体和政治类话题核心词汇
example: Public opinion on controversial issues can shift significantly in response to media coverage and political discourse.
variants: popular opinion, mass opinion, public sentiment

name: social welfare
function: 复合学术名词：社会福利
example: A comprehensive social welfare system is essential for protecting the most vulnerable members of society.
variants: welfare state, welfare system, welfare reform, social security

name: national security
function: 复合学术名词：国家安全
usageNote: 政治和法律类话题词汇
example: The tension between national security concerns and individual privacy rights is a defining challenge of the digital age.
variants: homeland security, state security, collective security

name: cultural heritage
function: 复合学术名词：文化遗产
example: The preservation of cultural heritage is essential for maintaining a sense of identity and continuity within communities.
variants: national heritage, world heritage, intangible cultural heritage

name: traditional values
function: 复合学术名词：传统价值观
usageNote: 文化和社会变迁类话题词汇
example: The clash between traditional values and modern lifestyles is a recurring theme in discussions of social change.
variants: core values, family values, moral values, shared values

name: modern society
function: 复合学术名词：现代社会
usageNote: 社会类话题高频词；常与in/contemporary/today's搭配
example: Modern society is characterized by rapid technological change and increasing cultural diversity.
variants: contemporary society, today's society, advanced society

name: ancient civilization
function: 复合学术名词：古代文明
usageNote: 历史和文化类话题词汇
example: The study of ancient civilizations provides valuable insights into the development of human culture and social organization.
variants: early civilization, lost civilization, great civilization

name: research into
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的研究
usageNote: into强调深入研究的过程；可与carry out/conduct/do搭配
example: Further research into the long-term effects of social media use on adolescent development is urgently needed.
variants: research on, research in, do research into

name: study of
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的研究
usageNote: of表示研究的对象或范围；可与carry out/undertake/conduct搭配
example: This paper presents a comprehensive study of migration patterns in Southeast Asia over the past fifty years.
variants: study on, study into, empirical study of

name: analysis of
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的分析
usageNote: 学术写作最高频表达之一；常与present/provide/carry out搭配
example: The analysis of survey data revealed significant disparities in educational attainment across socioeconomic groups.
variants: detailed analysis of, in-depth analysis of, statistical analysis of

name: investigation into
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的调查
usageNote: into强调深入调查；常用于正式和学术语境
example: The police have launched a thorough investigation into allegations of corporate fraud.
variants: investigation of, thorough investigation into, criminal investigation into

name: demand for
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的需求
usageNote: demand为不可数名词；常与high/low/increase/decrease in搭配
example: There is growing demand for professionals with expertise in data science and artificial intelligence.
variants: high demand for, low demand for, meet the demand for

name: need for
template: need for + N / need for sb to do
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的需要
usageNote: need可作可数或不可数名词；常与there is/address/meet搭配
example: There is an urgent need for comprehensive immigration reform that addresses both economic and humanitarian concerns.
variants: the urgent need for, a pressing need for, meet the need for

name: requirement for
template: requirement for + N / requirement for sb to do
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的要求
example: The requirement for all new buildings to meet energy efficiency standards has driven innovation in sustainable construction.
variants: a strict requirement for, a minimum requirement for, meet the requirement for

name: reason for
example: The main reason for the decline in manufacturing employment is the automation of production processes.
variants: the main reason for, the primary reason for, the underlying reason for

name: cause of
usageNote: cause后接of（非for）；强调因果关系的起点
example: Smoking is the leading preventable cause of death worldwide.
variants: the root cause of, the underlying cause of, the primary cause of

name: solution to
function: 学术名词+介词：...的解决方案
example: There is no single solution to the complex problem of urban homelessness.
variants: a viable solution to, an effective solution to, a long-term solution to

name: answer to
function: 学术名词+介词：...的答案/解决办法
example: Technology alone is not the answer to the environmental challenges facing humanity.
variants: the answer to the problem, a partial answer to, the only answer to

name: response to
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的回应
usageNote: 常与make/give/in搭配；in response to为常见状语结构
example: The government's response to the economic crisis was widely criticized as being too slow and inadequate.
variants: in response to, the initial response to, an appropriate response to

name: reaction to
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的反应
usageNote: 强调即时或情感反应；常与have/provoke/trigger搭配
example: Public reaction to the proposed tax increases was overwhelmingly negative.
variants: a positive reaction to, an adverse reaction to, a knee-jerk reaction to

name: attitude to/toward
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的态度
usageNote: 英式英语多用attitude to，美式多用toward(s)；常与have/adopt/display搭配
example: Attitudes toward remote working have changed dramatically since the pandemic.
variants: a positive attitude to, a negative attitude toward, a changing attitude to

name: approach to
usageNote: approach后接to（非of/for）；后可接名词或动名词
example: The approach to teaching foreign languages has evolved significantly with the integration of digital technology.
variants: a novel approach to, a holistic approach to, the traditional approach to

name: access to
function: 学术名词+介词：获取...的途径
usageNote: access为不可数名词；常与have/gain/provide/deny搭配
example: Universal access to quality education is widely recognized as a fundamental human right.
variants: equal access to, easy access to, gain access to, restricted access to

name: effect on
template: effect on + N / effect of N on N
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的影响
usageNote: effect后接on（影响的对象）；have an effect on为常见搭配
example: The effect of social media on mental health has become a major focus of public health research.
variants: a significant effect on, a profound effect on, the cumulative effect on

name: impact on
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的冲击
usageNote: impact可作名词或动词；have/make an impact on为高频搭配
example: The COVID-19 pandemic had a devastating impact on the global tourism industry.
variants: a significant impact on, a negative impact on, the full impact on

name: influence on
template: influence on + N / influence over + N
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的影响力
usageNote: influence后接on（影响对象）或over（控制范围）
example: Parents continue to exert considerable influence on their children's educational and career choices.
variants: a profound influence on, a major influence on, exercise influence on

name: role in
function: 学术名词+介词：在...中的角色
usageNote: 常与play/have/assume/fulfill搭配；后接动名词或名词
example: Technology plays an increasingly important role in shaping modern educational practices.
variants: a key role in, a pivotal role in, the central role in

name: part in
function: 学术名词+介词：在...中的部分/角色
usageNote: 常与play/have/take搭配；take part in为常见短语
example: Citizens have a crucial part to play in holding their elected representatives accountable.
variants: play a part in, have a part in, an active part in

name: increase in
usageNote: increase后接in（增长的领域）；an increase为名词用法
example: There has been a marked increase in the number of students choosing to study abroad in recent years.
variants: a significant increase in, a dramatic increase in, a steady increase in

name: decrease in
example: The decrease in biodiversity poses a serious threat to the stability of ecosystems worldwide.
variants: a sharp decrease in, a gradual decrease in, a steady decrease in

name: rise in
usageNote: rise为不规则动词rise的名词形式；常与sharp/dramatic/steady搭配
example: The sharp rise in energy prices has put significant strain on household budgets.
variants: a sharp rise in, a steady rise in, a dramatic rise in, give rise to

name: fall in
usageNote: fall为不规则动词fall的名词形式；英式英语中更常用
example: The fall in unemployment rates suggests that the economy is recovering from the recession.
variants: a sharp fall in, a steep fall in, a dramatic fall in

name: drop in
usageNote: drop强调突然的下降；比fall/decrease更口语化
example: The sudden drop in consumer confidence caught many economists by surprise.
variants: a sharp drop in, a sudden drop in, a significant drop in

name: shortage of
example: Many developing countries face a critical shortage of qualified healthcare professionals.
variants: a severe shortage of, a critical shortage of, a chronic shortage of

name: lack of
usageNote: lack可作名词或动词；lack of后接名词表示'缺乏'
example: The lack of affordable housing is one of the most pressing challenges facing urban populations.
variants: a serious lack of, a complete lack of, a general lack of

name: absence of
function: 学术名词+介词：...的缺失（正式）
usageNote: 比lack更正式；常与in the absence of搭配
example: In the absence of concrete evidence, it would be premature to draw definitive conclusions.
variants: the complete absence of, the total absence of, in the absence of

name: surplus of
example: The surplus of agricultural produce in developed countries contrasts sharply with food shortages in other regions.
variants: a surplus of, an excess of, a glut of

name: respect for
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的尊重
usageNote: 常与have/show/command/earn搭配；show respect for为高频短语
example: A successful multicultural society requires mutual respect for diverse cultural traditions and values.
variants: show respect for, have respect for, command respect for, earn respect for

name: admiration for
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的钦佩
usageNote: 比respect更强调赞赏和敬佩；常与have/express/deep搭配
example: There is widespread public admiration for the dedication and sacrifice of healthcare workers during the pandemic.
variants: deep admiration for, express admiration for, have great admiration for

name: sympathy for
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的同情
usageNote: 常与have/feel/express/show搭配；in sympathy with为常用短语
example: While there is considerable sympathy for refugees, public attitudes toward immigration policy remain divided.
variants: have sympathy for, feel sympathy for, express sympathy for

name: the prevalence of
function: 学术名词+介词：...的流行/ prevalence
usageNote: 常用于学术和医学语境；描述某现象的普遍程度
example: The prevalence of obesity among children has become a major public health concern in many industrialized nations.
variants: the high prevalence of, the increasing prevalence of, the widespread prevalence of

name: the prevalence of social media
template: the prevalence of + N in + N context
function: 学术名词+介词：...在特定语境中的普遍存在
usageNote: 描述某事物在特定环境中的普遍程度；学术写作中常用结构
example: The prevalence of smartphones in educational settings has sparked debate about their impact on student learning.
variants: the prevalence of X among Y, the growing prevalence of, the alarming prevalence of

name: more important than
template: more + 多音节形容词 + than
usageNote: 用于多音节形容词比较级；雅思写作比较论证核心表达
example: Environmental protection is arguably more important than short-term economic growth.
variants: more significant than, more crucial than, more vital than

name: the most important
template: the most + 多音节形容词
usageNote: 用于多音节形容词最高级；常与factor/aspect/reason搭配
example: Education is widely regarded as the most important factor in determining social mobility.
variants: the most significant, the most crucial, the most essential

name: more significant than
usageNote: significant强调统计学意义上的显著性；学术写作高频
example: The long-term economic benefits of investing in education are more significant than the initial costs.
variants: more important than, more considerable than, more substantial than

name: the most significant
usageNote: 强调最重要或最显著的；常与difference/challenge/factor搭配
example: Climate change represents the most significant challenge facing humanity in the twenty-first century.
variants: the most important, the most critical, the most far-reaching

name: more effective than
usageNote: effectiveness比较；常用于评估和对比方法/政策
example: Preventive healthcare measures have proven to be more effective than curative interventions in reducing overall mortality.
variants: more efficient than, more successful than, more productive than

name: the most effective
usageNote: 常用于总结论证；the most effective way/method/approach为高频搭配
example: Investing in early childhood education is widely considered the most effective way to reduce long-term social inequality.
variants: the most efficient, the best, the most successful

name: more efficient than
usageNote: efficiency强调资源利用效率；经济和技术类话题常用
example: Renewable energy sources are becoming more efficient than fossil fuels in terms of cost per unit of electricity.
variants: more effective than, more productive than, more economical than

name: the most efficient
usageNote: 强调最优的资源利用效率
example: Market-based mechanisms are often regarded as the most efficient means of allocating scarce resources.
variants: the most effective, the most productive, the most streamlined

name: increasingly important
function: 程度递进：越来越重要的
usageNote: increasingly替代more and more；更学术化；雅思写作高分表达
example: Digital literacy has become increasingly important in the modern workplace.
variants: increasingly significant, increasingly crucial, increasingly vital

name: increasingly common
function: 程度递进：越来越普遍的
usageNote: 描述趋势变化；雅思Task 1和Task 2均适用
example: Remote working arrangements have become increasingly common since the global pandemic.
variants: increasingly prevalent, increasingly widespread, increasingly frequent

name: increasingly popular
function: 程度递进：越来越受欢迎的
example: Plant-based diets have become increasingly popular among younger generations concerned about environmental sustainability.
variants: increasingly fashionable, increasingly trendy, increasingly favored

name: increasingly difficult
function: 程度递进：越来越困难的
usageNote: 描述难度递增；雅思写作论证可用
example: It is becoming increasingly difficult for young people to afford home ownership in major metropolitan areas.
variants: increasingly challenging, increasingly hard, increasingly problematic

name: less important than
function: 学术比较级（降）：不如...重要
usageNote: less + 原级 + than为降级比较结构；常用于权衡论证
example: While extracurricular activities are valuable, they are less important than academic achievement in university admissions.
variants: less significant than, less crucial than, less vital than

name: the least important
function: 学术最高级（降）：最不重要的
usageNote: the least + 原级为降级最高级；用于排序论证
example: Of all the factors contributing to job satisfaction, salary is often considered the least important.
variants: the least significant, the least crucial, the least relevant

name: far more complex
template: 程度副词 + more + 形容词
example: The issue of immigration is far more complex than simplistic media narratives often suggest.
variants: much more complex, significantly more complex, considerably more complex

name: much more difficult
template: 程度副词 + more + 形容词
usageNote: much/far/considerably修饰比较级；比simply more语气更强
example: Addressing climate change is much more difficult than merely setting ambitious emission targets.
variants: far more difficult, considerably more difficult, substantially more difficult

name: significantly more expensive
template: 程度副词 + more + 形容词
function: 强化比较级：显著更昂贵
usageNote: significantly强调统计学意义上的显著差异
example: Healthcare in the United States is significantly more expensive than in any other developed nation.
variants: considerably more expensive, much more expensive, far more expensive

name: slightly better
usageNote: slightly/marginally/a little弱化比较级程度；学术写作中更客观
example: The new teaching method produced slightly better results than the traditional approach.
variants: marginally better, somewhat better, a little better

name: marginally higher
usageNote: marginally强调差异微小；学术写作中用于精确描述
example: Test scores were marginally higher among students who received additional tutoring support.
variants: slightly higher, somewhat higher, fractionally higher

name: considerably lower
usageNote: considerably强调显著差异；常与lower/higher/fewer搭配
example: Unemployment rates in Northern Europe are considerably lower than in Southern Europe.
variants: significantly lower, substantially lower, markedly lower

name: as important as
usageNote: as...as结构表示同等程度；否定用not as...as或not so...as
example: Access to healthcare is just as important as access to education for social development.
variants: as significant as, as crucial as, as vital as

name: not as significant as
function: 平级比较（否定）：不如...重要
usageNote: not as/so + 原级 + as为否定平级比较；常用于让步论证
example: While technology is important, it is not as significant as human capital in driving economic growth.
variants: not as important as, not as crucial as, not as vital as

name: the more...the more...
template: the + 比较级, the + 比较级
usageNote: 表示两个变化成正比；雅思写作高分句型
example: The more governments invest in public education, the more competitive their economies become in the long run.
variants: the more...the better, the less...the more..., the harder...the more...

name: prefer A to B
template: prefer + N + to + N / prefer + V-ing + to + V-ing
usageNote: prefer...to结构；to为介词，后接名词或动名词
example: Many young people now prefer working remotely to commuting to a traditional office.
variants: prefer A rather than B, would prefer to...rather than...

name: would rather...than...
usageNote: than后接动词原形；常用于口语和半正式语境
example: Most employees would rather have flexible working hours than a higher salary.
variants: would sooner...than..., would just as soon...as...

name: extremely important
usageNote: extremely为程度副词最高级；雅思写作中用于强调
example: It is extremely important that policymakers take immediate action to address the housing crisis.
variants: extremely significant, extremely crucial, extremely necessary

name: highly significant
example: The correlation between socioeconomic status and educational attainment is highly significant.
variants: highly important, highly effective, highly recommended, highly unlikely

name: greatly beneficial
example: Regular physical exercise is greatly beneficial to both physical and mental health.
variants: highly beneficial, extremely beneficial, enormously beneficial

name: deeply concerned
example: Environmental scientists are deeply concerned about the accelerating rate of ice sheet loss in Antarctica.
variants: deeply worried, deeply troubled, deeply disturbed

name: seriously affected
function: 程度修饰：严重受影响的
example: Coastal communities are seriously affected by rising sea levels and increasingly frequent storm surges.
variants: badly affected, severely affected, profoundly affected

name: severely damaged
example: The region's agricultural infrastructure was severely damaged by the prolonged drought.
variants: badly damaged, extensively damaged, irreparably damaged

name: widely accepted
example: It is widely accepted that early childhood experiences have a profound impact on adult personality development.
variants: broadly accepted, generally accepted, universally accepted

name: broadly supported
example: The proposed reforms are broadly supported across the political spectrum.
variants: widely supported, generally supported, overwhelmingly supported

name: generally agreed
example: It is generally agreed that education is the most powerful tool for reducing social inequality.
variants: widely agreed, broadly agreed, universally agreed

name: totally different
function: 程度修饰（极端）：完全不同的
example: The outcomes of the two policy approaches were totally different from what had been predicted.
variants: completely different, entirely different, fundamentally different

name: completely wrong
function: 程度修饰（极端）：完全错误的
example: The assumption that technology alone can solve educational problems is completely wrong.
variants: entirely wrong, totally wrong, utterly wrong

name: absolutely essential
function: 程度修饰（极端）：绝对必要的
example: Adequate sleep is absolutely essential for maintaining cognitive function and emotional wellbeing.
variants: absolutely crucial, absolutely vital, absolutely necessary

name: relatively small
function: 程度修饰（相对性）：相对较小的
usageNote: relatively强调相对性；常用于对比论证中
example: While the initial cost may seem high, it is relatively small when compared to the long-term benefits.
variants: relatively low, relatively minor, relatively insignificant

name: comparatively low
function: 程度修饰（相对性）：比较低的
usageNote: comparatively与relatively近义；学术写作中可互换
example: Crime rates in this region are comparatively low compared to national averages.
variants: comparatively small, comparatively minor, comparatively insignificant

name: fairly good
function: 程度修饰（委婉）：相当不错的
usageNote: fairly程度适中，偏正面但不过分强烈；常被视为弱修饰
example: The results of the experiment were fairly good, though there is still room for improvement.
variants: fairly well, fairly common, fairly typical, fairly straightforward

name: quite common
function: 程度修饰（英式）：相当普遍的
usageNote: 英式英语中quite程度较强；美式英语中较弱；注意语境差异
example: It is quite common for students to experience anxiety before important examinations.
variants: quite normal, quite typical, quite understandable, quite remarkable

name: rather unusual
function: 程度修饰（英式）：颇不寻常的
usageNote: rather偏英式用法；常用于形容词前加强语气
example: It is rather unusual for such a young company to achieve such remarkable market success.
