variants: rather surprising, rather disappointing, rather concerning, rather unexpected

name: pretty good
function: 程度修饰（口语化）：相当好的
usageNote: pretty为非正式程度副词；雅思口语常用，写作中避免
example: The presentation went pretty well, and we got positive feedback from the panel.
variants: pretty well, pretty common, pretty much, pretty close

name: particularly relevant
function: 程度修饰（特指）：特别相关的
example: This finding is particularly relevant to policymakers concerned with urban transportation planning.
variants: especially relevant, highly relevant, extremely relevant

name: especially important
function: 程度修饰（特指）：特别重要的
usageNote: especially与particularly近义；常用于引出重点
example: It is especially important to consider the needs of disadvantaged communities when designing public services.
variants: particularly important, vitally important, crucially important

name: specifically designed
function: 程度修饰（特指）：专门设计的
example: The course was specifically designed to meet the needs of non-native English speakers.
variants: specially designed, expressly designed, exclusively designed

name: increasingly aware
function: 程度递进：越来越意识到
usageNote: increasingly + 形容词结构；描述渐进变化
example: Consumers are becoming increasingly aware of the environmental impact of their purchasing decisions.
variants: increasingly conscious, increasingly mindful, increasingly cognizant

name: increasingly difficult
usageNote: 雅思写作趋势描述高分表达
example: It is becoming increasingly difficult to find affordable housing in major metropolitan areas.
variants: increasingly challenging, increasingly problematic, increasingly complex

name: potentially dangerous
function: 程度修饰（可能性）：潜在危险的
example: The long-term use of certain medications may be potentially dangerous if not properly monitored.
variants: potentially harmful, potentially beneficial, potentially serious, potentially lethal

name: potentially harmful
function: 程度修饰（可能性）：潜在有害的
usageNote: 常用于风险评估和健康话题
example: Excessive screen time can be potentially harmful to children's physical and cognitive development.
variants: potentially damaging, potentially detrimental, potentially risky

name: potentially beneficial
function: 程度修饰（可能性）：潜在有益的
usageNote: 常用于论证某事物的积极潜力
example: Further research into this area could be potentially beneficial for developing new treatments.
variants: potentially advantageous, potentially helpful, potentially valuable

name: undoubtedly true
function: 程度修饰（确定性）：无疑真实的
example: It is undoubtedly true that education is fundamental to individual and societal development.
variants: undoubtedly correct, undoubtedly important, undoubtedly significant

name: certainly possible
function: 程度修饰（确定性）：当然可能的
usageNote: certainly表示确定性；常用于肯定回应或强调
example: It is certainly possible that technological advances will transform the nature of work within the next decade.
variants: definitely possible, quite possible, entirely possible

name: definitely necessary
function: 程度修饰（确定性）：肯定必要的
usageNote: definitely语气最强；口语中比学术写作更常见
example: Stricter regulations on industrial pollution are definitely necessary to protect public health.
variants: absolutely necessary, certainly necessary, unquestionably necessary

name: arguably the best
function: 程度修饰（可论证性）：可以说是最好的
usageNote: arguably为学术写作特色副词；表示有论据支持但承认可能有争议
example: This is arguably the most comprehensive study on the subject conducted to date.
variants: arguably the most, arguably the greatest, arguably the most important

name: probably the most
function: 程度修饰（可能性）：可能是最...的
usageNote: probably表示高概率但非确定性；比possibly/likely语气更强
example: Technology is probably the most significant driver of social change in the modern era.
variants: possibly the most, likely the most, almost certainly the most

name: possibly the least
function: 程度修饰（可能性）：可能是最不...的
usageNote: possibly表示较低确定性；常用于委婉表达
example: Of all the proposed solutions, this is possibly the least effective and most costly.
variants: probably the least, arguably the least, perhaps the least

name: seemingly impossible
function: 程度修饰（表象）：看似不可能的
usageNote: seemingly强调表面现象与实际情况的差异
example: What once seemed seemingly impossible has now become reality through scientific innovation.
variants: seemingly straightforward, seemingly obvious, paradoxically seemingly

name: apparently obvious
function: 程度修饰（表象）：明显显而易见的
usageNote: apparently表示从表面上看；可能暗示实际情况不同
example: While the solution may appear apparently obvious, its implementation presents significant practical challenges.
variants: apparently simple, apparently clear, apparently straightforward

name: presumably correct
function: 程度修饰（推测）：推测正确的
usageNote: presumably表示基于推理的假设；比probably更正式
example: The conclusions drawn from the data are presumably correct, pending further verification.
variants: presumably accurate, presumably true, presumably valid

name: aware of
usageNote: 雅思写作高频搭配；常与be/become/make sb搭配
example: It is crucial that citizens are aware of their rights and responsibilities in a democratic society.
variants: be aware of, become aware of, make sb aware of, fully aware of

name: conscious of
function: 形容词+介词：意识到...（正式）
example: Organizations must be conscious of their environmental obligations when making business decisions.
variants: be conscious of, become conscious of, self-conscious about

name: mindful of
usageNote: 比aware更强调主动关注；常与be/remain/stay搭配
example: Policymakers should be mindful of the potential unintended consequences of regulatory intervention.
variants: be mindful of, remain mindful of, stay mindful of

name: capable of
usageNote: capable强调内在能力；与able to可互换但有细微差别
example: Human beings are capable of extraordinary feats of creativity and innovation when given the right conditions.
variants: be capable of, prove capable of, quite capable of

name: incapable of
function: 形容词+介词：没有能力做...
usageNote: capable的否定形式；比unable to更正式
example: Some individuals seem incapable of understanding perspectives that differ from their own.
variants: be incapable of, prove incapable of, quite incapable of

name: able to
usageNote: able后接不定式（非of）；比can更正式
example: Technology has enabled us to communicate instantaneously across vast distances.
variants: be able to, unable to, better able to

name: unable to
example: Many young people are unable to afford the high cost of higher education without financial assistance.
variants: be unable to, find oneself unable to, remain unable to

name: responsible for
usageNote: 常与be/feel/hold搭配；后可接结果（表原因）
example: Human activity is primarily responsible for the observed increase in global temperatures.
variants: be responsible for, hold sb responsible for, feel responsible for

name: accountable for
function: 形容词+介词：对...负有责任的（正式）
example: Elected officials must be held accountable for their policy decisions and their consequences.
variants: hold sb accountable for, be accountable for, make sb accountable for

name: essential to
function: 形容词+介词：对...至关重要
example: Access to clean water is absolutely essential to maintaining public health and preventing disease.
variants: essential to/for, absolutely essential, vitally essential

name: vital to
function: 形容词+介词：对...至关重要的
usageNote: 与essential/crucial/critical近义；常用于正式论证
example: Foreign investment is vital to the economic development of many emerging markets.
variants: vital to/for, absolutely vital, crucially vital

name: crucial to
usageNote: crucial强调关键性和决定性；与key同义
example: Effective communication skills are crucial to success in virtually all professional fields.
variants: crucial to/for, absolutely crucial, vitally crucial

name: critical to
usageNote: critical可作关键的或批判的；此处为前者
example: Adequate funding is critical to ensuring the long-term viability of scientific research programs.
variants: critical to/for, absolutely critical, vitally critical

name: beneficial to
example: Regular physical exercise is universally acknowledged as beneficial to both body and mind.
variants: highly beneficial to, mutually beneficial, prove beneficial to

name: advantageous to
function: 形容词+介词：对...有利的（正式）
example: A bilingual education can be highly advantageous to students in an increasingly globalized job market.
variants: highly advantageous to, prove advantageous, mutually advantageous

name: harmful to
example: Prolonged exposure to air pollution is demonstrably harmful to respiratory health.
variants: extremely harmful to, potentially harmful to, prove harmful to

name: detrimental to
function: 形容词+介词：对...有害的（正式）
usageNote: 比harmful更学术化；常用于正式论证中
example: Excessive reliance on standardized testing may be detrimental to the development of critical thinking skills.
variants: highly detrimental to, potentially detrimental to, prove detrimental to

name: relevant to
usageNote: relevant后接to；与irrelevant to相对；学术写作高频
example: The research findings are directly relevant to ongoing policy debates about healthcare reform.
variants: directly relevant to, highly relevant to, particularly relevant to

name: applicable to
usageNote: 常与be/prove/remains搭配；后接适用范围
example: While this model works well in theory, it may not be directly applicable to real-world situations.
variants: directly applicable to, widely applicable, universally applicable to

name: pertinent to
function: 形容词+介词：与... pertinent（正式）
usageNote: 比relevant更正式；常用于学术和专业语境
example: Several questions were raised that are highly pertinent to the ongoing investigation.
variants: highly pertinent to, directly pertinent to, particularly pertinent to

name: similar to
example: The educational system in Finland is similar to that in other Nordic countries in several respects.
variants: very similar to, remarkably similar to, broadly similar to

name: comparable to
function: 形容词+介词：与...可比较的
example: The economic challenges facing developing countries today are comparable to those encountered by industrialized nations in the nineteenth century.
variants: broadly comparable to, directly comparable to, roughly comparable to

name: equivalent to
example: A master's degree from this institution is generally considered equivalent to degrees from leading international universities.
variants: broadly equivalent to, directly equivalent to, roughly equivalent to

name: different from
usageNote: 英式英语用different from/to，美式用different from/than
example: The organizational culture of the public sector is fundamentally different from that of the private sector.
variants: very different from, fundamentally different from, markedly different from

name: distinct from
function: 形容词+介词：与...有区别的（正式）
usageNote: 比different更正式；强调清晰的区分
example: Learning a second language is a process distinct from, though related to, first language acquisition.
variants: clearly distinct from, entirely distinct from, fundamentally distinct from

name: dependent on
usageNote: dependent后接on或upon；后接名词或动名词
example: The agricultural sector in many regions is heavily dependent on seasonal rainfall patterns.
variants: heavily dependent on, largely dependent on, wholly dependent on

name: reliant on
example: Modern economies have become increasingly reliant on digital infrastructure for their day-to-day functioning.
variants: heavily reliant on, increasingly reliant on, overly reliant on

name: contingent on
function: 形容词+介词：取决于...的（正式）
usageNote: 比dependent更正式；常用于法律和学术语境
example: The success of the project is contingent on securing adequate funding and stakeholder support.
variants: contingent on/upon, largely contingent on, wholly contingent upon

name: independent of
example: An independent judiciary is essential to ensuring that the legal system operates independent of political influence.
variants: wholly independent of, entirely independent of, largely independent of

name: characteristic of
usageNote: characteristic可作名词或形容词；be characteristic of为固定搭配
example: A willingness to embrace change is characteristic of successful organizations in dynamic industries.
variants: be characteristic of, typical of, distinctive of

name: typical of
usageNote: 常用于举例说明；与be/become/seem搭配
example: This kind of risk-taking behavior is typical of young entrepreneurs entering highly competitive markets.
variants: be typical of, fairly typical of, seem typical of

name: satisfied with
example: Overall, employees were reasonably satisfied with the new flexible working arrangements.
variants: be satisfied with, feel satisfied with, highly satisfied with

name: content with
usageNote: 比satisfied语气更温和；常与be/remain/seem搭配
example: While not enthusiastic about the proposal, most committee members were content with the compromise.
variants: be content with, remain content with, perfectly content with

name: concerned about
usageNote: concerned about/for表示担忧；concerned with表示涉及
example: Parents are increasingly concerned about the amount of time their children spend on screens.
variants: be concerned about, deeply concerned about, seriously concerned about

name: worried about
usageNote: 比concerned更口语化；但学术写作中也可使用
example: Environmental scientists are genuinely worried about the accelerating rate of biodiversity loss.
variants: be worried about, deeply worried about, increasingly worried about

name: familiar with
example: Students should become thoroughly familiar with academic referencing conventions before writing their dissertations.
variants: be familiar with, become familiar with, get familiar with

name: associated with
usageNote: associated强调关联性；常用于学术研究中
example: Several lifestyle factors have been found to be strongly associated with an increased risk of cardiovascular disease.
variants: closely associated with, strongly associated with, directly associated with

name: linked with
usageNote: linked常与with或to搭配；暗示可能存在因果关系
example: There is growing evidence that sleep quality is closely linked with academic performance among university students.
variants: closely linked with, directly linked to, inextricably linked with

name: connected with
usageNote: connected比associated/link更通用；可指任何类型联系
example: Several social and economic factors are closely connected with patterns of criminal behavior.
variants: closely connected with, directly connected to, intimately connected with

name: involved in
usageNote: involved in强调参与某事；与be/get/become搭配
example: All employees should be actively involved in decision-making processes that affect their work.
variants: be involved in, get involved in, actively involved in, deeply involved in

name: engaged in
function: 形容词+介词：从事/参与...（正式）
example: The company is primarily engaged in research and development of sustainable energy technologies.
variants: be engaged in, become engaged in, actively engaged in

name: interested in
function: 形容词+介词：对...感兴趣的
example: Young people today appear to be less interested in pursuing traditional career paths in large corporations.
variants: be interested in, become interested in, highly interested in

name: keen on
function: 形容词+介词：热衷于...（英式）
usageNote: 偏英式用法；比interested语气更强；非正式
example: Employers are particularly keen on candidates who demonstrate strong problem-solving abilities.
variants: be keen on, very keen on, particularly keen on

name: opposed to
example: A significant proportion of the local population is strongly opposed to the proposed construction of a new airport.
variants: be opposed to, strongly opposed to, vehemently opposed to

name: averse to
function: 形容词+介词：不喜欢...的（正式）
usageNote: 比opposed语气更弱；常用于not averse to双重否定
example: The company is not averse to taking calculated risks when entering new markets.
variants: be averse to, risk-averse, not averse to

name: subject to
function: 形容词+介词：受...支配/ subject to
usageNote: subject后接to；表示受制于某条件或规则
example: All research proposals are subject to rigorous ethical review before approval is granted.
variants: be subject to, remain subject to, solely subject to

name: exposed to
example: Children who are regularly exposed to multiple languages tend to develop stronger cognitive flexibility.
variants: be exposed to, become exposed to, heavily exposed to

name: vulnerable to
function: 形容词+介词：易受...影响的
example: Elderly people are particularly vulnerable to the effects of social isolation and loneliness.
variants: be vulnerable to, particularly vulnerable to, remain vulnerable to

name: resistant to
example: Some bacteria have become increasingly resistant to conventional antibiotics.
variants: be resistant to, prove resistant to, highly resistant to

name: immune to
usageNote: immune本义为免疫，引申为不受影响；常与be/remain/seem搭配
example: No society is immune to the challenges posed by rapid technological change and economic disruption.
variants: be immune to, remain immune to, seemingly immune to

name: proficient in
example: To succeed in the global marketplace, professionals must become proficient in at least one foreign language.
variants: be proficient in, become proficient in, highly proficient in

name: skilled at
example: Individuals who are skilled at adapting to change are more likely to thrive in dynamic work environments.
variants: be skilled at, highly skilled at, technically skilled in

name: competent in
usageNote: competent强调具备足够能力；与be/prove/highly搭配
example: A competent teacher must not only be knowledgeable but also competent in classroom management.
variants: be competent in, prove competent in, fully competent in

name: deficient in
function: 形容词+介词：缺乏...的（正式）
usageNote: deficient为正式用语；常用于学术和医学语境
example: Many diets in developing countries are deficient in essential vitamins and minerals.
variants: be deficient in, severely deficient in, chronically deficient in

name: lacking in
usageNote: lacking可作形容词用；in后接所缺品质或特征
example: The proposal was criticized for lacking in practical detail and feasibility assessment.
variants: be lacking in, seriously lacking in, somewhat lacking in

name: abundant in
function: 形容词+介词： rich in...（正式）
usageNote: abundant强调丰富性；常用于自然资源和生物描述
example: The region is abundant in natural resources, including oil, natural gas, and mineral deposits.
variants: be abundant in, extremely abundant in, remarkably abundant in

name: rich in
usageNote: rich in常用于描述营养成分、资源、文化等
example: A diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and whole grains is associated with a lower risk of chronic disease.
variants: be rich in, extremely rich in, particularly rich in

name: consistent with
usageNote: 学术写作高频搭配；常用于描述结果与理论/预期的一致性
example: The findings of this study are broadly consistent with those reported in previous research.
variants: broadly consistent with, entirely consistent with, fully consistent with

name: incompatible with
function: 形容词+介词：与...不兼容的
usageNote: consistent with的反义词；常用于描述矛盾关系
example: Unregulated economic growth is ultimately incompatible with environmental sustainability.
variants: wholly incompatible with, fundamentally incompatible with, directly incompatible with

name: suitable for
example: This methodology may not be suitable for studies involving small sample sizes.
variants: be suitable for, highly suitable for, particularly suitable for

name: appropriate for
example: The punishment must be appropriate for the severity of the offense committed.
variants: be appropriate for, highly appropriate for, deemed appropriate for

name: necessary for
example: Critical thinking skills are necessary for success in both academic and professional contexts.
variants: be necessary for, absolutely necessary for, essential for

name: important for
usageNote: important后接for；后接动作执行者或目标
example: Regular physical activity is important for maintaining good mental health throughout life.
variants: be important for, particularly important for, vitally important for

name: good for
usageNote: good for为通用搭配；学术写作中可用beneficial to替代
example: A diverse diet is good for overall health and can help prevent nutritional deficiencies.
variants: be good for, particularly good for, especially good for

name: bad for
usageNote: bad for为通用搭配；学术写作中可用harmful/detrimental to替代
example: Prolonged sedentary behavior is bad for both physical and mental wellbeing.
variants: be bad for, particularly bad for, extremely bad for

name: prone to
usageNote: prone强调倾向性；常用于描述负面倾向
example: Coastal regions are particularly prone to the effects of climate change, including rising sea levels.
variants: be prone to, particularly prone to, especially prone to

name: susceptible to
function: 形容词+介词：易受...影响的（正式）
usageNote: 比prone更正式；常用于医学和科学语境
example: Older adults are more susceptible to infections due to age-related declines in immune function.
variants: be susceptible to, highly susceptible to, particularly susceptible to

name: conducive to
function: 形容词+介词：有助于...的（正式）
example: A supportive work environment is conducive to employee creativity and productivity.
variants: be conducive to, highly conducive to, particularly conducive to

name: committed to
usageNote: committed强调承诺和投入；与be/remain/fully搭配
example: The organization is fully committed to achieving carbon neutrality by the year 2040.
variants: be committed to, remain committed to, deeply committed to

name: dedicated to
usageNote: 与committed近义；强调专注和奉献
example: The research center is dedicated to finding innovative solutions to renewable energy challenges.
variants: be dedicated to, remain dedicated to, solely dedicated to

name: devoted to
usageNote: 比dedicated更强调情感投入；常与be/remain搭配
example: She has been devoted to improving educational opportunities for underprivileged children throughout her career.
variants: be devoted to, remain devoted to, entirely devoted to

name: accustomed to
usageNote: accustomed to后可接名词或动名词；与be/get/become搭配
example: Having grown up in the digital age, young people are accustomed to instant access to information.
variants: be accustomed to, get accustomed to, become accustomed to

name: adequate for
usageNote: adequate强调足够但不过多；与be/consider/prove搭配
example: The existing infrastructure may not be adequate for the needs of a rapidly growing population.
variants: be adequate for, more than adequate for, barely adequate for

name: sufficient for
function: 形容词+介词：足以...的（正式）
usageNote: 比adequate更正式；反义词为insufficient for
example: The evidence presented is sufficient for us to conclude that the intervention was effective.
variants: be sufficient for, more than sufficient for, barely sufficient for

name: parallel to
function: 形容词+介词：与...并行的/类似的
usageNote: parallel本义为平行，引申为类似；常与be/run/draw搭配
example: The development of the internet is in many ways parallel to that of the printing press centuries earlier.
variants: be parallel to, run parallel to, draw a parallel to

name: identical to
function: 形容词+介词：与...完全相同的
example: The two studies produced virtually identical results despite using different methodologies.
variants: be identical to, virtually identical to, almost identical to

name: proportionate to
function: 形容词+介词：与...成比例的
usageNote: 常与be/seem/remains搭配；反义词为disproportionate to
example: The severity of the punishment should be proportionate to the seriousness of the offense.
variants: be proportionate to, directly proportionate to, roughly proportionate to

name: attributable to
function: 形容词+介词：可归因于...的
usageNote: 学术写作因果表达；常用于结论部分
example: The improvement in test scores is largely attributable to the implementation of targeted tutoring programs.
variants: largely attributable to, primarily attributable to, partly attributable to

name: due to
template: due to + N / due to + the fact that
usageNote: due to为高频因果表达；注意与because of的用法区别
example: The delay was largely due to unforeseen technical difficulties encountered during the implementation phase.
variants: largely due to, primarily due to, partly due to

name: liable to
template: liable to + V / liable for + N
usageNote: liable to后接动词原形表倾向；liable for后接名词表责任
example: Any system that relies on a single point of failure is liable to malfunction at critical moments.
variants: be liable to, legally liable for, personally liable for

name: tantamount to
function: 形容词+介词：等同于...的（正式）
usageNote: tantamount后只接to；表示实质上等同
example: Refusing to acknowledge the problem is tantamount to allowing it to worsen unchecked.
variants: be tantamount to, virtually tantamount to, effectively tantamount to

name: synonymous with
function: 形容词+介词：与...同义的/等同的
usageNote: synonymous本义为同义，引申为密不可分
example: In many people's minds, the brand has become synonymous with quality and innovation.
variants: be synonymous with, become synonymous with, virtually synonymous with

name: indispensable to
function: 形容词+介词：不可或缺的
example: Access to reliable internet connectivity has become indispensable to modern economic and social participation.
variants: be indispensable to, prove indispensable to, absolutely indispensable to

name: prevalent in
example: Obesity is particularly prevalent in populations that consume a diet high in processed foods.
variants: be prevalent in, highly prevalent in, increasingly prevalent in

name: predominant in
usageNote: predominant强调占主导地位；与be/remain/become搭配
example: English has become predominant in international business communication and academic publishing.
variants: be predominant in, remain predominant in, increasingly predominant in

name: implicit in
function: 形容词+介词：隐含在...中的
example: Certain cultural assumptions are implicit in the way that educational curricula are designed.
variants: be implicit in, remain implicit in, largely implicit in

name: explicit in
function: 形容词+介词：在...中明确的
example: The criteria for evaluation are made explicit in the course syllabus distributed at the beginning of the semester.
variants: be explicit in, make explicit in, become explicit in

name: integral to
function: 形容词+介词：对...不可或缺的
usageNote: integral强调作为整体必要部分；与essential/vital近义
example: Community engagement is integral to the success of any public health initiative.
variants: be integral to, remain integral to, prove integral to

name: pivotal to
usageNote: pivotal强调转折点或关键作用；与crucial/vital近义
example: Technological innovation is pivotal to maintaining competitiveness in the global marketplace.
variants: be pivotal to, prove pivotal to, remain pivotal to

name: instrumental in
function: 形容词+介词：对...起重要作用的
usageNote: instrumental强调作为工具或手段发挥关键作用
example: International cooperation was instrumental in achieving the successful ratification of the climate agreement.
variants: be instrumental in, prove instrumental in, largely instrumental in

name: 宾语从句-I believe that
function: 宾语从句：动词后接that引导的完整陈述
usageNote: 雅思写作和口语最基础的句型，用于表达个人观点或立场
example: I believe that education is the most effective tool for social mobility. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: I argue that government intervention is necessary. / I maintain that prevention is better than cure.

name: 原因状语从句-Because
template: Because + 从句, + 主句 / 主句 + because + 从句
function: 原因状语从句：Because引导直接原因
usageNote: 最基础的因果表达，语气强于since/as
example: Because the evidence is limited, any conclusion must be treated with caution. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Many species are endangered because their natural habitats have been destroyed. / Because resources are finite, allocation decisions are inevitably difficult.

name: 原因状语从句-Now that
template: Now that + 从句, + 主句
function: 原因状语从句：表示'既然/由于现在'，强调新情况
usageNote: 用于引出新的事实或变化作为后续行动的基础
example: Now that technology has advanced to this level, remote work has become viable for many industries. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: Now that the data is publicly available, researchers can conduct independent analyses. / Now that the problem has been acknowledged, solutions can be explored.

name: 条件状语从句-If
function: 条件状语从句：If引导真实条件
usageNote: 雅思写作中最基础、最高频的条件表达
example: If the current trend continues, significant environmental damage will be inevitable. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: If governments invest adequately in education, social inequality can be reduced. / If proper measures are not taken, the situation will deteriorate further.

name: 比较状语从句-than
function: 比较状语从句：Than引导比较
usageNote: 最基础的比较句型，用于对比两个事物
example: Online learning is more flexible than traditional classroom teaching is. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: The situation is more complex than it initially appears. / Developing countries face greater challenges than developed countries do.

name: 混合构式-when it comes to
template: When it comes to + n./-ing, + 主句
function: 混合构式：When it comes to表示'当谈到/涉及'
usageNote: 雅思口语和写作中极其常用的话题引入句型
example: When it comes to education, quality matters more than quantity. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: When it comes to environmental protection, individual actions are as important as government policies. / When it comes to choosing a career, passion should be a key consideration.

name: 混合构式-in terms of
template: In terms of + n., + 主句
function: 混合构式：In terms of表示'就...而言/在...方面'
usageNote: 雅思口语和写作中最常用的限定范围表达
example: In terms of cost-effectiveness, preventive measures outperform reactive approaches. [语域：正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: In terms of employment, the technology sector has shown the most growth. / In terms of quality of life, smaller cities can be surprisingly attractive.

name: 混合构式-instead of
function: 混合构式：Instead of表示'代替/而不是'
example: Instead of relying solely on exams, schools should adopt diverse assessment methods. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Instead of building more roads, cities should invest in public transportation.

name: 被动语态-be+done（一般现在）
template: am/is/are + 过去分词 (+ by + 施动者)
function: 被动语态：一般现在时的被动，陈述客观事实
usageNote: 学术写作中用于强调动作对象而非执行者
example: The study is conducted annually to track changes in public opinion. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: Data is collected from multiple sources. / Results are analyzed using statistical software.

name: 被动语态-be+done（一般过去）
function: 被动语态：一般过去时的被动，陈述过去发生的事件
usageNote: Task 1和2中描述历史事件或研究发现时常用
example: The experiment was conducted in controlled laboratory conditions. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: The survey was completed by over five thousand participants. / Significant changes were observed after the intervention.

name: 被动语态-be+done（现在完成）
function: 被动语态：现在完成时的被动，强调过去动作对现在的影响
usageNote: 学术写作中引述已完成的研究或实现的变化
example: Significant progress has been made in reducing infant mortality. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: New methods have been developed to address this challenge. / It has been shown that education correlates strongly with income.

name: 被动语态-be+done（过去完成）
function: 被动语态：过去完成时的被动，描述过去的过去
usageNote: 用于描述某过去事件之前已完成的动作
example: By the time the report was published, the data had already been questioned. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: The building had been demolished before the preservation order was issued.

name: 被动语态-be+done（将来时）
template: will be / is going to be + 过去分词
function: 被动语态：将来时的被动，预测未来事件
example: It is expected that renewable energy will be widely adopted within two decades. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: The findings will be presented at the international conference. / New regulations are going to be introduced next year.

name: 被动语态-be+being+done（进行被动）
function: 被动语态：进行时的被动，描述正在发生的被动动作
usageNote: 用于描述正在进行的被动过程
example: Research is being conducted to find a viable solution. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: The issue is being addressed at the highest levels of government. / New schools are being built to accommodate growing demand.

name: 被动语态-It is considered/regarded as
template: It is considered/regarded/perceived/viewed as + adj./n. + that/to
function: 被动语态：It is considered as表示被认为
usageNote: 用于表达对某事物的评价或归类
example: It is generally considered as essential that all children receive basic education. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Climate change is increasingly perceived as an existential threat. / The policy is widely viewed as a step in the right direction.

name: 情态被动-can be done
function: 情态被动：表示能力或可能性的被动
usageNote: 学术写作中讨论可能性的常用被动
example: The results can be interpreted in several different ways. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: This problem can be solved through international cooperation. / The issue could be addressed more effectively with adequate funding.

name: 情态被动-must be done
template: must/need to/have to + be + 过去分词
coreWords: must be,need to be,have to be
function: 情态被动：表示必要性或义务的被动
usageNote: 用于强调某事的必要性
example: This issue must be addressed urgently before it escalates further. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Further research needs to be conducted to confirm these findings. / Immediate action has to be taken to protect endangered species.

name: 情态被动-should be done
template: should/ought to + be + 过去分词
function: 情态被动：表示建议或适当性的被动
usageNote: 用于提出建议或表达适当性
example: Further research should be carried out to validate these preliminary results. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: More resources ought to be allocated to early childhood education. / The policy should be reviewed periodically to ensure its effectiveness.

name: 情态被动-may/might be done
function: 情态被动：表示可能性的被动
usageNote: 用于表达不确定性或推测
example: The decline may be attributed to several interconnected factors. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: This trend might be reversed if appropriate measures are implemented. / The discrepancy may be explained by differences in methodology.

name: 情态被动-would be done
function: 情态被动：表示意愿或将来的被动
usageNote: 用于条件句或表达意愿
example: If the proposal were accepted, significant changes would be implemented. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: The new regulations will be enforced starting next month.

name: 情态被动-used to be done
template: used to be + 过去分词 / be used to + -ing
function: 情态被动：used to be表示过去习惯的被动
usageNote: 用于描述过去常见但现已改变的被动
example: Traditional crafts used to be passed down through generations of artisans. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: Children used to be taught respect for elders as a core value. / Such problems used to be handled at the community level.

name: have/get sth done结构
template: have/get + 宾语 + 过去分词
usageNote: 表示安排或经历某事被处理
example: The data was analyzed by computer. / The survey was completed by 500 participants. [语域：半正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: We had the research approved by the ethics committee. / The company got the report published in a leading journal.

name: have sth done（经历）
template: have + 宾语 + 过去分词（经历不幸事件）
function: 使役被动：经历某事（常指不幸）
example: The city had its infrastructure severely damaged by the hurricane. [语域：半正式 | Task 2]
variants: The school had its funding cut by twenty percent last year. / Many families have had their lives disrupted by the conflict.

name: get sth done（完成）
template: get + 宾语 + 过去分词（强调完成）
function: 使役被动：get sth done强调完成某事
usageNote: 口语和半正式写作中常用
example: We need to get this problem solved as soon as possible. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语]
variants: The government should get the new policy implemented before the end of the year.

name: need/want + doing
function: 主动表被动：need doing = need to be done
example: The education system needs reforming to meet modern demands. [语域：半正式 | Task 2]
variants: This issue requires careful handling. / The report deserves reading in its entirety.

name: worth + doing
function: 主动表被动：worth doing表示值得被做
example: This research is worth conducting given its potential impact. [语域：半正式 | Task 2]
variants: The book is worth reading for its insights into cultural change. / This approach is worth considering despite its limitations.

name: 被动语态-by doing
function: 方式状语中的被动含义：By doing表示通过某种方式
usageNote: 描述达成某目标的方式或方法
example: By investing in education, nations can ensure sustainable development. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: By providing equal opportunities, societies can reduce inequality. / By working together, countries can address global challenges.

name: There be-一般现在
template: There is/are + n. (+ 地点/时间状语)
function: 存在句：There be句型的基本形式，表示存在
usageNote: 学术写作中引述事实、数据或现象的常用句型
example: There is compelling evidence that climate change is accelerating. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There are several reasons why people choose to study abroad. / There is a clear correlation between income and health outcomes.

name: There be+否定/限定
function: 存在句：There be的否定或限定形式
usageNote: 用于表达不存在或数量极少
example: There is no justification for cutting education budgets during economic downturns. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There are few alternatives to investing in human capital. / There is little doubt that technology drives progress.

name: There be+a growing trend
usageNote: Task 1和2中描述趋势的高频句型
example: There is a growing trend toward remote working in many industries. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: There is an increasing awareness of the importance of mental health. / There has been a rising demand for sustainable products.

name: There be+a need for
template: There is/are + a need/demand/requirement + for + n.
function: 存在句：There be+a need for表示存在需求
usageNote: 用于提出建议或指出需求
example: There is an urgent need for further research in this area. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There is a clear need for improved public transportation in urban areas. / There are growing demands for greater government transparency.

name: There be+a widespread belief
template: There is/are + a widespread/common/general/popular + belief/view/perception + that
example: There is a widespread belief that hard work leads to success. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There is a common perception that older workers are less adaptable. / There are differing views on whether technology enhances or hinders learning.

name: There seems/appears to be
function: 存在句：There seems to be表示似乎存在
usageNote: 用于表达不确定的存在或推测
example: There seems to be a correlation between screen time and sleep quality among adolescents. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There appears to be a trend toward earlier retirement in developed countries. / There tends to be a gap between policy intentions and outcomes.

name: There be-likely/unlikely to be
template: There is/are likely/unlikely/bound/sure to be + n.
usageNote: 用于预测存在的可能性
example: There are likely to be significant challenges in implementing this policy. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There is bound to be resistance to any major reform. / There is sure to be debate about the best way forward.

name: There is no use/point in
template: There is no use/point/good/sense/harm in + doing
function: 存在句：There is no use in表示做某事无意义
usageNote: 用于评价某行为的效用
example: There is no point in denying that climate change poses a serious threat. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: There is little use in implementing policies without proper enforcement. / There is no harm in exploring alternative approaches.

name: There is no alternative but to
template: There is no alternative/choice/option but to + v.
function: 存在句：There is no alternative but to表示别无选择
usageNote: 用于强调唯一可行的方案
example: There is no alternative but to invest heavily in renewable energy sources. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There is no choice but to reform the education system. / There was no option but to close the factory.

name: There used to be
template: There used to be + n.（过去曾存在）
function: 存在句：There used to be表示过去存在
usageNote: 用于对比过去和现在的变化
example: There used to be a strong sense of community in many neighborhoods. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: There used to be more manufacturing jobs in this region. / There didn't use to be such concern about data privacy.

name: There be+进行时
function: 存在句：There be+现在分词表示正在发生的事
usageNote: 用于描述正在发生的现象
example: There are increasing numbers of people working from home permanently. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: There is a growing body of evidence supporting this hypothesis. / There are more students choosing vocational training over university degrees.

name: There be+情态
usageNote: 用于表达对存在的推测或必要性
example: There may be other factors that have not yet been considered. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There must be a more effective way to address this issue. / There could be unintended consequences that need to be monitored.

name: There have been
template: There have/has been + n. + 过去分词/介词短语
function: 存在句：There have been现在完成时
usageNote: 用于描述从过去持续到现在的存在
example: There have been numerous studies examining the relationship between diet and health. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: There has been a significant increase in online learning platforms over the past decade. / There have been calls for greater regulation of social media.

name: 独立主格-名词+分词
function: 独立主格结构：名词+分词短语作状语
usageNote: 高级句式，用于增加句式多样性
example: Weather permitting, the outdoor event will proceed as scheduled. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: All things considered, the policy has been largely successful. / Time allowing, additional research will be conducted.

name: 独立主格-with/without复合
template: With/Without + n. + v.-ing/v.-ed/adj./adv./介词短语, + 主句
function: 独立主格结构：with/without+复合宾语
example: With technology advancing so rapidly, traditional industries face unprecedented challenges. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Without proper regulation being in place, the market remains volatile. / With so many factors at play, predicting outcomes is difficult.

name: 独立主格-There being
function: 独立主格结构：There being结构
example: There being no viable alternatives, the committee approved the proposal. [语域：高度正式 | Task 2]
variants: There being little evidence to the contrary, the conclusion was accepted.

name: 分词结构-悬垂分词
function: 分词短语：分词短语作状语
usageNote: 学术写作中简化句式的重要技巧
example: Having analyzed the data thoroughly, researchers identified three key patterns. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: Based on the evidence presented, the conclusion seems justified. / Considering all available information, the policy appears effective.

name: 分词结构-Generally speaking
function: 分词结构：副词+speaking作插入语
usageNote: 学术写作和口语中常用的评述性插入语
example: Broadly speaking, developed nations invest more in education than developing ones. [语域：半正式 | Task 1/2/口语Part 3]
variants: Generally speaking, younger people are more comfortable with technology. / Strictly speaking, the data does not support this conclusion.

name: 分词结构-Judging from
function: 分词结构：Judging from表示'从...判断'
usageNote: 用于基于证据做出判断
example: Judging from the available evidence, the trend is likely to continue. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: Judging by the response so far, the initiative has been well received.

name: 分词结构-Talking of/Speaking of
template: Talking of/Speaking of + n., + 主句
function: 分词结构：Talking of表示'谈到/说到'
usageNote: 口语和半正式写作中转换话题
example: Speaking of education, Finland's system is often cited as a model. [语域：半正式/口语 | 口语Part 3]
variants: Talking of environmental protection, individual actions matter as much as government policies.

name: 感叹句-How+adj.
template: How + adj./adv. + 主语 + 谓语!
example: How important it is that we preserve our cultural heritage for future generations! [语域：正式/文学 | Task 2]
variants: How quickly the world has changed in just a few decades! / How crucial education is to individual success!

name: 感叹句-What+n.
template: What + (a/an) + adj. + n. + 主语 + 谓语!
usageNote: 用于强调某事物的性质
example: What a significant impact a single teacher can have on a student's life! [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语]
variants: What challenges the modern world faces! / What an important role technology plays in education!

name: 祈使句+and/or
template: 祈使句 + and/or + 陈述句
example: Invest in education, and the returns will benefit society for generations. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Ignore the warning signs, and the consequences will be catastrophic. / Take action now, or it will be too late to reverse the damage.

name: 插入语-more importantly
function: 评注性状语：more importantly等连接性状语
example: The policy reduces costs; more importantly, it improves outcomes for the most vulnerable. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Technology enhances efficiency. Furthermore, it creates new opportunities for innovation.

name: 插入语-to say the least
template: ..., to say the least/most/put it mildly,
function: 评注性状语：to say the least表示'至少可以说'
example: The results were disappointing, to say the least. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: The situation is challenging, to put it mildly. / The proposal is ambitious, to say the most.

name: 插入语-if I may say so
template: ..., if I may say so/if you don't mind my saying so,
function: 评注性状语：礼貌性插入语
usageNote: 口语和半正式写作中的礼貌表达
example: The plan is somewhat unrealistic, if I may say so. [语域：半正式/口语 | 口语Part 3]
variants: Your argument lacks supporting evidence, if you don't mind my saying so.

name: 插入语-apparently/obviously
function: 评注性状语：评注副词作句首插入语
usageNote: 用于表达说话者的评价或态度
example: Obviously, education reform cannot succeed without adequate funding. [语域：半正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: Apparently, the policy has not achieved its intended goals. / Clearly, more research is needed in this area.

name: 插入语-personally speaking
example: Honestly speaking, I believe the benefits outweigh the drawbacks. [语域：口语/半正式 | 口语Part 3]
variants: Frankly speaking, the policy is unlikely to succeed in its current form. / Personally speaking, I value flexibility over stability.

name: 省略句-并列句省略
template: S + v. + O. + and/but/or + v. + O.（省略相同成分）
function: 省略句：并列句中的省略
usageNote: 避免重复，增强句式简洁性
example: Some prefer traditional methods; others, modern approaches. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: The first proposal was accepted; the second, rejected. / Developing countries need investment; developed countries, reform.

name: 省略句-so/neither省略
function: 省略句：so/neither+助动词避免重复
usageNote: 附和或否定附和时常用
example: The policy has proven effective, and so have similar initiatives in other countries. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: The first study found positive results, and so did the follow-up research. / The proposal is not feasible, nor is the alternative.

name: 引述-According to
template: According to + 来源, + 主句
usageNote: Task 1和2中引述数据或观点的常用方式
example: According to recent statistics, the literacy rate has improved significantly. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: According to the World Health Organization, mental health issues are on the rise globally. / According to the survey, a majority of respondents support the policy.

name: 引述-In the words of
template: In the words of + 某人, + 引述内容
function: 引述：In the words of引述某人原话
usageNote: 用于引用权威人士或名人观点
example: In the words of Nelson Mandela, 'Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.' [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: In the words of the leading researcher, 'The findings challenge everything we thought we knew.'

name: 引述-As + 人 + put(s) it
template: As + 某人 + put(s)/note(s)/observe(s)/remark(s) it, + 引述
function: 引述：As sb. puts it引述某人说法
usageNote: 引用某人观点的常用句式
example: As the report puts it, 'The gap between rich and poor continues to widen.' [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: As one expert noted, technology is neither inherently good nor bad. / As researchers have observed, early intervention produces the best outcomes.

name: 反问句-否定疑问
template: 否定词 + 助动词 + 主语 + v.?
function: 反问句：否定疑问句表达肯定含义
example: Is it not the responsibility of governments to ensure equal access to education? [语域：高度正式 | Task 2]
variants: Does it not make sense to invest in prevention rather than cure? / Can there be any doubt that education transforms lives?

name: 反问句-rhetorical
template: 疑问词 + 助动词 + 主语 + v.?（不需要回答）
usageNote: 用于引导读者思考，增强说服力
example: What would society look like without universal education? [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Why should future generations suffer for our mistakes? / How can we justify ignoring the plight of the most vulnerable?

name: 因果-Owing to/Due to
template: Owing to/Due to/Thanks to/Because of + n., + 主句
function: 因果表达：介词短语表示原因
usageNote: Task 1和2中表达原因的常用方式
example: Due to advances in technology, remote work has become increasingly feasible. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: Owing to inadequate funding, many schools lack basic resources. / Thanks to international cooperation, significant progress has been made.

name: 因果-As a result of
template: As a result of/In consequence of/On account of + n., + 主句
coreWords: as a result of,in consequence of
function: 因果表达：As a result of表示结果
usageNote: 用于强调因果关系的正式表达
example: As a result of sustained investment, literacy rates have improved dramatically. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: In consequence of rapid urbanization, traditional communities have been disrupted.

name: 平衡-On the one/other hand
template: On the one hand, ... . On the other hand, ... .
coreWords: on the one hand,on the other hand
function: 平衡表达：一方面...另一方面...
usageNote: Task 2讨论型文章的核心结构句型
example: On the one hand, technology creates employment opportunities. On the other hand, it eliminates traditional jobs. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: On the one hand, cities offer better amenities. On the other hand, they face greater environmental challenges.

name: 对比-rather than/instead of
template: 主句 + rather than/instead of + n./v.-ing
function: 对比表达：rather than/instead of表示对比选择
usageNote: 用于否定前者肯定后者
example: Governments should invest in prevention rather than wait for crises to emerge. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: We should focus on quality rather than quantity. / Instead of punishing offenders, we should rehabilitate them.

name: 对比-in comparison with
template: In comparison with/By comparison with/Compared with + n., + 主句
function: 对比表达：In comparison with表示比较
example: In comparison with the previous decade, current growth rates are significantly lower. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: Compared with urban schools, rural institutions receive far less funding. / By comparison, the second proposal seems more practical.

name: 数量-A large number of
function: 数量表达：描述数量或比例
usageNote: Task 1和2中描述数量或比例的高频表达
example: A significant proportion of the population lacks access to basic healthcare. [语域：正式 | Task 1/2]
variants: A growing number of young people are choosing to start their own businesses. / A considerable amount of resources is wasted due to inefficiency.

name: 程度-To a large extent
template: To a large/great/certain/some/significant extent/degree, + 主句
function: 程度表达：to some extent表示'在某种程度上'
usageNote: 用于限定论述的范围或程度
example: To a large extent, educational outcomes depend on socioeconomic background. [语域：正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: To some degree, technology has democratized access to information. / The policy has succeeded to a certain extent.

name: 程度-far from
function: 程度表达：far from表示'远非/根本不'
example: Far from solving the problem, the policy may have exacerbated it. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: The situation is far from satisfactory. / Far from being a burden, immigration can drive economic growth.

name: 程度-nothing like
template: nothing like/nothing short of/nowhere near + n., + 主句
function: 程度表达：nothing like表示'根本不像/远不如'
example: The current funding is nothing like what is needed to address the crisis. [语域：半正式 | Task 2]
variants: The results were nothing short of remarkable. / Actual progress is nowhere near what was promised.

name: 总结-To sum up/In conclusion
template: To sum up/In conclusion/In summary/All in all/Ultimately, + 主句
function: 总结表达：总结性连接语
example: To sum up, education remains the most powerful tool for social transformation. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: In conclusion, while challenges remain, the outlook is broadly positive. / Ultimately, the success of any policy depends on its implementation.

name: 综合-On balance
template: On balance/By and large/Overall/Taking everything into account, + 主句
function: 综合表达：综合考量后的结论
usageNote: 用于Task 2结论段中平衡各方观点
example: On balance, I believe the advantages of technology in education outweigh the disadvantages. [语域：正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: By and large, the policy has achieved its objectives. / Taking everything into account, the reform should be viewed as a success.

name: When it comes to...
example: When it comes to environmental protection, government policies play a pivotal role.

name: As far as...is concerned
example: As far as education is concerned, investment in early childhood development yields the most significant long-term benefits.

name: With regard to...
example: With regard to the data presented, a clear upward trend can be observed in renewable energy consumption.

name: In terms of...
example: In terms of cost-effectiveness, public transportation is undoubtedly superior to private vehicle ownership.

name: Regarding...
example: Regarding the issue of urban congestion, several measures have been proposed by city planners.
variants: With regard to..., In regard to...,

name: Concerning...
example: Concerning the decline in traditional media readership, digital platforms have fundamentally altered information consumption habits.

name: As for...
example: As for the disadvantages, they are relatively minor compared to the substantial benefits.

name: Turning to...
example: Turning to the social implications, this policy has had a profound effect on community cohesion.

name: From my perspective
example: From my perspective, the advantages of remote working far outweigh its potential drawbacks.

variants: Too many chiefs and not enough Indians., Many hands make light work (反义)

name: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
template: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step
example: Addressing climate change may seem overwhelming, but a journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
variants: Every great achievement starts small., Small steps lead to big changes,

name: Knowledge is power.
example: Investing in education is essential for national development because knowledge is power in the modern economy.
variants: Information is power., Education is the key to empowerment,

name: Time and tide wait for no man.
template: Time and tide wait for no man
example: We must act now to protect endangered species, for time and tide wait for no man.
variants: Time waits for no one., Seize the day (Carpe diem),

name: Better late than never.
example: He finally decided to go back to university at the age of forty, but as they say, better late than never.
variants: It's never too late., Late is better than never,

name: Honesty is the best policy.
example: In both personal relationships and business dealings, honesty is the best policy in the long run.
variants: Truthfulness pays., Being honest always works out best,

name: Do not put all your eggs in one basket.
template: Do not put all your eggs in one basket
function: 不要把所有鸡蛋放在一个篮子里
example: Investors are advised to diversify their portfolios and not put all their eggs in one basket.
variants: Diversify your investments., Spread your risks,

name: Necessity is the mother of invention.
template: Necessity is the mother of invention
example: The pandemic accelerated digital transformation dramatically, proving that necessity is the mother of invention.
variants: Need drives innovation., Hardship breeds creativity,

name: broaden one's horizons
example: Studying abroad is an excellent way to broaden one's horizons and gain a deeper understanding of different cultures.
variants: expand one's horizons, widen one's perspective, open one's eyes to new things

name: bookworm
example: My sister has always been a bookworm; she devours at least three novels every week.
variants: an avid reader, a bibliophile, someone who loves reading

name: learn by heart
example: In my country, students are often required to learn classical poems by heart from an early age.
variants: memorize, commit to memory, know by rote

name: pass with flying colors
example: Despite her initial anxiety about the final examination, she passed with flying colors.
variants: ace an exam, pass with distinction, do exceptionally well

name: drop out (of)
example: Financial hardship is one of the primary reasons why some students from disadvantaged backgrounds drop out of university.
variants: withdraw from, quit school, leave before completing

name: hit the books
example: I have an important exam next week, so I need to hit the books and do some serious revision.
variants: study hard, buckle down, get down to studying

name: keep up with the times
example: Traditional businesses that fail to embrace digital transformation will struggle to keep up with the times.
variants: keep pace with modern developments, move with the times, stay current

name: at the click of a button
template: at the click of a button
example: In today's digital age, information from around the world is accessible at the click of a button.
variants: at the touch of a button, with a single click, instantly

name: a double-edged sword
example: The internet is undoubtedly a double-edged sword, offering unprecedented access to information while posing significant privacy risks.
variants: a mixed blessing, a two-sided issue, something with both pros and cons

name: a blessing in disguise
example: Losing that job turned out to be a blessing in disguise, as it pushed me to pursue my true passion.
variants: a good thing that initially seemed bad, every cloud has a silver lining

name: go green
example: Many corporations are making efforts to go green by reducing packaging and switching to renewable energy sources.
variants: become environmentally friendly, adopt eco-friendly practices, go eco-friendly

name: carbon footprint
template: carbon footprint / reduce one's carbon footprint
example: Individuals can reduce their carbon footprint by using public transport, conserving energy, and eating less meat.
variants: environmental impact, carbon emissions, ecological footprint

name: Mother Nature
example: Spending time in the mountains really helps me reconnect with Mother Nature and escape from city stress.
variants: the natural world, the environment, nature

name: go to waste
template: go to waste / [something] goes to waste
example: It is alarming how much edible food goes to waste in developed countries while millions face hunger elsewhere.
variants: be wasted, be thrown away unused, be squandered

name: sustainable development
example: Achieving sustainable development requires balancing economic growth with environmental preservation and social equity.
variants: eco-friendly growth, green development, environmentally sustainable progress

name: social fabric
example: The breakdown of traditional family structures has had a profound impact on the social fabric of many communities.
variants: social structure, the fabric of society, social cohesion

name: gap between rich and poor
template: the gap between rich and poor / the rich-poor gap
example: The gap between rich and poor has widened significantly over the past three decades in many developed nations.
variants: wealth disparity, income inequality, the divide between haves and have-nots

name: bridge the gap
example: Technology can help bridge the gap between urban and rural educational opportunities.
variants: narrow the gap, close the divide, reduce the disparity

name: breaking news
example: I usually find out about breaking news through notifications on my phone rather than traditional television broadcasts.
variants: latest news, urgent news, news flash

name: word of mouth
template: word of mouth / by word of mouth / through word of mouth
example: The restaurant became popular not through advertising but through word of mouth and positive customer reviews.

name: in good shape
example: Regular exercise and a balanced diet are essential if you want to stay in good shape as you get older.

name: under the weather
example: I was feeling a bit under the weather last week, so I decided to take a few days off work to rest.
variants: not feeling well, a bit ill, off-color, out of sorts

name: a picture of health
example: Ever since she started her fitness regime, she has been a picture of health.
variants: the epitome of health, in perfect health, glowing with health

name: burn calories
example: High-intensity interval training is one of the most efficient ways to burn calories in a short period.
variants: expend energy, work off calories, calorie expenditure

name: watch one's weight
example: I try to watch my weight by avoiding sugary drinks and choosing healthier snacks.
variants: monitor one's weight, keep an eye on one's figure, manage one's diet

name: make ends meet
example: With the rising cost of living, many families struggle to make ends meet even with two incomes.
variants: get by, keep one's head above water, balance the budget

name: cost an arm and a leg
template: cost an arm and a leg
example: Buying a house in the city center would cost an arm and a leg, which is why most young people rent instead.
variants: be very expensive, cost a fortune, be prohibitively expensive

name: a drop in the ocean
template: a drop in the ocean / a drop in the bucket
example: While the donation was generous, it is still just a drop in the ocean compared to what is needed.
variants: a tiny fraction, a mere fraction, negligible amount

name: money doesn't grow on trees
function: 钱不是从树上长出来的（来之不易）
example: My parents always taught me that money doesn't grow on trees and that I should save for the future.
variants: money is hard to come by, you have to work for money, money isn't free

name: live beyond one's means
example: The proliferation of easy credit has encouraged many people to live beyond their means, accumulating unsustainable debt.
variants: overspend, spend more than one earns, live extravagantly

name: It is widely acknowledged that...
example: It is widely acknowledged that education is the most powerful tool for breaking the cycle of poverty.
variants: It is generally accepted that..., It is universally recognized that...,

name: There is a growing body of evidence that...
template: There is a growing body of evidence that + [clause]
example: There is a growing body of evidence that regular physical activity has profound benefits for mental health.
variants: An increasing amount of research suggests..., Evidence is mounting that...,

name: It is imperative that...
template: It is imperative that + [subject + base verb]
example: It is imperative that governments take decisive action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions before it is too late.
variants: It is essential that..., It is crucial that..., It is vital that...,

name: This begs the question of...
template: This begs the question of + [wh-clause/noun phrase]
example: The policy has been largely ineffective. This begs the question of whether the government truly understands the root causes of the problem.
variants: This raises the question of..., This leads one to ask...,

name: The bottom line is that...
example: The bottom line is that we cannot continue to ignore the environmental consequences of our consumption habits.
variants: The crux of the matter is..., The fundamental point is..., Ultimately,...

name: V → N: analyze → analysis
example: Careful analysis of the data reveals significant trends in consumer behavior.

name: V → N: analyze → analyst
example: Financial analysts predict that the economy will recover steadily over the next two years.

name: V → Adj: analyze → analytical

name: Adj → Adv: analytical → analytically
example: The report approaches the issue analytically rather than emotionally.

name: V → N: argue → argument
example: The argument for free education is supported by numerous economic studies.

name: V → Adj: argue → argumentative
example: He has an argumentative personality and often debates even minor points.

name: V → N: assess → assessment
example: Continuous assessment is generally considered more effective than a single final examination.

name: N → Adj: benefit → beneficial
example: Regular exercise is highly beneficial to both physical and mental wellbeing.

name: N → N: benefit → beneficiary
example: The primary beneficiaries of this policy are low-income families with young children.

name: V → N: create → creation
example: The creation of new jobs in the technology sector has boosted the local economy.

name: V → Adj: create → creative
example: A creative approach to problem-solving is essential in today's rapidly changing world.

name: Adj → N: creative → creativity

name: V → N: create → creator

name: N → Adj: culture → cultural
example: Cultural exchange programs promote mutual understanding between nations.

name: Adj → Adj: cultural → multicultural
example: Living in a multicultural city has exposed me to a wide variety of traditions and perspectives.

name: V → N: decide → decision
example: The decision to invest in renewable energy was influenced by both economic and environmental factors.

name: V → Adj: decide → decisive

name: V → N: develop → development

name: V → Adj: develop → developing

name: V → Adj: develop → developed

name: V → N: differ → difference

name: V → Adj: differ → different

name: Adj → Adv: different → differently

name: V → V: differ → differentiate

name: N → Adj: economy → economic

name: N → N: economy → economics

name: N → N: economy → economist

name: V → N: educate → education

name: V → Adj: educate → educational

name: V → N: employ → employment

name: V → N: employ → employee

name: V → Adj: employ → unemployed

name: N → Adj: environment → environmental
example: Environmental degradation poses a serious threat to future generations.

name: Adj → Adv: environmental → environmentally
example: Consumers are becoming increasingly environmentally conscious in their purchasing decisions.

name: V → N: evaluate → evaluation

name: V → N: exist → existence

name: Adj → Adv: financial → financially
example: The project is not financially viable without additional government support.

name: V → N: govern → government

name: Adj → Adv: healthy → healthily

name: V → Adj: innovate → innovative

name: V → N: instruct → instructor

name: V → N: invest → investment

name: V → N: invest → investor
example: Foreign investors are increasingly attracted to the country's technology sector.

name: V → N: know → knowledge
example: The acquisition of knowledge is a lifelong process that extends far beyond formal education.

name: N → Adj: knowledge → knowledgeable
example: She is extremely knowledgeable about environmental policy and sustainable development.

name: N → Adj: law → legal

name: Adj → Adv: legal → legally

name: V → N: lead → leader

name: V → N: lead → leadership
example: Strong leadership is essential for navigating organizations through periods of change.

name: V → Adj: lead → leading

name: Adj → Adv: natural → naturally

name: V → Adj: predict → predictable

name: V → N: produce → production

name: V → Adj: prosper → prosperous
example: The region has become increasingly prosperous due to its thriving tourism industry.

name: N → Adj: psychology → psychological
example: The psychological effects of prolonged isolation can be severe and long-lasting.

name: N → N: psychology → psychologist
example: A clinical psychologist can provide valuable support for individuals dealing with anxiety.

name: N → Adj: science → scientific
example: Scientific research has provided compelling evidence of human-induced climate change.

name: N → N: science → scientist
example: Scientists around the world are collaborating to develop sustainable energy solutions.

name: N → Adj: sign → significant
example: There has been a significant increase in the number of students choosing STEM subjects.

name: Adj → N: significant → significance
example: The historical significance of the site attracts thousands of visitors every year.

name: Adj → Adv: significant → significantly

name: Adj → N: social → society
example: Modern society faces unprecedented challenges in areas such as inequality and sustainability.

name: Adj → Adv: social → socially
example: Socially responsible companies increasingly prioritize ethical practices over short-term profits.

name: Adj → Adj: social → sociable
example: She has always been a sociable person who enjoys meeting new people and attending events.

name: Adj → Adj: social → antisocial

name: V → Adj: solve → solvable
example: While the issue is complex, it is not insurmountable and indeed solvable with coordinated effort.

name: Adj → N: strong → strength

name: N → V: strength → strengthen

name: Adj → Adv: strong → strongly

name: N → Adj: success → successful

name: Adj → Adv: successful → successfully
example: The project was successfully completed ahead of schedule and under budget.

name: Adj → Adj: successful → unsuccessful

name: N → Adj: technique → technical

name: N → N: technique → technology
example: Advances in medical technology have dramatically increased life expectancy worldwide.

name: N → Adj: technology → technological

name: N → Adj: tradition → traditional
example: Traditional teaching methods are being supplemented by digital tools in many classrooms.

name: Adj → Adv: traditional → traditionally

name: V → N: vary → variety

name: V → Adj: vary → various

name: V → Adj/N: vary → variable
example: Weather patterns have become increasingly variable due to climate change.

name: V → N: vary → variation
example: There is considerable variation in educational standards across different regions.

name: V → Adv: vary → invariably
example: Quality education invariably leads to better employment prospects and higher earnings.

name: Adj → N: wide → width
example: The width of the digital divide varies significantly between urban and rural communities.

name: Adj → V: wide → widen

variants: widens (三单), widened (过去式), width (n.), wide (adj.)

name: Adj → Adv: wide → widely
example: It is widely accepted that early childhood education has lasting benefits.

name: Adj → Adj: wide → widespread
example: There is widespread support for increasing the minimum wage across all demographic groups.

name: Adj-N: high-quality
example: Access to high-quality healthcare should be available to all citizens regardless of income.
variants: high quality (名词短语), top-quality, premium-quality, low-quality

name: Adj-N: low-cost
example: Low-cost airlines have made international travel accessible to millions of people.
variants: low in cost, inexpensive, affordable, budget-friendly

name: Adj-N: long-term
example: Long-term investment in education yields significant social and economic returns.
variants: in the long term (adv.), long-run, short-term

name: Adj-N: short-term
example: While short-term gains are tempting, sustainable development requires a longer perspective.
variants: in the short term (adv.), short-run, long-term

name: Adj-N: full-time
example: Full-time students often find it challenging to balance academic work with part-time employment.
variants: full time (名词短语), part-time, full-time employment/study

name: Adj-N: part-time

variants: part time (名词短语), full-time, part-time work/study

name: Adv-V: well-known
function: 众所周知的（复合形容词）

variants: well known, famous, renowned, widely recognized

name: Adv-V: well-educated
function: 受过良好教育的（复合形容词）
example: A well-educated workforce is essential for maintaining competitiveness in the global economy.

name: Adv-V: well-trained
function: 训练有素的（复合形容词）
example: Well-trained teachers are the cornerstone of an effective education system.
variants: well trained, highly trained, skilled, proficient

name: Adv-V: well-paid

variants: well paid, highly paid, generously remunerated, poorly paid

name: Adv-V: well-prepared
function: 准备充分的（复合形容词）

variants: well prepared, thoroughly prepared, ready, unprepared

name: Pron-V: self-employed
function: 自雇的/个体经营的（复合形容词）

name: Pron-N: self-confidence

name: Pron-Adj: self-sufficient
function: 自给自足的（复合形容词）

name: Pron-N: self-discipline
example: Success in online learning requires considerable self-discipline and time management.

name: Adj-Ving: long-standing
function: 长期存在的（复合形容词）
example: The issue of inequality is a long-standing problem that requires comprehensive policy responses.

name: Adj-Ving: far-reaching
function: 影响深远的（复合形容词）

name: N-Ving: groundbreaking

name: N-Ving: thought-provoking
function: 引人深思的（复合形容词）

name: N-Adj: cost-effective
function: 具有成本效益的（复合形容词）

name: N-Adj: energy-efficient

name: N-Ving: time-consuming

name: N-Adj: labor-intensive
function: 劳动密集型的（复合形容词）

name: N-Adj: user-friendly
function: 用户友好的（复合形容词）

variants: intuitive, accessible, easy to use, convenient

name: Adv-Adj: environmentally-friendly

name: N-Adj: old-fashioned
function: 老式的/传统的（复合形容词）

name: Adj-N: middle-aged

variants: middle age (n.), in one's forties/fifties, older workers

name: N-N: state-of-the-art

variants: internationally renowned, globally recognized, famous worldwide

name: Adj-N: world-class

variants: taxation, tax revenue, tax rate, direct tax

name: N-N: generation gap

variants: generational divide, age gap, generational difference

name: N-N: job market
template: the job market / [noun] in the job market

name: N-N: social media

variants: social networking, digital platforms, online communities

name: N-N: public transport

variants: public transit, mass transit, public transportation

name: N-N: health care

name: N-N: life expectancy

variants: expected lifespan, longevity, average life span

name: N-N: information technology

name: Adj-N: higher education

variants: tertiary education, university education, postsecondary education

name: Adj-N: developing country

variants: low-income country, emerging economy, the Global South

name: Adj-N: working class
example: The decline of manufacturing industries has had a devastating impact on the traditional working class.

name: Prefix over-: overpopulation
example: Overpopulation in urban areas has led to severe housing shortages and increased pollution.

name: Prefix over-: overestimate
example: It is common for people to overestimate their own abilities while underestimating others'.

name: Prefix over-: overload
example: Students are often overloaded with assignments during the examination period.

name: Prefix under-: underestimate
example: We should not underestimate the importance of early childhood education in shaping future outcomes.

name: Prefix under-: underdeveloped
example: Infrastructure in underdeveloped regions remains a major barrier to economic progress.

name: Prefix under-: underpaid
example: Essential workers such as nurses and teachers are frequently underpaid relative to their contribution to society.

name: Prefix inter-: international
example: International cooperation is essential for tackling global challenges such as climate change and pandemics.

name: Prefix inter-: interconnected
example: In our interconnected world, events in one region can have far-reaching consequences globally.

name: Prefix inter-: interdisciplinary
example: Interdisciplinary research often produces the most innovative solutions to complex problems.

name: Prefix multi-: multinational
example: Multinational corporations have significant influence on global trade and employment patterns.

name: Prefix multi-: multilingual
example: Being multilingual is a considerable asset in an increasingly globalized job market.

name: Prefix multi-: multimedia
example: Modern education increasingly incorporates multimedia tools to engage diverse learners.

name: Prefix non-: non-profit
example: Non-profit organizations often fill gaps left by government and private sector services.

name: Prefix non-: non-renewable
example: Our continued reliance on non-renewable energy sources is environmentally unsustainable.

name: Prefix non-: non-violent
example: Non-violent resistance has proven to be one of the most effective methods of social change.

name: Prefix anti-: anti-social
example: Anti-social behavior in public spaces can deter people from using community facilities.

name: Prefix co-: co-operate
example: Countries must co-operate to establish effective frameworks for combating cybercrime.

name: Prefix mis-: misinformation
example: The spread of misinformation on social media has become a major public concern.
variants: misinform (v.), disinformation, fake news, false information

name: Prefix mis-: misunderstanding
example: Cultural differences can sometimes lead to misunderstanding between people from different backgrounds.

name: Prefix pre-: predict
example: It is difficult to predict with certainty how artificial intelligence will reshape the labor market.

name: Prefix post-: post-graduate
example: Post-graduate studies require a high degree of self-motivation and independent research skills.

name: Prefix re-: recycle
example: Governments should implement policies to encourage businesses to recycle industrial waste.

name: Prefix re-: revise
example: Students are advised to revise their essays carefully before submitting them for assessment.

name: Prefix un-: unemployed
example: Unemployed young people are at greater risk of social exclusion and mental health problems.
variants: unemployment (n.), jobless, out of work, laid off

name: Prefix un-: unfortunate
example: It is unfortunate that many people still lack access to basic healthcare services.

name: Prefix dis-: disadvantage
example: One major disadvantage of online learning is the lack of face-to-face interaction with peers.

name: Prefix in-: insufficient
example: Insufficient funding for public schools has resulted in overcrowded classrooms and overstretched teachers.

name: Prefix im-: impossible
example: It is impossible to ignore the profound impact that social media has had on modern communication.

name: Prefix il-: illegal
example: Illegal dumping of industrial waste continues to contaminate waterways despite stricter regulations.
variants: illegally (adv.), against the law, unlawful, prohibited

name: Prefix ir-: irresponsible
example: It would be irresponsible for policymakers to ignore the warnings of climate scientists.

name: Suffix -tion/-sion: globalization
example: Globalization has created both unprecedented economic opportunities and significant social challenges.

name: explanation for
function: 学术名词+介词：对...的解释
example: One possible explanation for this phenomenon is the increasing availability of affordable streaming services.
variants: a plausible explanation for, a satisfactory explanation for, the most likely explanation for

name: 主语从句-That
template: That + 完整陈述句 + is + adj./n.
function: 主语从句：用That引导，表达客观事实或观点，后接系表结构
usageNote: That从句作主语时，谓语动词常用单数；正式写作中可用it作形式主语改写
example: That education is important is undeniable. [语域：正式 | Task 2/学术写作]
variants: That technology has changed our lives is evident. / That poverty remains a global issue is tragic.

name: 主语从句-What
template: What + 从句 + is + 表语
function: 主语从句：What兼具先行词和关系代词功能，表示'所...的事物'
usageNote: What引导的主语从句在雅思写作中极为常用，用于聚焦核心议题
example: What matters most is sustained effort rather than innate talent. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: What concerns researchers is the lack of empirical evidence. / What determines success is perseverance.

name: 主语从句-Whether
template: Whether + 从句 + 谓语 + 其他
function: 主语从句：Whether表示'是否'，用于表达不确定性或两种可能性
usageNote: 常用于讨论有争议的话题，后接remains to be seen/depends on等
example: Whether the policy will achieve its goals remains to be seen. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: Whether online learning can replace traditional education is still debatable.

name: 主语从句-How
template: How + 从句 + is + 表语
function: 主语从句：How表示方式或程度，强调'如何/怎样'
usageNote: 常用于探讨方法、途径或程度差异
example: How governments respond to this crisis will define their legacy. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: How we balance economic growth and environmental protection is crucial.

name: 主语从句-Wh-疑问词
template: Wh-词 + 从句 + 谓语 + 其他
function: 主语从句：各类疑问词均可引导主语从句
usageNote: 根据具体语义选择疑问词，注意who作主语从句引导词时指人
example: Who should take responsibility for climate change is a contentious issue. [语域：正式 | Task 2/口语Part 3]
variants: Where to allocate limited resources is a dilemma facing all policymakers.

name: 形式主语-It is clear that
template: It is + adj. + that + 从句
function: 形式主语：It为形式主语，真正主语是that从句
usageNote: 雅思写作最高频句型之一，用于表达明确判断
example: It is clear that technology has fundamentally changed education. [语域：正式 | Task 2/通用]
variants: It is evident that further research is needed. / It is widely believed that exercise improves mental health.

name: 形式主语-It is undeniable that
template: It is + 强调性adj. + that + 从句
function: 形式主语：使用强形容词表达毋庸置疑的判断
usageNote: 用于加强论证力度，适用于议论文的论点陈述
example: It is undeniable that globalization has transformed national economies. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: It is indisputable that education is the foundation of social progress.

name: 形式主语-It is widely accepted that
template: It is + 副词 + 过去分词 + that + 从句
function: 形式主语：表达普遍共识或公认观点
usageNote: 引用公众认知或学术共识时使用，增强论证说服力
example: It is widely accepted that regular physical activity enhances cognitive function. [语域：正式 | Task 2/学术]
variants: It is generally acknowledged that early education shapes character.

name: 宾语从句-I acknowledge that
function: 宾语从句：表达承认或认可对方观点
usageNote: 用于让步论证，先承认对立观点再反驳，体现思辨深度
example: I acknowledge that technology has brought considerable convenience to daily life. [语域：正式 | Task 2]
variants: I admit that this approach has its limitations. / I recognize that change takes time.
